records.text: Hey there @pepsishark , Thanks for posting in the Community and welcome. We'd like to gather some additional info to have a better idea of what is happening on your side. Please send us: The exact Spotify version you're running. Did you notice if this started to happen after a specific event such as an OS update? Did you notice the same regardless of the playlist you choose to add songs to? A video recording. You can attach the video in your next reply in mp4 format, or upload it to YouTube or Google Drive and make it public. Note: Be aware this is a public space, so please don't share any personal info on the video you'll send us. Keep us in the loop! AlejaR Moderator Help others find this answer and click "Accept as Solution" . If you appreciate an answer, maybe give it a Like .
records.author: AlejaR

metadata.title: Re: Playlist recommend not showing up
metadata.search_snippet: Thank you

records.text: Ways To Get Fresh Music Out of Spotify DJ Without Blocking Everything Account Wide This is a request for feature improvements. I am already aware of "exclude from taste profile," "do not play this artist," "hide this track," and the ability to type and speak instructions to the DJ. Please take the time to actually read the following, as your representatives did not do. (BTW, "hide this track" only works per session. It will play the song again in the next login. This is also true when you tell DJ, "no not play any music from my library." It will do this for a short period of time. Then default back to playing whatever it wants.) (The pdf is attached and the actual text is pasted below.) Thank you. Hi, you're through to Spotify support. Please describe your issue to get started. Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 agent You at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 We're gathering your account information. This will only take a few seconds. Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 We will connect you to an advisor! Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 An advisor is available. Would you like to chat now? Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 Yes You at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 We’ll connect you now. An advisor will review the conversation before responding. Spotify at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 To verify you’re the account owner, we may ask you to provide proof. Spotify at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 Note: If this is requested, please hide any sensitive information before sharing documentation with us, e.g. payment card or bank account numbers. Spotify at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 Danish Md is now connected and reviewing your question. They’ll message you shortly. Hey there! I will be assisting you today! I hope you are having a great day. Danish Md at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 I do understand your issue, please give me some time I will check and get back to you. Danish Md at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 Can you please elaborate your issue once? Danish Md at 10:40 AM, Jan 9 I have a complaint that is a major issue to me concerning Spotify's artist blocking and song hiding features. You at 10:40 AM, Jan 9 To confirm are you an artist? Danish Md at 10:42 AM, Jan 9 I am an artist, but this is completely unrelated to my artist account and solely related to my listening activity. You at 10:43 AM, Jan 9 Hello? You at 10:45 AM, Jan 9 Perfect do you have any screenshot or any error message? Danish Md at 10:46 AM, Jan 9 No You at 10:46 AM, Jan 9 No worries, please tell me any other issue if you want me to add it? Danish Md at 10:48 AM, Jan 9 Do you speak English, Danish Md? Your response time is very slow. You at 10:48 AM, Jan 9 I am really for this, I was forwarding the issue to the specialist team. They will look into and and the backend team will make the changes. Danish Md at 10:49 AM, Jan 9 What issue and what changes? We haven't even discussed it. You at 10:49 AM, Jan 9 I will mention the issue now, that is the reason I was asking that please add the exact issue on it so I can get it fixed through them. Danish Md at 10:50 AM, Jan 9 May I please to speak to someone else now? You at 10:51 AM, Jan 9 You mean you wanted to talk to the manager? Danish Md at 10:51 AM, Jan 9 Sure. You at 10:52 AM, Jan 9 You can tell me, I will help you with this. If you have any suggestions I will help you with this. Danish Md at 10:52 AM, Jan 9 So far, you don't seem to understand anything I've said since we began this conversation. I would prefer to speak to another agent or your supervisor. You at 10:53 AM, Jan 9 I understand that you want to share some concern regarding the Artist blocking and song hiding features. Danish Md at 10:54 AM, Jan 9 Please elaborate the issue, I will surely help you with this. Danish Md at 10:55 AM, Jan 9 When I listen to Spotify, I like as much variety and new music as possible with as few repeats as possible. The AI DJ X feature is very bad about playing the same 20 songs over and over even when I have thousands of songs in my library. Also, the shuffle feature on Spotify is very bad, but that's a separate issue that someone should address... You at 10:57 AM, Jan 9 I thought that I had found a solution by using the "don't play this artist" and "hide track" features while listening to DJ X, which finally made me very happy. However,... You at 10:58 AM, Jan 9 No we can also add the shuffle feature with this, I will add that too. Danish Md at 10:58 AM, Jan 9 Okay so now the solution is not working for you? Danish Md at 10:59 AM, Jan 9 I thought when I did that, it only applied to the DJ feature. I just found out that it is global on my account. I reorganized my playlists, singles and albums on my PC app, and now it tells me, "we can't play this until you allow this artist in the spotify phone app"! This is ridiculous! You at 11:00 AM, Jan 9 I still want to be able to play those songs and artists when I feel like manually playing them. I just don't want the DJ to play them. You at 11:01 AM, Jan 9 And why can't I re-enable them on my PC anyway if I have to do that? I have to go through everything I've just organized on the PC on my phone now and re-enable each artist and song individually? That's awful! You at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 Aare you trying it on the application or the web browser? Danish Md at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 And I don't want to do that anyway because then the DJ way start playing it again! You at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 The PC app. You at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 No worries, I will share this to the team. Danish Md at 11:05 AM, Jan 9 Okay so it is happening on every device? Danish Md at 11:05 AM, Jan 9 Thank you for letting us know, We really appreciate your providing the valuable information and helping us get better. Yeah sure I will definitely take your feedback on this and inform the relevant team. I will go ahead and pass this information to our content folks. As they are the team who will look into this and get this right. Danish Md at 11:06 AM, Jan 9 I pay for the family plan. I've been paying this premium amount for years. One reason I do that is because my original account started playing nothing but stoner-rock on the DJ. I like stoner-rock, but I don't want to hear it exclusively, so I created a second profile (this one) with no stoner-rock. Now, I'm running into another issue. It's getting really old. You at 11:06 AM, Jan 9 In case I become unavailable on your next reply, your conversation will be connected to the next available advisor so request you to stay connected. Don't worry, I've saved notes on your account, so the next advisor will know what we've discussed and take it up from there. Appreciate your patience I truly understand your concern and I agree that you have bene paying for so long and you should definitely get to use all the features. Danish Md at 11:09 AM, Jan 9 Another thing is that when I reorganized, all I did was create some folders on the PC in my Spotify library called "Singles", "Albums", "Local File Albums", etc. and moved the playlists to those folders. I didn't even block those artists or anything, but just moving them now gives me the message, ""we can't play this until you allow this artist in the spotify phone app". You at 11:10 AM, Jan 9 Sean Andrei T is now connected and reviewing your question. They’ll message you shortly. Hello there! You are now connected to English Support. I'm Sean, and I'm here to assist you with any of your concerns. How are you doing? Sean Andrei T at 11:15 AM, Jan 9 hello You at 11:17 AM, Jan 9 I can see that your case has been reassigned. Don't worry, I'm here to help further. Sean Andrei T at 11:18 AM, Jan 9 ok You at 11:18 AM, Jan 9 All caught up now. Sean Andrei T at 11:20 AM, Jan 9 ok You at 11:20 AM, Jan 9 Seems like you're having issues with the listening experience you're having when trying our DJ feature. May I know when did this problem start? Sean Andrei T at 11:21 AM, Jan 9 That's a difficult question to answer. Really, since the feature became available, but even before that, I've always had issues with Spotify's shuffle algorithm. It constantly plays the same 20 songs even when I have thousands of songs in my library. I hoped the DJ would recommend fresh music including old music I hadn't heard yet and new music that was just released, but it mostly plays stuff already in my library and hits or very popular songs that are from the same bands in my library or very similar bands that were very popular at the same time rather than new, fresh, underground stuff like I want to hear. You at 11:26 AM, Jan 9 Regarding the issues you're having with the genre of music being played, you can try this step so our DJ can recommend other songs: Simply hold down the DJ button to fine-tune your session with requests like “more upbeat” or “more new releases” and DJ will refresh your session. Or you can skip a section altogether by tapping the DJ button at the bottom of your screen. At launch, the most successful sessions are generated through a combination of “genre,” “mood,” “decade,” or “artist” requests. You can also ask DJ to: • • Play new releases • Explore your library in new ways, such as playing your liked songs • Play songs you haven’t heard in a while • Mix different artists • Adjust for tone/pace/energy levels (e.g., genre, high BPM) • Retrieve specific tracks, artists, albums, etc. However, hyper-specific queries could result in low-quality recommendations. Sean Andrei T at 11:26 AM, Jan 9 Thanks for the added context. Let me look into that further. Sean Andrei T at 11:27 AM, Jan 9 As I told the other agent, I was in love with the "do not play this artist" and "hide this track" features when I discovered them, but I thought they only applied to the DJ, not my entire account. Now, i realize i can no longer manually play those bands or songs when I want to, and since organizing my playlists on my pc, i can no longer play them. You at 11:27 AM, Jan 9 I understand your concern about that. We also have an option to exclude music from your Spotify Taste Profile, tap the three dots next to a song or playlist and select "Exclude from your taste profile," which stops it from influencing recommendations, perfect for functional listening like sleep music or kids' tunes, and you can easily toggle it back on later. This gives you more control so your personalized playlists stay relevant to your actual music taste. Have you already tried that? Sean Andrei T at 11:30 AM, Jan 9 By the way, I'm about to take my break now so in case I was not able to respond on your next reply immediately, your case will be connected to the next available advisor. Don't worry though, one of my colleagues will take over our conversation here and pick up where we left off once you reply. After that reply, there may be a moment of silence for about 4-5 minutes which is for when they're trying to connect. Sean Andrei T at 11:30 AM, Jan 9 Those features *seem* like they would help, but they don't. no matter the mood or genre i give it, it still plays **bleep** i've already heard a million times. i tried telling it "do not play any music currently in my library", which it does, but only for a short length of time, then I have to do it over and over. it always defaults back to it's basic routine after a set of songs and disregards my requests. i don't want to do that every time i run the thing. the way that I *thought* those 2 features i mentioned worked were great, until i went back and tried to manually play them and wasn't able to. if that alone were fixed, i think that would help tremendously! You at 11:32 AM, Jan 9 yes, i use the the "exclude from taste profile" feature. it helps some, but not enough. You at 11:34 AM, Jan 9 alright You at 11:36 AM, Jan 9 Ananth is now connected and reviewing your question. They’ll message you shortly. Hi, thanks for reaching out! I'll be helping you today. Ananth at 11:37 AM, Jan 9 alright. you have a very long conversation to catch up on. You at 11:37 AM, Jan 9 Yah allow me a moment to understand your concern. Ananth at 11:38 AM, Jan 9 ok You at 11:39 AM, Jan 9 I understand your concern and take it as a feedback. Ananth at 11:42 AM, Jan 9 We also have an option to exclude music from your Spotify Taste Profile, tap the three dots next to a song or playlist and select "Exclude from your taste profile," which stops it from influencing Ananth at 11:42 AM, Jan 9 that was discussed already You at 11:43 AM, Jan 9 I will take this as a feedback and make sure it reaches the right folks and make sure that you won't face this issue ever again. Ananth at 11:46 AM, Jan 9 how will you do that? You at 11:46 AM, Jan 9 I have completely understood your issue and I will communicate the same to the right folks and they will make sure that you won't face this issue again. Ananth at 11:48 AM, Jan 9 which issue in particular? You at 11:49 AM, Jan 9 This is the only way to turn it off • Listening to DJ only happens when you press play. • To listen, head to the Music Feed on Home, click on the DJ card, and press play. • When you’re done, just press stop, exit out of it, or hit play on another track, playlist, podcast, or audiobook! Ananth at 11:54 AM, Jan 9 I honestly have no idea what you're talking about. It is unrelated to my issue. You at 11:55 AM, Jan 9 do you have a corporate email address to contact about my concerns? You at 11:56 AM, Jan 9 Thank you for the details. Allow me a moment to check this for you. Ananth at 11:57 AM, Jan 9 **bleep** where you can write all your concern. Ananth at 11:59 AM, Jan 9 (they bleeped out the support email address) Out of the 4 or 5 agents I spoke to, I think you understood the least, but thank you for the email address at least. You at 12:01 PM, Jan 9 (I emailed he support email address this conversation and got the following email, which is why I'm here now.) Hey there! Thanks for contacting us. We understand you used the option to exclude content and also to not play this artist in our DJ app; however, those options have been applied to the rest of your app and your entire account. For now, the DJ app works this way, our goal is always to offer better listening experiences and fair value for every plan, but I can see how this situation feels far from that. I’ve shared your feedback internally so it can be reviewed by the right teams — comments like yours really help us improve and shape future decisions. To help us get your idea heard, it’d be awesome if you could add it to our ideas board: https://community.spotify.com/t5/Ideas/ct-p/newideas Other listeners can then vote and show their support. Our product teams regularly review the forum, and many amazing features have emerged from it. Thanks again for being part of Spotify and for your idea. We truly appreciate your time, and we’ll keep working to make your experience better. Andrea Spotify Customer Support
records.author: Mojo_Cat

records.text: Ongoing Issues Review: May 2026 Welcome to this month's Ongoing Issues Review where we keep you in the loop about some of the most trending ongoing issues reported here in the Spotify Community. This is where we share updates from behind the scenes and let you know what has been fixed, what we’re tracking, what we need your help with, and where things stand. You can check the full Ongoing Issue section here . FIXED 1. "Add" button in playlists - limited number of suggested songs Some changes have been made in the backend by the internal teams and the number of recommendations you're getting should be back to normal. Just make sure you're running the latest version . 😎 2. Newly saved music in "Your Library" only shows placeholder cover until actually playing a track We got confirmation in the thread that all cover art loads correctly now! 💚 3. Issues with the Search function across devices in the EU We had reports about issues with Search within Spotify and that users were getting a 'Couldn't find this' error. We can confirm that this was fixed! 4. New Episodes "Continue listening" section broken/not showing up The relevant team made some changes backstage so this should now be fixed for everyone 😎 5. Tracks skipping on desktop This was fixed - m ake sure to keep your app up-to-date with these steps . The version should be at least 1.2.88 . 6. Playback stops after a track ends Things should be up and running again - just make sure you have app version 9.1.36 (or a later one) installed ! 🎊 7. "Music/Following" filter doesn't work with large amount of followed artists Everything works fine now and we already received some user confirmations in the thread! 🎊 8. Playback issues on multiple devices All good now! Just make sure to restart the app in case you still get this behavior. 9. Spotify Windows Installer Bug, Impossible to Install Spotify! This should now be resolved for everyone - thanks to everyone who let us know in the thread! 💚 10. Downtime May 12th, 2026: Some services are down This was a short-lived ongoing issue, which was quickly resolved! ✌️ 11. Downtime May 13th: Support Site not working The same goes for this one, everything was quickly back to normal! 12. Currently playing song not highlighted in playlist while watching the music video We can confirm that this should be fixed now. We'd suggest keeping your app updated so you don't miss out on any developments. 13. Move up feature in queue creates duplicates Our teams have made some changes and this issue should be fixed as well! 😎 14. Discover Weekly playlist is blank or missing entirely We’re happy to say that this should now be fixed for everyone! 🎊 15. Spotify app crashes during video podcast on Apple TV We can confirm some changes have been made backstage and this should no longer happen! 🤞 16. Blank Now Playing View on iPad We just got confirmation that this should be fixed now! 🎊 UNDER INVESTIGATION 17. Your Episodes, Remove finished episodes after playing doesn't work We've been getting reports that the setting to remove finished episodes from the Your Episodes list no longer works for podcasts that have been downloaded either manually or via auto-download. Streamed episodes are still removed from Your Episodes as expected. 18. Unable to Join Family Plan Some of you may have noticed that there are some issues when adding new members to your Family plans. This is on our radar and it's being looked into. 19. Google Cast/ Chromecast devices unavailable for Spotify Connect Happy to announce that a new update will be available to you starting next week, which should clear this ongoing issue! 🤞 Keep us posted on how things go after you get the 9.1.48 update. 20. Saved tracks not showing up on DJ platforms We've received reports that saved tracks on users' playlists in DJ platforms or software like Serato and Rekordbox fail to load or are missing. 21. CarPlay shows the previous track's album artwork The relevant team is currently looking into this. If you're experiencing this issue, make sure to add your vote and Subscribe to the thread from the three dots menu at the top to stay tuned. ✍️ NEED MORE INFO 22. "Make this a playlist" malfunctioning. This should be fixed, but we haven't heard back from anyone experiencing this in the thread yet 👀 23. The Spotify embed is accessible due to the ""Preview" label We've asked for more info, so we can investigate further, so if you're experiencing this, make sure to join the thread! 24. Artist View crashes iPad app A new app version is slowly being rolled out and you should notice some improvements with it 🙂 Make sure to update the app - it should be at least 9.1.46. So far, we've only heard back from one user, so let us know if this was fixed on your end as well 💚 25. Fitness videos aren't working . We're trying to gather some more info from anyone affected by this, so make sure to share if you're experiencing this ✍️ NOT RIGHT NOW 26. [iOS] Switch to video button overlaps with lyrics We've heard back from the respective team and they're unable to provide an ETA for a fix at this time, but rest assured this will remain on their radar. 27. [Android Auto] Back button not working. Bricking the app . This issue is being investigated by the Android Auto team directly in Google's support community, so we'd suggest adding your feedback to Google's bug report here to stay on top of any updates. 🏎 28. DJ Recommending the same set of songs We appreciate your feedback on this, but we're unable to provide an exact ETA for a fix at this time. Make sure you're always running the latest version of Spotify so you don't miss out on any updates. 29. Playlists invisible on iPhone and iPod All details have already been passed on to the right team, but we're afraid we don't have a timeline for a fix at the moment. 30. Playing song after playlist ends does not reset queue This is the current expected behavior - playing a new track no longer reshuffles the queue, rather it replaces what your current track is and once you finish the last song, you will go back to the beginning as normal. ✔️ [New!] Focused Spotlight: Accessibility Fixes In this month's edition, we're trying something new. Alongside our usual ongoing issues roundup, we're including our first Focused Spotlight , shining a light on a specific area where your reports have led to real improvements. For this first spotlight, we're focusing on accessibility . May 21st is Global Accessibility Awareness Day , so it felt like the right time to share what's been happening behind the scenes when it comes to making Spotify easier to use for everyone. Over the past few months, a number of accessibility-related issues reported through the Community and our support channels have been investigated and resolved. Here's a rundown of some of what's been fixed between in recent months: KEYBOARD AND NAVIGATION 1. iPad Magic Keyboard shortcuts stopped working | Fixed February 2026 iPad users with a Magic Keyboard found that the space bar stopped working for play/pause. This has been fixed. Keyboard shortcuts on iPad with Magic Keyboard are working again as expected. 2. Search keyboard not appearing on mobile | Fixed February 2026 On mobile, double-tapping the Search icon wasn't bringing up the keyboard automatically, which meant extra steps to start typing. The keyboard now appears as it should when you tap into Search. Community thread 3. Play/pause keyboard button not working on video podcasts (Mac) | Fixed February 2026 On Mac, the play/pause button on the keyboard wasn't responding properly during video podcasts. This has been resolved. Keyboard media controls now work consistently across audio and video content on Mac. Community thread 4. iPad Library flickering with Magic Keyboard | Fixed April 2026 The iPad Library screen was flickering when connected to an Apple Magic Keyboard. This visual glitch has been fixed. The Library now displays normally with an external keyboard attached. 5. Tab switching resetting your scroll position on Android | Fixed April 2026 On Android, switching between tabs (like Home, Search, Library) was resetting your scroll position, so you'd lose your place. Tab switching now preserves where you were. You won't be sent back to the top when moving between sections. TRANSCRIPTS AND CAPTIONS 1. Podcast transcripts couldn't be turned off | Fixed February 2026 The "Read Along" transcript option was at times covering the podcast artwork, and there was no way to turn it off. Users now have more control over transcript display. This has been resolved. 2. Podcast episode list kept resetting to the top | Fixed February 2026 Podcast episode lists were resetting to the top of the list whenever you navigated away and came back. Your scroll position is now saved, so you can pick up where you left off. These are just a few of the recent fixes, however we’re hoping to bring you more Team Spotlights in the future to highlight different areas where your feedback has made a difference. If you've run into any issues recently, please keep sharing it here. It really does help us make Spotify better for everyone! For more information on Accessibility at Spotify, or for our dedicated Accessibility feedback form head here ! What if you spot an OI? Check our Ongoing Issues board and hit +VOTE if you’re affected—it helps us prioritize. Need quick help? Post your question in the Help Boards Got feedback? Share it in Ideas If you'd also like to see some changes before other users you can sign-up to join our thousands of Beta testers here . https://open.spotify.com/track/0xjkgYSzHjBZNvyUaC9cXX?si=3efa4844d42a4462
records.author: Dessi

records.text: #MusicMonday Review - February 2026 Part 2 #MusicMonday is the hashtag I've been using for quite a while to share music recommendations from up-and-coming artists. Always fresh, and always different, trying to look for trends before they become one. You can check February's Part 1 review for more music. In this edition, let's explore the dark side of technology, not without looking back on how history, and our families, can give us a guiding light to navigate through life. Give them a listen, with a word from the artists themselves. 🎧 Thistle Sifter – Ghost Acres "We're totally dependent on farming, and it's surrounded by all these sort of comforting myths and stories for the very young children about this lovely livestock farm where there's one horse, one cow , one pig, one cat, one dog living in harmony, and that's how we'd like to see it, but the reality is it's the greatest cause of habitat destruction, greatest cause of extinction, one of the greatest causes of climate breakdown, of fresh water pollution, of marine pollution, also major cause actually of air pollution. We don't think about that very often. It's a major cause of soil loss, a major cause of water use. It is amazing how little it features in our environmental consciousness. We talk about transport, we talk about home insulation, we talk about industry, and yet somehow we don't want to go there." We begin our travels in Utrecht, NL with a Progressive Post-Rock track about the classic line of thought regarding the countryside as the seed of innocence and purity, while making the city an evil, corrupt, entity. Is there more to the story? "Ghost Acres was inspired by the book Regenesis by George Monbiot, and also features a quote from Monbiot talking about the effects of modern day agriculture on our natural environment. In an age where most developed countries outsource food production, ghost acres represent the real amount of land that is needed to produce the products that we consume. The title of the track symbolizes the idea that most Westerners live their lives dependent on ghost acreage, far removed from the origins of the food they are consuming." spotify:track:3k3E4vHumb9ANAwncKKAhb:small CALL IT VAIN – Saturday Superhero I’m left with these growing pains But nothing else feels the same As it once did, the silence Covered in laughs as a kid What I’d do to bring it all back It’s just a sigh for attention To teach me a lesson again I find myself staring at the ceiling, then I try to, don’t like to Lock it up, can’t cry, no time to We go now to Yorkshire, England for an Indie Rock track about that person whose guidance saves you from breaking down, and makes you nostalgic from remembering everything you did together: "I wrote this when I was 18 and wanted to use it as a way of expressing my relationship with my dad. I’ve always had a lot of respect for my dad and I’m very grateful for everything he’s done for me. I’ve always wanted to make him proud and that’s something I go into in the song. I talk about feelings of self-doubt, admiration and anything else that makes a father-son relationship. This is my favourite song to play live because it always reminds me of one of the reasons I started playing music in the first place, because of him." spotify:track:32Hv3TIUaYi1MK6FfTDdL4:small The Corner Laughers – Rainbow Cardigan I climbed up to Kendal Castle on a cold and rainy day Saw the ruins all around us and the town so old and grey Wrapped in a rainbow cardigan to keep the blues at bay I went whistling through the graveyard, for I didn't know the way Stumbled in the muddy puddles past the stone walls in decay Wrapped in a rainbow cardigan to keep above the fray From Redwood City, CA, we get an Indie Pop/Folk song about being old, and young, at the same time, with all the faults, and responsibilities, that makes us who we are : "Often when I write a song, it starts with a little scrap of something and only after it’s all stitched together is the pattern unveiled. Here, the concept seems to be of a woven textile piece representing the knitting together of time and space – a kind of cosmic map or web. The song is a bit of a microcosm of the album as a whole, with its imagery of colors and shades, its distinctive blend of sadness and joy, anxiety and wonder. There is, in fact, a real rainbow cardigan, which I fell in love with and bought from a little shop with a wonderful owner in the town of Kendal, England. Looking around at the ruins, I realized that old as they were, in the grand, universal scheme of things, they – and all of humankind’s civilizations and buildings – they really aren’t old at all. And that leads into how it feels to me, heading into my own 'middle ages', sometimes unspeakably ancient and sometimes like a little child. Perhaps you’ve felt the same? We then move to one of my favorite places on Earth – Lake Lagunitas, in Fairfax, California, where I grew up – and to the way reliving past mistakes can seem to replay endlessly. I was thinking of earthquake faults and topographic maps, which lead me back to textiles. All the good and bad and colors and textures of life woven and wrapped up together, I suppose." spotify:track:2zRXdw8Ool5XE5VaHxA4Li:small Baby Condor – Backcountry Towns Your eyes betray all lies you ever told your smile fails to disguise the ones still on your tongue If only you were stolen from your golden coach to roam the concrete jungle home to common folk You’d know We fly now to Deventer, NL for a Modern Americana track about how simple life in the country is, without the complications of technology: "Well it’s basically a protest song against the modern oligarchs, mainly because of what’s happening in the US with the big tech bros. consolidating power by controlling the information flow and killing our critical thinking. Small town simple truths and humaneness for the win 😄 " spotify:track:5qGzVDh9YMXhz1OSK35V1u:small Howling Hawk – Nine Miles High There’s poison in the air and there’s lead in the water And we're dead in the water if we believe the news Nine miles high, there’s an eye in the sky They see inside and they don’t like the view They see you in the morning and they see you in the dark And you’re halfway there if you take it all to heart Nine miles high, electric is the night They’re anywhere and everywhere you are Our musical trip ends in London, England with a Britpop track helping someone with a serious technology addiction, where sunshine seems so far behind: "A friend of ours was going absolutely mental, spending too much time online and believing in all the noise. He got really paranoid, thought people were spying on him through his phone. The song is about pulling someone out of that hole." spotify:track:3vxnvMgQC2JnnSBqpi3Xgo:small #MusicMonday 2025 It's time to recap 2025 with this year's compilation. 76 titles from original artists covering all types of genres, represent the multiple trends independent artists were proud to establish. Have a listen: spotify:playlist:71fadol0hgsdSjLpM828HZ Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!
records.author: osornios

records.text: Ongoing Issues Review: April 2026 Hey folks! Welcome to this month's Ongoing Issues Review. We want to keep you in the loop with regards to some of the most trending ongoing issues reported here, in the Spotify Community. We're sharing updates from behind the scenes to let you know what's been fixed, what we’re tracking, and what we need your help with. Still experiencing an issue? You can check the full Ongoing Issue section here . FIXED 1. Interaction with AI DJ does not work 📌 We’ve received reports that for some folks, interactions with the AI DJ showed the error “Your current speaker does not support interaction with DJ”, even when no external speaker was connected. If you’re still experiencing this, please make sure your Spotify app is up to date. 2. [Android Auto] Larger playlists do not load when offline Reports came in of larger playlists not loading in Android Auto when offline. Some of you experienced a “Spotify is not responding” message on the car screen. This should now works as expected! 3. Cannot open desktop player on MacBook 📌 The Spotify desktop app was failing to launch on macOS for some users. Should you still experience this, please make sure to give these steps a try. If it persists, let us know here in the Community and we'll investigate. 4. Mandopop category not loading in Browse The Mandopop genre page in Browse appeared empty or failed to load content. We appreciate the reports, and rest assured things are all fixed here too! 5. Sorting albums by release year from oldest to newest makes the library not to work Applying this sort order caused Your Library to stop functioning or displaying properly. This has been fixed! 6. Can’t play downloaded songs on search on offline mode Downloaded tracks could not be played from Search results when the app was offline. Thanks for all the information provided whilst this was looked into, we've got no new reports here! 7. Spotify Personalized Mixes don't show up Personalized mix playlists were missing entirely from users’ libraries or Home views. These now appear correctly again. 8. popup screen every time app takes focus (choose a device to connect ) Reports came in that some folks were being repeatedly prompted to select a playback device whenever returning to the app. This is now fixed 9. Smart Shuffle feature missing on playlists The Smart Shuffle toggle had disappeared from some playlists. This is all fixed now, and as always we appreciate the reports. 10. Custom Order sorting option missing inside folders Playlists inside folders couldn’t be manually reordered. The Custom Order option is available again! 11. If a local file is FLAC, the artist name repeats with a semicolon Reports came in to let us know Artist metadata was duplicated for FLAC local files. This is now resolved. 12. Black screen when playing music videos on Google TV Streamer 4K 📌 Videos played audio only and showed a black screen for some folks. A working solution was identified and shared in the thread here . 13. Toggle for the Auto-Download feature goes off screen on iOS if the name of a podcast is too long Some UI issues were identified, causing the Auto-Download toggle to be pushed out of view. This has been corrected. 14. Queued Songs Not Being Tracked in On Repeat Playlist Reports came in to let us know songs played from the queue were not influencing the On Repeat playlist. This has been fixed. 15. Recommended songs at the bottom of my playlist won't refresh - button does not work The Refresh button for recommendations wasn't generating a new list. This is now working as expected. 16. Liked songs appear empty across devices Some folks' Liked Songs libraries showed as empty across multiple devices. This is now fixed! 17. Discover Weekly suggesting songs from family members listening history and updating twice a week Reports came in to suggest recommendations were being influenced by Family members activity and refreshed twice a week. This has now been fixed. UNDER INVESTIGATION 18. IOS Spotify app resumes playback after disconnecting bluetooth audio device On iOS, we're getting reports that Spotify unexpectedly starts or resumes playback after a Bluetooth device is disconnected. This is currently being looked into by the relevant team. 19. Spotify disconnects from bluetooth when open and changes playlists Bluetooth audio appears to drop when some of you are opening Spotify or switching playlists, forcing manual reconnection. Our teams are actively investigating this. 20. Make this a playlist feature is creating a copy of the whole Liked Songs playlist We've seen reports that using the Make this a playlist option incorrectly duplicates all Liked Songs instead of only the selected subset. This is under investigation. 21. Can't upload show art Reports confirm some folks are unable to upload or update artwork for podcast shows. This is being investigated. NOT RIGHT NOW 22. Desktop App now hiding media controls when using Expand Now Playing view button 📌 This is currently expected, however if you'd like you can add your vote to this idea . 23. Certain UI elements overlap on foldable devices 📌 We’re aware of this, and whilst there’s no ETA for a full resolution right now, rest assured it's on the team’s radar. In the meantime, we recommend keeping the app up to date. 24. Lyrics don't stay on the screen when changing songs 📌 We can confirm that this is expected. However, our Idea Exchange is always open for votes or to submit your own here . 25. "Your Episodes" can't be removed from Your Library 📌 We’ve received reports that Your Episodes can’t be removed from the Your Library section on desktop. The team has identified a temporary workaround, which requires changes on our side. If you’re experiencing this, please use the Send us a message option on this page so we can assist you directly. 27. Short silence tracks on playlist excluded from shuffle 📌 Shuffle mode will remove duplicate tracks from the queue to prevent accidental repetition, which helps keep sessions more varied and fresh. If you’d prefer to hear repeated tracks, you can switch to linear playback. 28. Command-L focuses search field but doesn't put cursor there This behavior is known and remains on our radar, we recommend keeping your app up to date. 29. Tab switching resets view state instead of preserving scroll/navigation position Right now, resetting scroll and navigation position when switching tabs is currently known and expected. We appreciate the comments. What if you spot something? We encourage you to check our Ongoing Issues board and hit +VOTE if you’re affected, it really helps! Need quick help? Post your question in the Help Boards. Got feedback? Share it in Ideas. Want to see some changes before other users you can sign-up to join our thousands of Beta testers here . Thank you for all your reports and for sharing in the Spotify Community! The Community Team 💙
records.author: MihailY

metadata.title: Ongoing Issues Review: April 2026
metadata.search_snippet: Hey folks! Welcome to this month's Ongoing Issues Review. We want to keep you in the loop with regards to some of the most trending ongoing issues reported here, in the Spotify Community. We're sharing updates from behind the scenes to let you know what's been fixed, what we’re tracking, and what we need your help with. Still experiencing an issue? You can check the full Ongoing Issue section here . FIXED 1. Interaction with AI DJ does not work 📌 We’ve received reports that for some folks, interactions with the AI DJ showed the error “Your current speaker does not support interaction with DJ”, even when no external speaker was connected. If you’re still experiencing this, please make sure your Spotify app is up to date. 2. [Android Auto] Larger playlists do not load when offline Reports came in of larger playlists not loading in Android Auto when offline. Some of you experienced a “Spotify is not responding” message on the car screen. This should now works as expected! 3. Cannot open desktop player on MacBook 📌 The Spotify desktop app was failing to launch on macOS for some users. Should you still experience this, please make sure to give these steps a try. If it persists, let us know here in the Community and we'll investigate. 4. Mandopop category not loading in Browse The Mandopop genre page in Browse appeared empty or failed to load content. We appreciate the reports, and rest assured things are all fixed here too! 5. Sorting albums by release year from oldest to newest makes the library not to work Applying this sort order caused Your Library to stop functioning or displaying properly. This has been fixed! 6. Can’t play downloaded songs on search on offline mode Downloaded tracks could not be played from Search results when the app was offline. Thanks for all the information provided whilst this was looked into, we've got no new reports here! 7. Spotify Personalized Mixes don't show up Personalized mix playlists were missing entirely from users’ libraries or Home views. These now appear correctly again. 8. popup screen every time app takes focus (choose a device to connect ) Reports came in that some folks were being repeatedly prompted to select a playback device whenever returning to the app. This is now fixed 9. Smart Shuffle feature missing on playlists The Smart Shuffle toggle had disappeared from some playlists. This is all fixed now, and as always we appreciate the reports. 10. Custom Order sorting option missing inside folders Playlists inside folders couldn’t be manually reordered. The Custom Order option is available again! 11. If a local file is FLAC, the artist name repeats with a semicolon Reports came in to let us know Artist metadata was duplicated for FLAC local files. This is now resolved. 12. Black screen when playing music videos on Google TV Streamer 4K 📌 Videos played audio only and showed a black screen for some folks. A working solution was identified and shared in the thread here . 13. Toggle for the Auto-Download feature goes off screen on iOS if the name of a podcast is too long Some UI issues were identified, causing the Auto-Download toggle to be pushed out of view. This has been corrected. 14. Queued Songs Not Being Tracked in On Repeat Playlist Reports came in to let us know songs played from the queue were not influencing the On Repeat playlist. This has been fixed. 15. Recommended songs at the bottom of my playlist won't refresh - button does not work The Refresh button for recommendations wasn't generating a new list. This is now working as expected. 16. Liked songs appear empty across devices Some folks' Liked Songs libraries showed as empty across multiple devices. This is now fixed! 17. Discover Weekly suggesting songs from family members listening history and updating twice a week Reports came in to suggest recommendations were being influenced by Family members activity and refreshed twice a week. This has now been fixed. UNDER INVESTIGATION 18. IOS Spotify app resumes playback after disconnecting bluetooth audio device On iOS, we're getting reports that Spotify unexpectedly starts or resumes playback after a Bluetooth device is disconnected. This is currently being looked into by the relevant team. 19. Spotify disconnects from bluetooth when open and changes playlists Bluetooth audio appears to drop when some of you are opening Spotify or switching playlists, forcing manual reconnection. Our teams are actively investigating this. 20. Make this a playlist feature is creating a copy of the whole Liked Songs playlist We've seen reports that using the Make this a playlist option incorrectly duplicates all Liked Songs instead of only the selected subset. This is under investigation. 21. Can't upload show art Reports confirm some folks are unable to upload or update artwork for podcast shows. This is being investigated. NOT RIGHT NOW 22. Desktop App now hiding media controls when using Expand Now Playing view button 📌 This is currently expected, however if you'd like you can add your vote to this idea . 23. Certain UI elements overlap on foldable devices 📌 We’re aware of this, and whilst there’s no ETA for a full resolution right now, rest assured it's on the team’s radar. In the meantime, we recommend keeping the app up to date. 24. Lyrics don't stay on the screen when changing songs 📌 We can confirm that this is expected. However, our Idea Exchange is always open for votes or to submit your own here . 25. "Your Episodes" can't be removed from Your Library 📌 We’ve received reports that Your Episodes can’t be removed from the Your Library section on desktop. The team has identified a temporary workaround, which requires changes on our side. If you’re experiencing this, please use the Send us a message option on this page so we can assist you directly. 27. Short silence tracks on playlist excluded from shuffle 📌 Shuffle mode will remove duplicate tracks from the queue to prevent accidental repetition, which helps keep sessions more varied and fresh. If you’d prefer to hear repeated tracks, you can switch to linear playback. 28. Command-L focuses search field but doesn't put cursor there This behavior is known and remains on our radar, we recommend keeping your app up to date. 29. Tab switching resets view state instead of preserving scroll/navigation position Right now, resetting scroll and navigation position when switching tabs is currently known and expected. We appreciate the comments. What if you spot something? We encourage you to check our Ongoing Issues board and hit +VOTE if you’re affected, it really helps! Need quick help? Post your question in the Help Boards. Got feedback? Share it in Ideas. Want to see some changes before other users you can sign-up to join our thousands of Beta testers here . Thank you for all your reports and for sharing in the Spotify Community! The Community Team 💙

records.text: Support and report Here are more powerful kid-friendly and no-swearing ideas that Spotify or other music apps could add: Clean Lyrics & Safe Listening “No Swearing Ever” mode. AI that removes curse words automatically. Clean-only search results. Kid-safe lyric checker before songs play. Replace bad words with funny sound effects. Family-safe radio stations. And Crashes reports A powerful feature for Spotify could be: “Scan Before Listening” Mode Before a song plays, the app could automatically scan: Lyrics Swear words Violent content Sexual content Hate speech Loud scary sounds Disturbing themes Explicit cover art Then it could warn or block the song before you hear it. Powerful Features Green Safe Badge Shows songs that are fully family-friendly. Yellow Warning Badge Mild language or emotional themes. Red Block Badge Explicit or inappropriate songs blocked automatically. AI Lyrics Scanner Reads lyrics in seconds before playback. Live Audio Detection Detects hidden swearing even if lyrics are missing. Parent Safe Mode Parents lock unsafe music completely. “Only Positive Music” Filter Allows uplifting songs only. Holiday Safe Listening Christmas, Easter, Thanksgiving, Hanukkah, and family holiday playlists checked automatically. Community Reporting System Users help report unsafe songs or playlists. Custom Word Blocking Block specific curse words or topics. Safe Podcast Scanner Scans podcasts before listening too. Mood Safety Check Warns about extremely dark or disturbing songs. Cover Art Scanner Blurs inappropriate album covers. Trusted Creator Verification Shows artists and playlists with family-safe reputations. Kid Voice Assistant “This song may not be safe for children.” School & Church Safe Mode Automatically filters playlists for classrooms or family gatherings. AI Music Clean Version Creator Generates cleaner versions of songs when possible. Family Dashboard Parents can review listening history safely. Positive Community Rewards Rewards creators for uplifting content. Safe Concert & Live Event Filtering Filters livestream concerts with family settings. That kind of system would make streaming music much safer, more family-friendly, and more caring for listeners of all ages. And a lot of security and functions and report even videos like **bleep** shaking Automatic skipping of explicit songs. “Only Positive Lyrics” setting. Safe versions of trending songs. Block songs with harmful topics. Kid-Friendly Music Modes Cartoon music mode. Disney-style musical playlists. Bedtime lullaby worlds. Animal-themed songs and sounds. Train adventure music playlists. Space exploration soundtracks. Superhero workout music for kids. Singalong bouncing lyrics. Learning songs with animations. Happy-only playlist filter. Parent Controls Parents approve playlists before listening. Weekly listening reports for families. Time limits for music sessions. “School mode” with calm focus music. Block certain artists or genres. Family shared safe library. Age-based music recommendations. Emergency mute for explicit audio. Separate kids and adult profiles. Parent-controlled downloads. Positive Community Features Kindness playlists. Anti-bullying music channels. Encouraging voice messages between songs. Safe comment sections. Family concert livestreams. Positive artist badges. Uplifting music awards. Community talent shows for kids. Safe friend playlist sharing. Reporting system for unsafe songs or playlists. Fun & Creative Features Music-themed mini games. Interactive lyric coloring books. Storybook songs with narration. Holiday magic playlists. Musical treasure hunts. Instrument learning mode. Dance challenge mode for families. Custom cartoon playlist covers. Virtual music worlds for children. Calm rain, snow, and nature music adventures. Advanced AI Ideas AI lyric cleaner for any song. AI detects hidden inappropriate meanings. Emotion-safe playlists. Smart “happy mood” music generator. AI-generated educational songs. Auto-lower loud sounds for kids. Family-safe remix creator. Personalized encouragement playlists. AI bedtime storyteller with music. Voice-controlled kid-safe DJ assistant. These ideas help create: safer music listening, family-friendly entertainment, positive learning experiences, and cleaner lyrics without swearing or harmful content. Here are even more powerful ideas for a super family-friendly and caring version of Spotify with safe lyrics, holiday themes, live events, protection systems, and community support: Powerful Kids & Family Safety Features Automatic swear-word blocker in songs. AI removes or mutes curse words live. “Kids Safe Lyrics Only” mode. Family-approved playlists only. Instant reporting for inappropriate songs. AI scans lyrics before songs are added. Block violent or hateful lyrics automatically. Parent dashboard with safety controls. Safe podcast and audiobook filtering. Anti-bullying music moderation system. Caring & Support Features Mental wellness playlists. Positive encouragement messages. Calm-down mode for stress. Community kindness rewards. Emergency support music resources. Friendly moderation teams. Positive creator badges. “Hope & Healing” music section. Family support communities. Inspirational daily music recommendations. Holiday & Seasonal Worlds Christmas Santa village music world. Snowfall visual music player. Carol singalong mode. Christmas countdown concerts. Family fireplace ambience. Hanukkah Hanukkah candle music experience. Dreidel-themed playlists. Peace and light visual themes. Kwanzaa Cultural celebration playlists. Family unity music collections. Easter Spring celebration themes. Inspirational Easter music. Animated Easter egg playlist hunt. Thanksgiving Family dinner soundtrack mode. Gratitude playlist sharing. Cozy autumn ambience. Halloween Family-friendly spooky music. Haunted house sound experiences. Costume party DJ mode. Memorial Day Honor and remembrance music. Patriotic orchestra collections. Martin Luther King Jr. Day Inspirational speeches with music. Peace and unity playlist events. Live Events & Outside Experiences Outdoor concert maps. Family-safe music festivals. Live synchronized fireworks music. Theme park soundtrack events. Interactive holiday concerts. Community singalong nights. Neighborhood music celebrations. Inside App Worlds Virtual Christmas town. Interactive music museums. Animated train ride music journeys. Space exploration soundtrack worlds. Fantasy castles with orchestral music. Storybook music adventures. Seasonal changing app backgrounds. Family game-night music hubs. Live choir performances. Music-powered visual effects. Unlimited Powerful AI Features AI family DJ assistant. AI lyric cleaner. AI-generated holiday remixes. Smart emotional music balancing. AI detects unsafe playlist names. Auto-block explicit album art. AI-generated positive radio stations. Voice-controlled family playlists. Safe music discovery engine. Personalized family celebration soundtracks. Advanced Community Protection One-tap report system. Auto-review for dangerous content. Trusted family creator verification. Safe comments only mode. Child-protected public profiles. AI moderation 24/7. Anti-harassment protections. Family-friendly livestream moderation. Safer sharing permissions. Positive community ranking system. Cinematic & Magical Features Music synchronized snow effects. Galaxy star music visualizers. Movie-style soundtrack creator. Real-time choir harmonies. Disney-style fantasy transitions. Holiday parade livestreams. Magical orchestra experiences. Interactive storytelling playlists. Soundtrack builder for family movies. Music adventures with animated scenes. These ideas would make Spotify: safer for children, more caring and uplifting, family-focused, holiday-friendly, creative, magical, and community-centered.
records.author: Adamlazar

records.text: #MusicMonday Review - November 2025 #MusicMonday is the hashtag I've been using for quite a while to share music recommendations from up-and-coming artists. Always fresh, and always different, trying to look for trends before they become one. You can check October's Part 2 review for more music. Is life a blessing or a curse? When situations drag you down, this issue's tracks make you realize you may not be the only one there, and things can get better. Give them a listen, with a word from the artists themselves. 🎧 Craig Edmonds – August Flowers Locked out Of my brain again I feel knocked out Every now and then Like there's a chain lock Buried deep within And I can't find the combination to get me in We begin in London, England, for a singer & songwriter Indie Pop track about feeling distant with who you are, and letting things go: "It started as a poem really, the chorus was a standalone thing I wrote before the music (something I don't usually do) and then everything followed from that. To me, the words were about depression creeping in. When its something you've lived with for a long time, you understand that it isn't going anywhere, but how you recognize it, and how you cope with it are the parameters that can change. So I wrote the verses to match that sentiment. How it feels to me to describe depression to someone in the first verse, and reassurance/hope in the second verse. I knew the song needed a lift somewhere, so in the middle 8 its like an affirmation of feeling grateful to be alive and to be breathing and present." spotify:track:6nRE6hksBAbsBHsSJAETqf:small Richie Flo – REGGAE (DO WHAT WE WANT) Don’t rush! Girl we ago tek wi time My aim is to entertain yuh, for the night, Wooii Cause inna reggae, I a pro and mi nuh no novice So when yu see mi di vibe mi bring and it nuh diminish So mek we reggae guaranteed ﬁ mek yuh astonish No hablo ingles, yeah, we do it even in Spanish Seh don’t be silly, reggae music nah go vanish Di worl a ﬁ wi, reggae music dun govern it Seh inna reggae bands man a play di snares a hit Wi nah guh lef it, Roots rock and rub a dub a it We fly now to St. Thomas, Jamaica for a Reggae track about dancing all night long, to that drum and bass sound, with the girl of your dreams right next to you. This groove is like magic: "I received the instrumental from House of Riddim. When I heard the beat I immediately thought of doing a authentic reggae song. The song is a love song to reggae music. It is difficult to say exactly what inspired the song. I wanted to create a song that reggae music lovers would share a similar appreciation for the genre." spotify:track:3y7Ihy5RwgguUA92HWHYc2:small The Corvids – Ghost (In Your Arms) You've heard enough of my voice choke me while I try to speak the city's in flames but the fire won't speak for me I'm a minute from home but I feel so far away and I don't care if I don't but you made me feel so safe in your arms that day We go back to Pontefract, England for an Alternative Rock track about longing for a moment of your past you won't let go, without saying anything out loud: "When we wrote Ghost (In Your Arms) , the idea was to create an energetic crowd-pleaser. We stripped things back technically to keep it simple but gave it a strong, driving pulse that we knew would translate into something fun for our live sets. It’s also a way for us to break up our discography a little—injecting a track that’s built less on complexity and more on immediacy. From the very start, we wanted people to be able to recognize it within the first few seconds, something that hooks them right away and sets the tone for the rest of the song." spotify:track:6dqIVjjVcMyI9CKSyx8pcg:small The PainKillers – Do You Know can you see me see my tears are we really seeing clear can you feel it feel my fear I'm falling down now Hold me near We now travel west to Manchester for a L o-Fi, Alt Rock infused track about those strong feelings that make you question everything about yourself. Do you know? "It was from an experience I had coming off some awful medication I had to take for my chronic pain condition it really effected me and made me feel super low so I got quite down and depressed and it’s about realizing your changing and doing something about it as I know lots of people experience that too one way or another hope that makes sense ha!" spotify:track:2DB6qXte00q8f2XqGOy0Ke:small Shady Quid – Dogtail I’ve been frantically running around Running round in circles wearing myself down I’ve dug myself into another hole in the ground I’m deep down below and there’s no one around I shout up for help but they can’t hear a sound I'm stuck hear forever in this pit I am bound I deserve to be here but I want to be found I want to be found We end up in Newcastle upon Tyne, England, for a Country influenced, Americana track about life being unfair, where you never seem to win. Are we paying for our sins? "I would say it’s about the poor young man’s experience with moving from adolescence to adulthood. The metaphor of the pit or hole he finds himself in after wearing himself down with worry is carried through both verses. Ultimately, at the end of the song, it’s about realizing that you have to grow up and stop feeling sorry for yourself, and therefore becoming an adult." spotify:track:3J7NT7TeCUPnwMwTO1uZPG:small Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!
records.author: osornios

This groove is like magic: "I received the instrumental from House of Riddim. When I heard the beat I immediately thought of doing a authentic reggae song. The song is a love song to reggae music. It is difficult to say exactly what inspired the song. I wanted to create a song that reggae music lovers would share a similar appreciation for the genre." spotify:track:3y7Ihy5RwgguUA92HWHYc2:small The Corvids – Ghost (In Your Arms) You've heard enough of my voice choke me while I try to speak the city's in flames but the fire won't speak for me I'm a minute from home but I feel so far away and I don't care if I don't but you made me feel so safe in your arms that day We go back to Pontefract, England for an Alternative Rock track about longing for a moment of your past you won't let go, without saying anything out loud: "When we wrote Ghost (In Your Arms) , the idea was to create an energetic crowd-pleaser. We stripped things back technically to keep it simple but gave it a strong, driving pulse that we knew would translate into something fun for our live sets. It’s also a way for us to break up our discography a little—injecting a track that’s built less on complexity and more on immediacy. From the very start, we wanted people to be able to recognize it within the first few seconds, something that hooks them right away and sets the tone for the rest of the song." spotify:track:6dqIVjjVcMyI9CKSyx8pcg:small The PainKillers – Do You Know can you see me see my tears are we really seeing clear can you feel it feel my fear I'm falling down now Hold me near We now travel west to Manchester for a L o-Fi, Alt Rock infused track about those strong feelings that make you question everything about yourself. Do you know? "It was from an experience I had coming off some awful medication I had to take for my chronic pain condition it really effected me and made me feel super low so I got quite down and depressed and it’s about realizing your changing and doing something about it as I know lots of people experience that too one way or another hope that makes sense ha!" spotify:track:2DB6qXte00q8f2XqGOy0Ke:small Shady Quid – Dogtail I’ve been frantically running around Running round in circles wearing myself down I’ve dug myself into another hole in the ground I’m deep down below and there’s no one around I shout up for help but they can’t hear a sound I'm stuck hear forever in this pit I am bound I deserve to be here but I want to be found I want to be found We end up in Newcastle upon Tyne, England, for a Country influenced, Americana track about life being unfair, where you never seem to win. Are we paying for our sins? "I would say it’s about the poor young man’s experience with moving from adolescence to adulthood. The metaphor of the pit or hole he finds himself in after wearing himself down with worry is carried through both verses. Ultimately, at the end of the song, it’s about realizing that you have to grow up and stop feeling sorry for yourself, and therefore becoming an adult." spotify:track:3J7NT7TeCUPnwMwTO1uZPG:small Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!

records.text: In Our Headphones: January 2026 Howdy folks, Welcome to 2026! We're kicking off the year by bringing back one of our favorite musical traditions, 'In Our Headphones'. There's no better way to beat the winter chill we're having here, than by sharing the tracks that have been keeping us moving, thinking, and vibing over the last few weeks. And as the famous saying goes: "New Year, New Beats!". So welcome one and all, to "In Our Headphones" January 2026 ! Our team of Community Moderators have been busy hitting "play" all month long, and we're thrilled to kick-off the first edition in the year of the fiery horse. We’ve gathered a diverse collection of tracks directly from the personal playlists of team members Vasil, Joan, Ivelina, Dessi, Elena, and Kiril. Whether they were discovering new indie gems, or revisiting timeless classics, these are the songs that defined their January. Starting off with @Vasil 's who wanted to share with us a pleasant new favorite of his from a young Bulgarian artist named Mona with the tune called Sila (meaning strength/power in Bulgarian). spotify:track:4MT6GrseYj0lRmXh6CS8jU:small Next up, another song that re-emerged in his most listened tracks was an old favorite of his which he used to listen to a lot while he was working abroad in the US! It's always a nice feeling when you rediscover a song that's been buried down in your playlists for a while. spotify:track:3l9CW99AHtExIRV4hW2N5m:small To top things off, we have the newest addition to his Liked Songs, expanding his collection of "phonk" masterpieces! spotify:track:66iH61IAYwCNnQG6qJ1gIy Moving on, we have the submissions of @Joan . Continuing a trend he's been on for a while now, January has been very space/sci-fi themed! He claims to remain clueless what it is about this time of year that brings these vibes, yet he states that it keeps happening to him. Here's what he shared in his own words: "This time round, I finally got to explore the discography of the young Ukrainian band with the amazing name Labyrinthus Stellarum (sorry to anyone if a portal to another dimension opened in your room if you read that out loud). Their synth-heavy black metal songs brimming with awesome themes of space, sci-fi and aliens are really atmospheric and immersive, yet also have some beautiful melodies and are really catchy and fun too! Each album is a banger in its own way. They remind me of my favourites from Mechina and that's a big compliment coming from me!" spotify:track:1GOUPRx5hdTLoId4MwhU0Q spotify:track:56m3H4IrnOC3TYdVoELSqA spotify:track:673mWKIRiWIiAXixEmtRgz Speaking of artists that sound like other artists that Joan loves, he recently found this guy Master Boot Record, who very much reminded him of his chum The Algorithm . Joan added: "He has his own style though, very chiptune, fun and epic metal-infused electronic bangers. Didn't think I'd ever find another worthy computer metal artist, but I'm sure glad that I did." spotify:track:0gWWHHxdPOGrda1IXGpDoY spotify:track:0G79BRxeEzF0OcjCriLEV5 spotify:track:2Z8Cy3SGbCXEMrAdIsdIX5 Now comes the time to hear the special submissions of @Ivelina ! Why special you might ask? Keep on reading and you'll find out... Ivelina has been going to Pilates classes for a little over a year now, and she's been enjoying some very relaxing and very awesome sounding reggae remixes of famous songs. Here we have the two in particular that she loves the most and she also adds that: "They simply make the workouts so much more enjoyable!" https://open.spotify.com/track/622x3ps1iSIjTZPcEMCwnX?si=5f3ed816d16f4a54 https://open.spotify.com/track/3UESPBdyP6tRay7KoZOIGp?si=d7146e2c80dc4ac6 And now, the cozy submissions of @Dessi ! Here's what she said about her picks: " I don't know if it's because of the cold weather and spending a lot of time cosying at home, but Aretha always warms my soul so I've been enjoying her music a lot lately!" spotify:track:3NfxSdJnVdon1axzloJgba:small Dessi also eagerly waits for her Discover Weekly to come out, as she's been getting some pretty good recommendations in the past couple of months. Here are some of her latest finds: spotify:track:2qcht4Pl9MOi1EIjmpsz1B:small spotify:track:29pp9gao3MsPN3dp5mrrgf:small spotify:track:77sfcW5VjDAbZwmdIqCKKb:small Moving from space, to pilates, to cozy songs we now have @Elena with a brand new theme. Elena has been on an "organizing and cleaning spree" in her house throughout the months of December and January. Her motivation has been powered exclusively by Florence and the Machine. She admits that: "The whole album has been on constant repeat and max volume at all times. Besides the 2 singles Kraken has been one of my absolute faves - so strong and unexpected!" https://open.spotify.com/track/4BswiLDcr4ChDGl2NrfhC1?si=ffb2a8d526584511 Thank you so much for sharing everyone. Finally, we arrive at my submissions (yep, @Kiril 's)! Maybe I've been sleeping under a rock, or maybe taste for good music comes at a later stage (age) in life, but this January I've finally appreciated the great music of the Grateful Dead. I can happily say that I am a newly recruited "deadhead" now! Enjoy two of their songs that always go together back to back on their live concerts! https://open.spotify.com/track/3euDGpS2R0NC2Xssqxohva?si=10563104d8dc4f03 https://open.spotify.com/track/4DpBfWl3q8e0gGB76lAaox?si=4877894fd6294149 So we close the door on another month of our Moderators favourite songs, and that's it for January! We hope these tracks brought some fresh energy to your speakers! We’d love to hear what you think of our picks, and more importantly, what have you been listening to lately? Head over to the comment section to share your own January jams. The full playlist is below, happy listening! https://open.spotify.com/playlist/6lv6DjjryalvuDqg0oirRb?si=cKSvcxjSRo2k4C3SaCjstA

records.text: I miss when Spotify felt human I've used Spotify for a long time. I remember my dad telling me about this cool thing coming to the US way back in 2011 when I was a middle schooler. We shared an account between myself, my dad, and my brother. I dug around on Spotify looking for my favorite songs from the CDs that were burned just for my mom's minivan (now scratched or lost). Spacehog, Queens of the Stoneage, Snow Patrol, Death Cab, The Killers, Coldplay, Spoon, U2. I stopped using my iPod Nano because I loved Spotify so much. We would open Spotify at the family computer and play music while an oven pizza heated up. I vaguely remember the first lyrics feature coming out. I think they were partnered with Musixmatch, which is where I already went to find all the lyrics to songs I didn't get as a young child, but suddenly got as a teenager. It wasn't like the realtime lyrics they have now. Users would type in the lyrics to the songs they were listening to, which meant they were wrong half the time, but we were all getting them wrong together (reminiscent of 'Hold me closer, Tony Danza'). What I do remember all too vividly is Spotify phasing out the Starred playlist around 2014 or 2015, I think. One day, I opened the desktop app and the star button was GONE, my starred playlist was CAST ASIDE and replaced by 'Your Music' or something like that. The star was replaced with a heart, which, in hindsight, just looked like a play to align Spotify design with Instagram. But the heart was okay. It made sense. I spent years building playlists, deleting playlists, growing my music library, finding artists I hadn't heard of before. I got through pubescent depression with Spotify. I had my first kiss with music playing on Spotify. I drove my first car with Spotify, graduated high school with Spotify, moved 1,200 miles from my home with Spotify. I had a 17 hour playlist I'd been adding songs to since 2018 for one of my friends since we were living in different countries. Even now in 2026, I'm sitting on my couch in a boxed up apartment as I prepare to move in 60 hours. I've left my speaker unpacked so I can still enjoy music from my Spotify playlists as I get ready for a fresh start. I remember when I could finally start paying for my own premium subscription; I was earning less than $40K a year and made it a point to spare budget on the monthly so I could get my ad-less experience. I ate beans and rice for weeks , but wouldn't give up that subscription. But I feel like we're the only human thing left about Spotify, the users. When the heart button was replaced with a plus sign a few years ago, I think that was when I really noticed the change. It was small, but it felt significant. To take a representation of love and replace it with what was a effectively an add-to-cart button felt ugly. No hate to the designers. All the hate to the branding decision-makers. I'd bet a decent amount that a c-level or higher is the reason that change was even suggested in the first place. I don't want to come off as completely ungrateful. I found some of my favorite artists because of Spotify recommendations in my playlists or from Discover Weekly pre-2020. But the recent infiltration of genAI music and artists, genAI song/brand description features, AI DJ, and whatever other f*ckass features I've left out have made me feel gross. I'm not trying to con myself into thinking that early investors in Spotify were looking out for my listening experience, or that they cared about any of us. I'm just saying that now it feels like everything they do is to fill their already-full pockets. Remove the human element to increase profitability. At least have the decency to lie to me without contradicting the first half of your own mission statement: Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity—by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art. Isn't removing humanity from music dumb as sh*t? I just feel like music is such an inherent part of the human experience. Rhythm & melody are in us. You can hear it in our speech and see it in our strides. The thing is that there aren't other great streaming platforms. The smaller ones that have gained traction -- I don't trust them to not sell out if they gain any more traction. You won't catch me paying for a YouTube Music, Apple Music, or Amazon Music subscription; they're entirely capable of making sh*tty UI decisions without my extra data. But it's okay I think. I'm trying to recreate the CDs we used to keep in the minivan, and there are 2 music shops within a 7 minute walk from me. A neighbor gave me a CD/cassette deck for my new apartment. I fixed my busted record player with the help of a random guy I met at a Goodwill. I'm learning about more venues where I can find smaller, local bands. I'm going to try to ween myself off of Spotify. I hope it works out.

records.text: “Emotional Safety” or “Relapse-Avoidance” listening feature. Suggestion for Spotify: Emotional Safety & Relapse-Avoidance Feature Spotify could introduce a feature that allows users to automatically isolate and manage songs associated with emotional relapse, unhealthy memories, grief spirals, toxic relationships, addiction triggers, or depressive episodes. The goal is not to censor music, but to help users create healthier listening environments while still preserving personal choice and emotional awareness. Core Feature Concept Instead of only tagging songs manually, Spotify could automatically compile emotionally triggering songs into a separate private playlist or category based on: - Repeated skips - Emotional tagging - Listening behavior patterns - Late-night replay cycles - Sudden emotional listening spikes - User feedback and avoidance behavior These songs would remain accessible, but Spotify would avoid inserting them into: - Daily mixes - Autoplay - Recommended tracks - Mood playlists - Casual shuffle sessions - Regular sound trips This creates emotional boundaries without fully removing the music from the user’s library. Proposed Features 1. Automatic “Relapse Playlist” Compilation Spotify can create a private playlist containing songs users emotionally associate with: - Painful memories - Toxic relationships - Depression - Anxiety - Addiction relapse - Emotional spirals This playlist acts more like emotional quarantine than deletion. The songs stay available if the user intentionally wants to revisit them, but Spotify avoids casually resurfacing them during normal listening. 2. Emotional Separation System The system could: - Detect emotionally repetitive listening loops - Identify songs linked to emotional distress patterns - Separate these tracks from healthy daily recommendations - Prevent accidental emotional triggering during normal listening sessions 3. “Do Not Reintroduce” Option Users can choose: - Hide from autoplay - Hide from algorithmic recommendations - Hide from mood mixes - Hide during vulnerable hours (late nights, etc.) - Keep only inside the isolated playlist This gives users control without permanently deleting meaningful songs. 4. Emotional Awareness Prompts Optional reminders such as: «“This track is currently placed in your Emotional Boundary playlist.”» Or «“You previously chose to avoid this song during regular listening sessions.”» The prompts should remain subtle and respectful. 5. Recovery-Oriented Redirection Instead of replaying emotionally harmful loops, Spotify could gently redirect users toward: - Calmer music - Recovery playlists - Grounding sounds - Confidence-building tracks - New music discovery - Emotionally stabilizing recommendations Why This Matters Music strongly affects memory, emotional conditioning, and mental state. Many people unintentionally replay songs that reinforce: - Emotional dependency - Rumination - Depression - Heartbreak cycles - Substance cravings - Isolation A system that helps separate emotionally harmful patterns from daily listening could improve digital wellbeing while still respecting artistic freedom. Important Considerations - All emotional data should remain private and encrypted. - Users should always maintain full control over their music. - Songs should never be globally labeled as “negative.” - Emotional responses are personal and contextual. - The feature should support self-awareness, not censorship. Possible Feature Names - Emotional Boundaries - Recovery Mode - Mindful Listening - Safe Listening - Reset Playlist - Emotional Filter - Reflection Mode Closing Thought Sometimes healing is not about deleting memories, but preventing them from constantly reappearing when you’re trying to move forward. Music platforms already shape emotions through algorithms — this feature would simply give users more control over that emotional influence. Additional Feature Clarification: “Blocked Playlist” System The feature could work as a dedicated private “Blocked Playlist” where users can place songs they no longer want appearing in their regular listening experience. Instead of deleting or permanently hiding the music, Spotify would: - Compile these songs into a separate reviewable playlist - Prevent them from appearing in autoplay - Exclude them from Smart Shuffle - Remove them from recommendations and mood mixes - Avoid resurfacing them during casual listening sessions Users would still retain full access to the songs and could revisit the playlist anytime intentionally. This creates a healthier balance between: - preserving meaningful music memories - and preventing unwanted emotional triggering during everyday listening Key Difference From Existing Features Unlike simply hiding a song, this system would function more like: - an emotional boundary list - a controlled archive - or a personal “do not auto-surface” collection The important part is that Spotify’s algorithm would actively understand: «“Do not casually reintroduce these songs into my listening experience.”» Suggested User Controls Users could: - Add or remove songs anytime - Review blocked songs privately - Restore songs back into normal recommendations - Set temporary or permanent blocking - Block entire artists, albums, or playlists if desired Why It Matters Many users do not necessarily want to erase certain songs permanently. Some songs carry emotional significance, but users may not want them unexpectedly appearing during vulnerable moments, commutes, late nights, workouts, or random shuffle sessions. This feature would give users healthier emotional control over algorithmic resurfacing while still respecting personal choice and music ownership. Thank you for your time and consideration. I appreciate the work Spotify continues to do in creating deeply personal listening experiences for millions of users worldwide.
records.author: emilvaldivia
