records.text: AI DJ algorithm feels ad driven Plan Premium Country U.S My Question or Issue I tend to use DJ quite often but recently the use of top songs on Spotify or songs with the focus being popular with others and not my normal curated picks has left me dumbfounded. The songs I see given to me during my time using DJ has been flat out baffling. For some context I tend to stay near the same genres as Underground, Webcore, Shoegaze and Metal. But find DJ giving me songs from Mexico like Ranchera, or just plain Latin music in general. I also have noticed a uptick in what it calls currently top songs on Spotify which include the most out of touch for my algorithm mainstream songs to include things like Taylor Swift and more. Would it be possible at this moment in time to disable the music recommendations on DJ that tend to lean more into the what others enjoy rather then recommending what I may enjoy? I have a growing suspension that this DJ may be ad driven based on if the artist paid to be on the discovery incentive or not.
records.author: tritonpanda

records.text: Option to turn off AI features There should be the option to turn off AI features in the app settings. I'm having a less enjoyable experience using Spotify than 5 years ago when I first joined. It's far more complicated that it needs to be. I'm still unhappy about the desktop UI change in 2023. The AI prompted playlists are not good, and a waste of money in my opinion. I've attempted to use them to find new music, writing a detailed description of the kinds of sounds and genres I would like to hear whilst allowing it to scan my listening history. I find the recommendations are not allowing me to expand my music taste. They send you round in circles. I've had better results without AI through manual searches. Because of the UI layout, it difficult to browse for new music. I never use the AI DJ, it's a novelty I don't wish to pay for. I think if Spotify wants to experiment with AI, that's fine but they shouldn't inflict it on their users. If I could switch to an older version of Spotify I would instantly.

records.text: Add feedback option for Daily Mixes and Made for You playlists Hello everyone, I wanted to make a suggestion that I honestly think would really improve the Spotify experience. I feel like there should be an option for users to give feedback on Daily Mixes and the Made for You playlists . Sometimes the recommendations are great, but other times they feel kind of random and not really based on what I’m currently listening to. For example, there are times when a certain artist keeps showing up in my Daily Mix cover or in the playlist suggestions even though I barely listen to them anymore, and there’s no real way to let Spotify know that the recommendations are off. It would be really helpful if there was a feature where we could rate the playlist suggestions, like/dislike the mix as a whole, or leave feedback on whether the recommendations actually match our taste right now. I think this would help improve and make the personalized playlists feel more accurate and less random over time. Would anyone else like to see something like this?

records.text: Re: February 2026 Spotify for Developers update: thread This is why people will leave development on the Spotify platform. Things like this. This limiting of the API and placing it behind a paywall is going to negatively impact simple development. I'm am using the API to show real-time updates on own music via the /artists endpoint. With this update I'm inclined to switch to Apple Music with MusicKit, which has way better functionality then Spotify at the moment. The removal of the preview_url was a signal of the downfall. People will be inclined to switch to the Deezer API. But knowing the industry, I'd reckon that they'll too will update their API, so you'll be stuck on this endless cycle of finding a new service for simple tasks. Oh well, I'm happy that I have an Apple Developer account. And it's less of a *bleep*fest than these new ""improvements"". Do better Spotify. Fix the AI recommendations first. Of course no hate to ThePodfather since he's basically the messenger of bad news. Man, I feel bad for small hobbyist developers.

records.text: Bring back the old Discover Weekly algorithm Discover Weekly used to be the strongest reason I subscribed to Spotify. For years, it was how I found my favorite artists — songs I'd never have heard otherwise, but that landed perfectly in my taste. Something has changed. For months now, the tracks Discover Weekly serves me are not just unfamiliar, they're songs I actively dislike. The playlist no longer feels like discovery — it feels like a random feed. And it's not just me. Several friends and colleagues have brought this up, all describing the same experience: a playlist that used to feel hand-picked now feels generic and off-target. I'm the type of listener your discovery features were built for: someone who actively wants to find new artists. But discovery only works if the recommendations start from my actual taste. When that foundation breaks, I have no reason to stay on the platform. If Discover Weekly doesn't return to what it once was, I'll cancel my subscription — and based on the conversations I'm having, I won't be the only one.

records.text: Re: How do I clear the que on Android Galaxy Hey there @Karaptian , Thanks for getting in touch about this here in the Community. Currently, if you tap on a song in a playlist/album, the playback won't stop after that song, but it'll carry on to the next song in that playlist/album. If you only want to hear one song, you can search for it from the main Search menu and play it from there. If you don't want to get recommendations, you need to disable the Autoplay option from the app settings. That being said, you can only clear the songs/playlists from the queue you've added to it manually. For example, if you're listening to a playlist, and add a song to the queue, the "Clear" option will appear in the queue so you can remove it easily. Also, if you want to remove particular songs from it, you can swipe left them and they'll be removed. Hope this helps. If you need a hand with anything else, the Community is here for you.
records.author: Eni

If you don't want to get recommendations, you need to disable the Autoplay option from the app settings. For example, if you're listening to a playlist, and add a song to the queue, the "Clear" option will appear in the queue so you can remove it easily. Also, if you want to remove particular songs from it, you can swipe left them and they'll be removed. Hope this helps. If you need a hand with anything else, the Community is here for you.

records.text: Trying to filter explicit recommendations Hey guys. I have created a playlist, and Spotify is recommending a few songs to me. Most of the recommendations are fine, but there are a few songs in the "recommended based on what is in this playlist" section under my playlist that don't qualify as explicit because they don't have explicit lyrics, but have album covers with explicit nudity. I am trying to live a life my free of that, and I have tried many different times to get Spotify to stop recommending those songs, but it does not offer a way to remove those recommendations. The closest thing it offers is an option that says "exclude from taste profile," and then it is still there afterwards, saying that listening to the song won't affect what is recommended to my playlist. It is not helping at all. Is there anything I can do to get rid of these recommendations? I really don't want to scrap my playlist just so I don't have to see explicit content, but it's getting really irritating.

records.text: Re: Shared playlists & audiobooks not working on Kids/Managed accounts Glad I gave up on waiting for Spotify even before they decided to backtrack and retcon it to "this is what it is supposed to be like" - which is obviously not true, seeing that new releases are available on managed accounts. Like many in this thread, I cancelled my Premium Family subscription and after the recommendations here, we are trying out Deezer. So far I like it, it feels like Spotify used to be like a few years ago, fresher and snappier. And no videos and other useless detractions from what I had it for: LISTENING. To music AND radio plays. I miss Spotify Connect a little bit on my few devices that supported it, but Deezer has direct Google Cast & Airplay support that works well for us, and Connect didn't work at all for Managed Accounts anyway which for us made it almost useless. Spotify really botched their family account concept, and lost a decade-long faithful customer in the process. I'm not coming back.

Spotify really botched their family account concept, and lost a decade-long faithful customer in the process. I'm not coming back.

records.text: Re: Unwanted items in library Hi there, @Momma_Jo Thank you for your message. Just to clarify, it's considered as normal behavior of the app having in the home page "recommended for today" , "new releases for you" , "based on your listening" and other boards because that's how the Spotify algorithm works and that's how the app is built, so you can discover new music all the time. On another note, you can identify any recommended music content on the Now Playing Bar as it will have a label next to the artist name, like this one: Also, bear in mind that you can disable Smart Shuffle in your mobile app to avoid those added recommendations by following these steps: Tap your profile picture at the top. Tap Settings and privacy . Select Playback . Switch Include Smart Shuffle in play modes to off if it's on. Lastly, it's also worth checking if the " Autoplay " option is enabled in the same Playback section of the configuration. If you have any other doubts, just let us know!
records.author: OscarDC

records.text: Re: Saving Playlists by Spotify Hi there, @Beezkneez Thanks for reaching out! Just to confirm, do you mean playlists created directly by Spotify (daily mix, some artist's radio, weekly discovery, recommendations)? If you mean those types of playlists, keep in mind that the " daily mix " and the " weekly discovery " ones change every day or every week, so you cannot save them exactly as they were the first time you listened to them. If you liked one of Spotify's created playlists a lot, you can add it to your own library with the (+) button. Or you can create a new playlist from your own profile adding the songs you loved from those playlists to it, like @Eni mentioned before. If you're referring to playlists created by yourself and you are not able to save new songs there, you can share more details about it (the type of device you're using to save the content in your playlists with the corresponding OS version, and the version of your Spotify app). Keep us posted!
records.author: OscarDC

records.text: Re: When listening to Release Radar or Discover Weekly and hiding a song, the wrong song is playe... Hi there, @supershippy Thanks for reaching out! Keep in mind that when you hide a song on Release Radar or Discover Weekly, the app might skip one or more songs, playing a track further down the list rather than the next one in order. This issue may be happening because the Hide function acts as a skip rather than removing the song entirely. Also, take into account that Hide song may only affect your current session and could be not synched between devices (a song might be hidden in a specific playlist on your mobile but then you can see it again on your desktop device on the same playlist). We suggest using the Don't play this option instead of just hiding the song. You can go to the artist's profile > 3-dots menu > Don't play this artist to permanently block them and improve your recommendations. Hope this helps! Keep us posted!
records.author: Paula_Vi

records.text: Re: Spotify Suggestions Hey @autonewman, thanks for providing some more context, I understand what you mean now. You can influence your taste profile by using the 'x' and '+' buttons on mobile. So, when autoplay is active and new songs are queued after the end of a playlist, you can tap 'x' on the songs that you don't like or want to be played. That should hopefully shake up the algorithm so that it recommends a wider variety of songs. If you're noticing this happening on one specific device, it's worth trying a clean reinstall of the app, which gets rid of any cache or cookies that could be influencing the algorithm. Let me know if that changes anything. Regarding paid song recommendations, I am not too clued up on this subject, but I did find an article which goes into detail as to how that works (specifically the last paragraph on commercial considerations). I don't think this is necessarily the case for you, but still worth a read.

records.text: Chat-based music recommendations using natural language (mood & daily context) Hello Spotify team and community, I’d like to share an idea based on my daily listening experience. I really enjoy discovering music on Spotify, especially when I unexpectedly encounter songs I’ve never heard before — both new releases and older hidden gems. It feels similar to listening to the radio in the past, but more personal. I was thinking it would be great if Spotify had a chat-based feature, where users could describe their current mood or situation in natural language, for example: “I want relaxing, instrumental music for my morning commute.” Then Spotify could respond with several playlists like: Calm & gentle morning focus Stress-free commute Soft instrumental music to ease into work This would allow users to explore music not by genre or artist, but by how they feel in that moment. I believe this kind of interaction could make music discovery more flexible, intuitive, and emotionally engaging. Thank you for considering this idea!
records.author: MusicForestExplorer

records.text: Block specific podcasts from auto-playing or appearing in recommendations I rarely listen to podcasts, but tonight I decided to put on a sleep podcast since I was having trouble falling asleep. Unfortunately, after the sleep podcast ended Spotify started auto-playing a popular podcast that included an extremely graphic, violent and sexual rant. I woke up having a nightmare as a result of Spotify playing this disgusting content that could have been prevented.Turning off explicit content wouldn't have helped because the podcast wasn't even flagged as explicit in the first place, and besides I'm an adult and don't want to exclude good music just because it has an F bomb or two. I already reported the podcast, but there's no way to stop that specific podcast from auto-playing or showing up on my home page again which is disappointing. Heck, there's not even a way to mark it as "not interested" which would have been at least somewhat helpful.

records.text: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs I think this is becoming an urgent issue. My Discover Weekly (and, more generally, the entire Spotify experience) is becoming increasingly crowded with AI-generated music. In my opinion, these songs do not have the same artistic merit, creativity, or emotional depth as music created by humans. Therefore, I do not want AI-generated songs included in my recommendations. As a user, it is becoming increasingly frustrating to distinguish genuine artists and songs from AI-generated tracks that are often produced in large quantities simply to generate revenue from the platform. It ruins the user experience because you have to go track by track detecting if it’s a real song or AI slop. Please consider implementing an option in the user settings that allows listeners to exclude AI-generated music from their recommendations. Otherwise, many users may choose to migrate to other platforms that place a greater emphasis on supporting music and human artistry.

records.text: Betreff: LABEL AI MUSIC Right, Spotify community managers, moderators and rock stars should at least merge related ideas to collect the votes in one thread in my opinion. Concerning recognising and labeling AI-generated content you can use Deezer, they have a software for that and put a note about it on their album pages. They also automatically exclude these releases from your recommendations, so you don't have to do that yourself. It's a lot of work having to check all Spotify tracks in Discover Weekly and Release Radar (230 per week in my case) in Deezer of course, and I might just quit listening to them and use the related Deezer playlists instead. Deezer is still available for free in some countries, so you don't have to subscribe to them if you only want to check Spotify's AI music. But they have lossless streaming for a long time already, and I also like the better sound compared to Spotify Free.

records.text: Block genres After six months of Discover Weekly trying to convert me, I have come to the conclusion that my Spotify account may be possessed by the spirit of Jesus Christ. Every Monday I perform an exorcism by hiding tracks, skipping songs and blocking artists. Yet the following week He returns. Stronger. More determined. More CEDM. Meanwhile, Daylist, AI DJ and my Mixes understand my taste perfectly. Only Discover Weekly seems convinced that my true calling is "Jesus Is The King (D&B Remix)". My suggestion: please give users the option to block an entire genre from recommendations. Sometimes I don't dislike a few artists—I simply never want recommendations from a specific genre cluster. For now I have blocked every Christian EDM artist I could find. If next week's Discover Weekly is still full of worship drum & bass, I will assume Spotify's recommendation engine has achieved sentience and is actively trying to save my soul.

records.text: Re: Despite Premium plan, randomly my currently playing playlist is abandoned and forced into shu... Hey, @20joneskat , Welcome to the Spotify Community! I suspect that this might have something to do with the Autoplay feature - when your playlist ends, it populates the play queue with similar song recommendations so that the playback never ends. You can disable it in the app's Settings and privacy > Playback. If you've noticed that this happens when using Smart Shuffle for your playlist, I'd suggest disabling it entirely as well. The option is again found under Settings and privacy > Playback > Include Smart Shuffle in play modes. As for this occurring even mid-playlist, perhaps the app has been backgrounded for too long and the operating system needs to reclaim resources, so it suspends it. What you can experiment with here is switching between having Background App Refresh enabled and disabled to see if that does the trick. Hope this helps! Cheers 🤘
records.author: Yordan

records.text: AI "verified artists" with "About" never saying it's AI Recently had Lyra Vesperi in my Discover Weekly, which I immediately knew was AI from the art. But how much of the music Spotify is pushing is AI and harder to catch? Why do they allow it and not make it it's own genre or at least tag it with a 'Generative AI' flag? I don't like the truth that Spotify is sneaking AI 'music' into it's recommendations. Time spent listening to the slop is listening time where Spotify doesn't have to pay a real artist. How can I trust any 'artist' on Spotify now? Am I forced to google every artist I hear to make sure Spotify isn't trying to trick me? For all that's holy, Please remove all AI content, or give listeners the option to 'opt-in' to AI music. Nobody wants to listen to AI music. It's not art, and it's not music.

records.text: Re: Spotfy keeps recommending me random songs even though I turned automatic suggestions OFF Hey @Meneer , welcome to the Spotify Community! 🎵 Do you happen to be using the free version of Spotify? In that case, there are a few things that you can try: If the song recommendation has an 'X' button, you can click that. This will make sure that the song is not played again in that particular playlist. Spotify learns from your listening habits, so the recommendations should get better over time in general. To block any specific artist, you can open their profile and click the 3 dots menu. Click don't play this artist to skip them in recommendations as well. I am linking some related articles with more details that you can check out: Hide and Unhide Songs | Your Taste Profile | Understanding recommendations on Spotify I hope that helps. Let me know if you need anything else. 🙂
records.author: Amay

records.text: Re: [All Platforms] Blocking podcasts shows Please share instructions or add a feature to Spotify on how to block podcasts. I paid for the app to avoid videos and not have recommendations I'm not interested in forced. The excessive video and podcasts recommendations (in the typical YouTube clickbait format -- "3 ways you can build wealth you wouldn't believe" if a foolish thumbnail) are really degrading the Spotify experience; to the point of probable cancellation for a 10+ years user. It doesn't make sense to use it if it doesn't let me do that. If I wanted a video or a podcast, I could use YouTube Premium. It's annoying for adult users, and it's a deal breaker for children's usage. It's really degrading the experience and I could not find instructions on how to block, not even specific podcast creators like we can with artists; much less not get any recommendations at all.

records.text: Improve Song Radio Recommendations to Prioritize Sound-Based Discovery I’ve noticed a clear difference in the quality of recommendations between “Go to Song Radio” and simply playing a song directly from search (with shuffle turned off). When using Song Radio, the suggestions often feel repetitive - frequently looping in artists I already listen to or leaning heavily toward more popular, algorithm-driven choices. In contrast, when I search for a specific track and let Spotify continue playing naturally, the following songs are often more diverse, unique, and introduce smaller or lesser-known artists. It feels like the search-based playback is more focused on the actual sound and characteristics of the song, rather than broader listening habits or popularity signals. It would be great to see Song Radio improved to match this experience - prioritising sound-based discovery and surfacing more varied and emerging artists, rather than repeating familiar or mainstream recommendations.
records.author: Cliffin

records.text: Having the option to create multiple profiles under one cost Please consider adding the ability to create multiple user profiles under a single, standard Spotify account, similar to how Netflix, Disney+, or Prime Video work. Why this would be useful: Convenience: It allows family members or couples to keep their music libraries, daily mixes, and wrapped statistics completely separate without the need to manage entirely different email logins. Smart Device Sharing: Many households share a single tablet or smart speaker. Swapping profiles quickly on a shared device would stop one person's listening habits from ruining the other person's algorithms and recommendations. Better Value: It fills the gap for users who want to share a plan but don't need the full setup or separate email account requirements of a Duo or Family plan. Please vote for this if you want an easier way to share Spotify at home : )
records.author: Susan256

records.text: Give people the ability to shut off idiotic "audiobooks" recommendations Spotify's "audiobook" recommendations are awful, terrible, embarrassing, extremely dumb, and utterly useless. I never listen to audio books with Spotify, I never have and I never will. I have absolutely no interest in audio books. I do not want Spotify's audio book recommendations. I want to completely turn them off, I want them to disappear forever, I want to have a switch in my app settings that says "Turn off audiobook recommendations" so that this totally useless garbage will never clutter my home screen with its seemingly endlessly idiotic suggestions about conspiracy theories, orgasms, cooking, and the Third Reich. This algorithm is utterly braindead, totally fried, and more desperate to get my attention than an all-caps clickbait article. Give your users the option to shovel this garbage out of their home feeds.

records.text: Please Don’t Include Audiobook Chapters in Music Playlists Dear Spotify Team, I use Spotify every day and truly appreciate the personalized playlists such as “On Repeat,” “Your Artist Mixes,” and the “Daily Mixes.” These features are a big part of what makes Spotify enjoyable for me. However, I’ve noticed that these automatically generated playlists often include individual chapters from audiobooks I listen to occasionally. In music‑focused playlists, this doesn’t make much sense: audiobook chapters interrupt the musical flow and don’t fit the purpose or structure of these playlists. My request would be to avoid mixing audiobook content into music playlists. It would be wonderful if Spotify could separate music and audiobooks more clearly within personalized recommendations. Thank you for your attention and for the great work you’re already doing. Kind regards Peter ( )
records.author: rocketman99

metadata.title: Please Don’t Include Audiobook Chapters in Music Playlists
metadata.search_snippet: Dear Spotify Team, I use Spotify every day and truly appreciate the personalized playlists such as “On Repeat,” “Your Artist Mixes,” and the “Daily Mixes.” These features are a big part of what makes Spotify enjoyable for me. However, I’ve noticed that these automatically generated playlists often include individual chapters from audiobooks I listen to occasionally. In music‑focused playlists, this doesn’t make much sense: audiobook chapters interrupt the musical flow and don’t fit the purpose or structure of these playlists. My request would be to avoid mixing audiobook content into music playlists. It would be wonderful if Spotify could separate music and audiobooks more clearly within personalized recommendations. Thank you for your attention and for the great work you’re already doing. Kind regards Peter ( )

records.text: Re: blatant advertisement on Home page of desktop Spotify Hey @pavowren , Thanks for reaching out! Spotify doesn’t play or include ads that interrupt the music listening experience of Premium subscribers. However, you might still see sponsored content in other areas of the app. This might include personalized recommendations based on your listening history, or you might get a banner letting you know you're also eligible for other Premium subscriptions, such as e.g. Premium Duo or Family. Nevertheless, these are just recommendations and will not interrupt your ad-free music listening time 🙂 As for the 'Not Interested' button seemingly not doing anything, we do thank you for bringing this to our attention. In case there's anything else in the meantime, don't hesitate to reach back to us. We're just a post away 🙌🏼

records.text: Listening to ambient music ruined my recommends Plan Free/Premium Country Device (iPhone 8, Samsung Galaxy 9, Macbook Pro late 2016) Operating System (iOS 10, Android Oreo, Windows 10,etc.) My Question or Issue I didn’t like a single ambient track or save a playlist, just listened to some on my echo every once in a while. Others clearly have had the same issues for years and mods respond with empty platitudes and options that don’t work. Now everything that shows up on my home page, discover weekly, etc is ambient. The algorithm obviously doesn’t work and I’m sick of dealing with this. It’s not worth shelling out $13 a month if the app doesn’t work as intended. I’m going to Amazon Music.

records.text: "Refresh" button to instantly update Daylist and Daily Mixes I propose adding a "Refresh" or "Regenerate" button to personalized playlists like the Daylist and Daily Mixes. This button would allow users to instantly update the entire tracklist with new recommendations if the current selection doesn't match their mood or liking. Currently, if a Daylist or Daily Mix doesn't look appealing, i have to wait hours (for Daylists) or an entire day (for Daily Mixes) for a dynamic update.Additionally, these algorithmic playlists tend to loop and repeat the exact same songs frequently, instead of diving into Spotify's vast catalog. A manual refresh button would give users immediate control to break these repetition loops, discover new music actively, and enjoy a truly personalized, intentional listening experience without artificial waiting times.
records.author: Blandem

A manual refresh button would give users immediate control to break these repetition loops, discover new music actively, and enjoy a truly personalized, intentional listening experience without artificial waiting times.

records.text: Disable All AI Features In Spotify I want the ability to turn off all AI features from my Spotify settings. While I'm sure some customers find value in these features (or at the very least are willing to swallow them) they are a waste of space for me, and make the platform less enjoyable for me. I don't want an AI D.J. (Hate.) I don't want to ask an AI for recommendations. (Hate.) I don't want an AI to generate a playlist for me. (Hate.) Again, I don't expect to Spotify to get rid of these feature entirely. But the the very presence of this stuff on the home screen drags down the experience for me. We should be able to toggle this stuff out.
records.author: anj222

records.text: Pin favorites to the home screen tab On the home screen tab of the app, there is already a grid of 8 playlists that you've recently played. Which is helpful sometimes, but then they constantly get replaced by other playlists. So i wish they were recommendations based on the time of day or based on recently played or the type of music you've recently been listening to or your birthday. And then I wish we could also just pin a song, playlist, album, podcast if we would rather have that take up a space, rather than the suggestions. Right now the 8 spots don't seem to have any reason why they are there except that I recently tapped on that playlist or album.

records.text: Re: Add "remove video content" option to Spotify Family This is what support told me today: After checking, I can confirm that for the managed accounts can only access music on Spotify; no podcasts, Radio or audiobook content included. Here's the Young listener's app features * Only have access to music (no podcasts, radio or audiobook content/recommendations) * Have their own Library and listening experience/history * Can listen offline * Can use Spotify Connect in speakers only (excluding 'Cast Touch'). No other device types are allowed. * Can't make purchases or see countdown pages * Can't join collaborative and interactive/social features In your case, you can revert your daughter's account to regular, then restricted the Explicit content. Stephen C. at 09:23 AM, Jan 8

records.text: Re: Discovery weekly hasn't worked for months Thanks @Maria for answering. Using a different connection does not help, I've tried this at home with pc (ethernet or wifi), with phone (4-5g), with or without VPN. A new week arrived, again the recommendations are the most radio-friendly songs with Finnish lyrics imaginable. Genre buttons are "Disco, Jazz, Stoner rock, Electro, Liquid Funk" which actually match what I'd expect genre-wise based on my recent listening history, but the buttons don't do anything on pc (a loader is looping), on phone when I press a genre button I can quickly see "This playlist is empty. Go back home..." text and it goes back to the playlist with only Finnish lyrics.

records.text: Re: Daylist seems to be repeating itself I also want to share my disappointment with the daylist - it started out great when it launched and help me discover new music, but for months now 90% of the songs are the same after every refresh. I excluded it from my taste profile, because I suspected it is causing a feedback loop where it reinforces showing the same songs over and over again but it has not helped much. Obviously I happen to listen some songs or bands more frequently or I get obsessed with some song for some time, but the daylist recommendations are just absurd. records.text: Genre/artist filters for AI DJ Dear Spotify Executive Leadership and Board of Directors, The AI DJ is a very nice feature. Having been on the platform for years now as a paying subscriber, I appreciate the rotation of my listening preferences. However, I do not appreciate genres creeping into DJ recommendations. There are certain genres or artists I will never listen to. As there is an option to checkmark a good song and add it to a playlist, I would like an option to select "Never" for a particular artist. The algorithm is placing literal garbage in my list. If you want to retain me as a paid subscriber, please resolve this.

records.text: Create a "Not Interested" button for podcasts you recommend Dear Spotify, Every day I open my page and find (the same three) political podcasts being suggested to me. I do not follow these podcasts and have no interest in them. Spotify is a place for me to get away from the noise of politics. A simple fix would be to create a "Not interested" option for podcasts like you do for music suggestions. Even better, let us block podcasts that we have no interest in listening to. The biggest issues are the thumbnail photos: I do not want to see certain talking heads nor politicians on my home page. Please... and thank you.

records.text: Add a “Super Like” to Improve Recommendations Description: The current “Like” button treats all positive feedback the same. But not all likes are equal — some songs are casual approvals, others are core favorites. I’d like to see a “Super Like” option that signals a stronger preference. For example, a Super Liked song could: Heavily influence Daily Mixes and AI DJ sessions. Increase weighting for similar artists/genres. Anchor radio stations and autoplay sessions. Be prioritized in future recommendations. This would: Improve recommendation quality by increasing signal strength. Help users shape their taste profile more precisely. Reduce the need to repeatedly skip or manually curate to influence the algorithm.
records.author: dicen

records.text: More Variety in Spotify Radio & Generated Playlists I find it really frustrating that so many Spotify-generated playlists and radios based on my music taste basically contain the exact same songs over and over again. The whole reason I choose a radio or a generated playlist is because I want to discover something different when I’m tired of my own music. But instead, it often feels like I’m just listening to the same playlist in different versions. Even across completely different playlists, many of the same songs keep repeating. I’d love more variety, deeper discovery, and less repetition in Spotify’s recommendations.

There is no auto-play setting in the Apple TV app. I don't know if there used to be or not, but it definitely did NOT auto-play after albums until recently (late fall?). I don't know who on earth would want this feature to be on by default, much less with no way to turn it off. Otherwise, I love you.

records.text: Re: LABEL AI MUSIC I recently got duped buy an AI artist called Orion7 with a blues cover of an Eminem song. Spotify included the song in my release radar and initially I thought it was a great idea but immediately soured when I couldn't find any information about the artist. Found a reddit thread complaining about the same artist and someone posted a link to YouTube that showed the song was actually AI generated. I wholeheartedly agree. Ai music needs to be labeled and we need to have the ability to block it from our recommendations.

records.text: Re: DJ Recommending the same set of songs I was able to fix the AI DJ's recommendations so that it wouldn't keep suggesting the same songs I'd liked before. Previously, I had created a playlist that completely duplicated the “Liked Songs” playlist, and that was causing the recommendations to go haywire. I went to the settings for that playlist and selected “ Exclude from your taste profile ” After that, I added a couple of albums by other users that I found on playlost.fm, and my recommendations were restored; now it suggests new songs much more often.

records.text: Allow shows/episodes appearing in your recommended feed to be toggled. I use Spotify to listen to only music, and I don't feel comfortable to sift through YouTube videos to get to music, just as I don’t want to listen to music videos on YouTube. I believe Spotify has a really good thing going with its music options, and I fit into Spotify’s main target audience of people who listen to music . I also know that most people do not get Spotify to watch Call her Daddy or Crime Junkie.

records.text: Re: Allow users to block certain artist Hey @Joseph1234 , thanks for reaching out to the Community! On Android and iOS, you can remove content recommendations from the "Music" and " Podcasts & Shows" feeds of the Home screen via the Not Interested button 👎 . You can find more info about it in the Status update here . You can also block specific artists you don't want the app playing by heading to the artist page you'd like to block > tap on the 3 dots > select Do not play this artist . I hope this clears things up!
records.author: Billy-J

records.text: Dislike Button podcast Dear Spotify Team, The same political podcasts keep appearing on my home page, and start automatically even though I don’t follow them and skip them immediately. The algorithm feels repetitive and does not seem to learn from my behavior. Other platforms like YouTube offer a clear “Not interested” option to control recommendations. Spotify should provide a similar solution. I am specifically asking for a clear “Dislike” button for podcasts — just like the one available for music — so unwanted content stops being suggested. I would appreciate a concrete solution.
records.author: Luu4

records.text: Re: Spotify sucks I wish that Spotify would allow me to modify the Start Page (or Home Screen). It is really frustrating and the reason that I've stopped using Spotify for podcasts all together. I use Apple now and I love it.

records.text: Inappropriate podcast recommendations Hi, we have an account that my whole family uses on our family computer. Unfortunately, month or two ago we started seeing podcast recommendations that are showing up on the home screen that are very inappropriate for my family - shows containing violence and sexy or semi-nude content. I need to find a way to disable this - I haven't found a button or setting where I can turn these off. If we can't remove them, we'll have to switch to another platform, unfortunately.

I'd like for it to be possible for songs on the local files playlist to influence recommendations, for instance, being added to on repeat playlist or being played by AI DJ. This would be helpful for instance if you've created AI songs, and added them to your playlist.

records.text: Re: How can I remove the “playing recommended songs for you” feature To stop “playing recommended songs for you,” open your music app’s settings and disable Autoplay, Smart Shuffle, or Recommendations, if available. If no option exists, turn off repeat/autoplay after your queue ends or create a manual playlist to avoid automatic suggestions.
records.author: mubashiriqbal

metadata.title: Re: How can I remove the “playing recommended songs for you” feature

If no option exists, turn off repeat/autoplay after your queue ends or create a manual playlist to avoid automatic suggestions.

records.text: Re: Remove an entire genre from my taste profile That's a great idea. We have to have control over our taste. I don't care if Spotify thinks I'll like a song; if I exclude a genre, it means I don't want any recommendations from it at all, and that should be respected.

records.text: Limited control over listening history Users cannot edit or clean their listening history. A single accidental listen can affect recommendations for a long time. Adding options like removing a track from the taste profile would help keep recommendations accurate.

records.text: #MusicMonday Review - May 2026 #MusicMonday is the hashtag I've been using for quite a while to share music recommendations from up-and-coming artists. Always fresh, and always different, trying to look for trends before they become one. You can check April's review for more music. It doesn't matter if you want to raise attention to government, environmental, or personal issues, music is the perfect way to express yourself, whatever genre you may prefer. Give them a listen, with a word from the artists themselves. 🎧 June Holland – Mouldy Orange I spent so much time just Sat on my own They said I disrupted the rest of the kids So they left me alone But I was a fire until you put out my flames Labelled me naughty and stupid When deep down I knew I had brains Can't help but be mad that school Could've been easier If only I'd have had an educated teacher We begin our trip in South Manchester, England for a soulful Indie Folk track about a girl who has being punished for being herself, but with time understood she's perfect just as she is: "It’s a personal experience, I was always labelled the naughty kid in primary school I got in trouble a lot but no one ever suggested it could be ADHD, I’m not sure it was really explored in my school especially in girls… When I reached my twenties I finally got diagnosed with ADHD and although it didn’t come as a surprise it did bring up a lot of emotions including anger and grief for the young misunderstood girl I was growing up. So this song was kind of like therapy for me. Getting out all the emotions I felt and reflecting on my life. But I also wanted to create this song and share my experience because I know there will be many others like me out there and I want to make them feel seen and know that they aren’t alone. The response has been amazing so far it’s been wonderful to see people connecting with the track. 🥹🧡 😉 " spotify:track:4KepSGT6osc3dZwyCldBAC:small Shines – Late for Days Since you went away it's hard to fall asleep your t-shirt smells the same you left it here with me Let's travel east to Grimsby, England, for an Indie Rock track about those really long conversations that seem to go on for hours in the middle of the night : "I think it's one of them open ended songs that sort of content different meanings. Whether it be sort of the breakup of a relationship, or a friendship or just sort of whatever's left after that's happened. But also it came together really quick for us. Just got sort of jamming over a little riff and we're sort of working like that for all our stuff this year. It's not overthinking stuff, not polishing stuff, and just keeping it nice and fresh, and just getting it out there. I'd say the style of this song is more shoegaze than what we've done prior, but there's a lot of elements in our first album that's shoegaze. It's probably inspired by great bands like Beach Fossils , Wild Nothing , you know, The Drums . I like all them type of bands. They've all got a good groove." spotify:track:2s5fhMX6PvJNh3Sx2heiMb:small Fine Night Elements – All We Know Valleys low to mountains high Rivers running from the beginning of time Sun shines through to the forest floor Last flower raises its head But only once more Leave us alone I hear them cry Don't speak your language but why should we die? Do you ever think that this world does not revolve around you? Up north in Gateshead, England, we get an Alt Rock track blended with some Indie vibes about human's inherent appetite to destroy what's in our path, and take everything we can. It's all we know : "Initially the music was laid down just through jamming, but the experience that inspired the lyrics are just the constant information about global warming that we’re consistently ignoring. The track discusses the path we’re on as a species and how we need to change." spotify:track:2vZ5Hd8r42VoqrmtMz61kP:small Draken – It Serves You Right You screwed up, you crossed the line So don’t act so surprised, my friend We always knew it was a matter of time Before you’d be knee deep in sh*t again Slowly dying Thought you were death defying They got you dead to rights And truth be told… it serves you right We fly now to Oslo, Norway for a Metal track that tells what happens when arrogance makes you blind, while leaving your decency behind. Bassist Hallward explains: "It's about power hungry politicians, and corruption. I wrote the lyrics when there was a series of political scandals in Norway; politicians taking advantage of the system, misuse of public funds, etc." spotify:track:3ZqpEOcb4Xq0Yo6T2FKqpV:small dimblue – One Hundred Thoughts I'm not defined by my sense of revenge I drop my innocence and I don't want it back Thoughts travel so fast that I cannot bend My heart is broken Some things you cannot mend Compelling answers don't always let you rest Satisfaction isn't always the best Some say that truth lies in the middle ground But our subconscious wish is still to be found We now travel to Bari, Italy for an Indie Folk track with some Lo-Fi details about taking time to reflect on something you have experienced, and trying to come up with an adequate response: "Overthinking is exactly what gave life to this track. All those thoughts that flow incessantly, often leaving us exhausted. I believe the song was born from the desire to bring some order, while at the same time immersing myself in the creative flow. I think the filter was the instrument itself—the acoustic guitar—which somehow created the sense of order I was searching for in that creative moment. The electric guitar lines, on the other hand, allowed me to push into the background all those thoughts that tend to overlap." spotify:track:60fe6PmHwQuwrc9mcbaG2s:small Ismena – How Are You? You came into my life and you said you were mine And started a fire in my mind I thought I knew who you were but you're a liar And now I don't trust a single word Maybe one day it will turn out right Who do you think you're standing beside? Finally I am enlightened I see you for who you are We end out travels in Utrecht, NL. for a d reamy, melancholic Indie Folk track about being hurt to the point of making sure you won't cross paths with that person ever again: "T his was a very personal experience that affected me deeply. It's about trusting someone and gradually finding out that this person is not who they say they are. It can be hard to grasp when someone turns out to be completely different than what you always thought they were. It's also about finally accepting their true nature and not letting them affect you anymore. " spotify:track:0ubUu2y2iaCqUFyLZsdF65:small Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!
records.author: osornios

records.text: #MusicMonday Review - March 2026 #MusicMonday is the hashtag I've been using for quite a while to share music recommendations from up-and-coming artists. Always fresh, and always different, trying to look for trends before they become one. You can check February's Part 2 review for more music. In this issue, let's explore the inner works of the mind, and decision making. What makes us anxious? What gives us the sense of freedom? Give them a listen, with a word from the artists themselves. 🎧 Fayette – I Should Never satisfied Never enough time Only perfect moves Now I crave the mess that makes me mine Always out of rhyme Never being true Holding on to flawless lines Now I crave the mess that makes me mine We could, we should, and we will begin our journey in Nijmegen-Arnhem, Netherlands for a Downtempo Indie Pop track about the decisions we make, and the process we go through, for better or for worse: " The song came from noticing how often my inner voice says ‘I should’ instead of ‘I want.’ It wasn’t one single person or moment, but more a pattern I saw in myself — constantly trying to meet invisible standards and over-correcting every little flaw. The track grew out of that tension between perfectionism and authenticity, and the realization that I was performing control instead of allowing myself to be human. Writing it felt like loosening that grip a little. But it’s a process of course. 😉 " spotify:track:6FF3hIQaLWDHGwVBGtyMb0:small Sophia Galaté – Please Don't Talk To Me you know you haven't seen me around I've been spending all my days inside I'm just tryna get my mind right even if it takes me such a long time and I'm not gonna let nobody bother me I really think I've lost my mind I'm not in the space to even entertain anything until I've really changed I'm getting my life together babe From hesitation, we now go to isolation in Los Angeles, CA with a soulful R&B jam about living better by healing in peace on your own: "So in the summer of 2023 I was, I wouldn't say depressed, but I was just going through a very low point in my life, and a family friend of mine called me to catch up on the phone, and she was like so bubbly on the other line. Like 'hey girl, how are you? How's your summer? Are you talking to anyone? Are you going out?' To which I replied, 'No, I'm literally just inside going to therapy, and if anyone tries to talk to me during this time, I'm just like, please don't talk to me'. And she was like 'Ha, that should be a song'. Then I conveniently just received a bunch of beats from this producer overseas, and one of them was the beat to Please Don't Talk To Me . It just fit perfectly. I just basically wrote all the lyrics kind of over the course of the month, and it was just like it was exactly what I was going through at the time, but what's so cool about the song is that the song doesn't sound depressing at all. It's very much like a celebration of your alone time and being excited to get your life together and move into the next phase." spotify:track:7aH4Gbn3k600txlFtTV7Az:small Filthy Red – The Afters So he called her once Called her twice Smoking cheap cigs from a dodgy local And spouting bollocks through the night He can’t get what he wants So now he’s gagging for a fight But you can’t get close to anyone When you’re a snakeskin parasite Let's fly now to Kingston upon Hull, England for an Indie Rock track about waking up, and asking yourself, just for a moment, how things got derailed the night before: "You know, usually I’ll come up with a line or melodic idea that sticks with me when I write. I came up with the first verse to this one pretty quickly and it kind of wrote itself from there. Once I had that verse nailed I started to really delve deep and it turned into this vibe about how we can get so stuck in the rat race, working a 9-5 that when it gets to the weekend we lose ourselves in the chaos of letting go sometimes and don’t always know when to stop. I know a few people where that’s definitely the case. So I guess that’s what it ended up being about really. Slogging it out through the week and then when the free time hits, letting loose, but maybe going a little too far sometimes." spotify:track:0xjaxlBRjmWNO5hAgL9Prx:small ABFAB – Wide Open Spaces The sun sinks low behind the hills The air is quiet, the world stands still Empty roads stretch for miles unseen A painted sky in shades of tangerine Tires hum like a lonesome tune Under the glow of a silver moon A sign says nowhere just up ahead But I'm alive in the life I've led We now travel far to Cape Town, South Africa for a catchy Pop song about the freedom of driving by yourself under the blinking stars of the night, leaving everything behind, and looking forward to what tomorrow may bring: "The song is about me and my drummer on the roads to our gigs. We wrote it for all the long drives we have had and the opportunities we have experienced. It’s all self experiences with all my writing" spotify:track:3WKsyeKrAkC5pFJWqCa8mc:small Portrait Generation – Grassroots Being as optimistic as I can Not my fault it’s just the way that things are ran It seems that triumph's written in their fate Well it’s easy when it’s handed on a plate For us the struggle's real Their wealth will be concealed The labour grows the fruit Not for the Grassroots We finish our trip in Southend-on-Sea, England, for an Indie Rock track with no lack of drive or passion, but knows that breaking through may depend on your class: " So the story behind Grassroots really came from the sad reality that grassroots bands are really struggling in the music industry at the moment and that there’s an illusion of equal opportunity when it’s just not the case! Bands with financial backing from either a label or parents (for example) are the ones that succeed, and working class musicians continue to struggle working full time jobs alongside the music just trying to stay afloat. " spotify:track:3GLVWuFPKPWHAmMynVVu9X:small Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!
records.author: osornios

metadata.title: #MusicMonday Review - March 2026
metadata.search_snippet: #MusicMonday is the hashtag I've been using for quite a while to share music recommendations from up-and-coming artists. Always fresh, and always different, trying to look for trends before they become one. You can check February's Part 2 review for more music. In this issue, let's explore the inner works of the mind, and decision making. What makes us anxious? What gives us the sense of freedom? Give them a listen, with a word from the artists themselves. 🎧 Fayette – I Should Never satisfied Never enough time Only perfect moves Now I crave the mess that makes me mine Always out of rhyme Never being true Holding on to flawless lines Now I crave the mess that makes me mine We could, we should, and we will begin our journey in Nijmegen-Arnhem, Netherlands for a Downtempo Indie Pop track about the decisions we make, and the process we go through, for better or for worse: " The song came from noticing how often my inner voice says ‘I should’ instead of ‘I want.’ It wasn’t one single person or moment, but more a pattern I saw in myself — constantly trying to meet invisible standards and over-correcting every little flaw. The track grew out of that tension between perfectionism and authenticity, and the realization that I was performing control instead of allowing myself to be human. Writing it felt like loosening that grip a little. But it’s a process of course. 😉 " spotify:track:6FF3hIQaLWDHGwVBGtyMb0:small Sophia Galaté – Please Don't Talk To Me you know you haven't seen me around I've been spending all my days inside I'm just tryna get my mind right even if it takes me such a long time and I'm not gonna let nobody bother me I really think I've lost my mind I'm not in the space to even entertain anything until I've really changed I'm getting my life together babe From hesitation, we now go to isolation in Los Angeles, CA with a soulful R&B jam about living better by healing in peace on your own: "So in the summer of 2023 I was, I wouldn't say depressed, but I was just going through a very low point in my life, and a family friend of mine called me to catch up on the phone, and she was like so bubbly on the other line. Like 'hey girl, how are you? How's your summer? Are you talking to anyone? Are you going out?' To which I replied, 'No, I'm literally just inside going to therapy, and if anyone tries to talk to me during this time, I'm just like, please don't talk to me'. And she was like 'Ha, that should be a song'. Then I conveniently just received a bunch of beats from this producer overseas, and one of them was the beat to Please Don't Talk To Me . It just fit perfectly. I just basically wrote all the lyrics kind of over the course of the month, and it was just like it was exactly what I was going through at the time, but what's so cool about the song is that the song doesn't sound depressing at all. It's very much like a celebration of your alone time and being excited to get your life together and move into the next phase." spotify:track:7aH4Gbn3k600txlFtTV7Az:small Filthy Red – The Afters So he called her once Called her twice Smoking cheap cigs from a dodgy local And spouting bollocks through the night He can’t get what he wants So now he’s gagging for a fight But you can’t get close to anyone When you’re a snakeskin parasite Let's fly now to Kingston upon Hull, England for an Indie Rock track about waking up, and asking yourself, just for a moment, how things got derailed the night before: "You know, usually I’ll come up with a line or melodic idea that sticks with me when I write. I came up with the first verse to this one pretty quickly and it kind of wrote itself from there. Once I had that verse nailed I started to really delve deep and it turned into this vibe about how we can get so stuck in the rat race, working a 9-5 that when it gets to the weekend we lose ourselves in the chaos of letting go sometimes and don’t always know when to stop. I know a few people where that’s definitely the case. So I guess that’s what it ended up being about really. Slogging it out through the week and then when the free time hits, letting loose, but maybe going a little too far sometimes." spotify:track:0xjaxlBRjmWNO5hAgL9Prx:small ABFAB – Wide Open Spaces The sun sinks low behind the hills The air is quiet, the world stands still Empty roads stretch for miles unseen A painted sky in shades of tangerine Tires hum like a lonesome tune Under the glow of a silver moon A sign says nowhere just up ahead But I'm alive in the life I've led We now travel far to Cape Town, South Africa for a catchy Pop song about the freedom of driving by yourself under the blinking stars of the night, leaving everything behind, and looking forward to what tomorrow may bring: "The song is about me and my drummer on the roads to our gigs. We wrote it for all the long drives we have had and the opportunities we have experienced. It’s all self experiences with all my writing" spotify:track:3WKsyeKrAkC5pFJWqCa8mc:small Portrait Generation – Grassroots Being as optimistic as I can Not my fault it’s just the way that things are ran It seems that triumph's written in their fate Well it’s easy when it’s handed on a plate For us the struggle's real Their wealth will be concealed The labour grows the fruit Not for the Grassroots We finish our trip in Southend-on-Sea, England, for an Indie Rock track with no lack of drive or passion, but knows that breaking through may depend on your class: " So the story behind Grassroots really came from the sad reality that grassroots bands are really struggling in the music industry at the moment and that there’s an illusion of equal opportunity when it’s just not the case! Bands with financial backing from either a label or parents (for example) are the ones that succeed, and working class musicians continue to struggle working full time jobs alongside the music just trying to stay afloat. " spotify:track:3GLVWuFPKPWHAmMynVVu9X:small Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!

records.text: #MusicMonday Review - October 2025 Part 2 #MusicMonday is the hashtag I've been using for quite a while to share music recommendations from up-and-coming artists. Always fresh, and always different, trying to look for trends before they become one. You can check October's Part 1 review for more music. We continue our music listening journey with five unique genres, with different takes in what reality means nowadays, and, as usual, do not disappoint. Give them a listen, with a word from the artists themselves. 🎧 NEVEN – 12 Bars I love the twelve-bar blues alright, but the twelve-bar windows ain't for me, I stood my ground you locked me up, drug lords and killers walking free, I got twelve bars on my window baby, I sing twelve-bar blues tonight Our journey lands us in Liverpool, England, for a Blues Rock track about having strong convictions, and putting up a fight for them when necessary: "It's a mix of personal experience and just something that we see everyday as well really - people getting penalized and locked up for speaking up their mind while a lot of big criminals get away with what they do. Basically it's criticism of corrupt justice systems globally. But of course it's also wordplay on 12 bars, which in this context represents the bars on a prison-cell window as well as 12 bars in the traditional blues structure." spotify:track:5eN21PrnxXyygeu4CjZTaQ:small Cranc. – Something Don't Feel Right I'm tired off all these pound shop lad bands going on about how working class they are My dad was born in a council estate, my mum came here on a boat but you don't hear me going on about it... wait? "All my life I felt like something was wrong in this land," she said But there ain't not secret plot or dark forces trying to implant microchips in your bloodstream The rot... the rot! It's inside us; it's us We travel now to Barcelona, Spain for a Post Punk track about being uneasy, without being able to explain or ignore why. All you can see is the emptiness in people's eyes: "The track grew out of feelings of otherness. The seemingly progressive protagonist purports to loathe the mining of working class experience for artistic respect, while arguably doing the same thing himself. Conspiratorial apocalyptic movements instinctively feel how wrong the world is, but their alienation leads them astray. Everything is not quite as it seems. The song was written in a single night in a kind of inchoate fury against where we are right now." spotify:track:6xXccrqvVevWLnc6nM9Gfq:small Moss Side Sauna Club – MY WEIRD APPETITE Up on the English coastline, Up where the air is clearer, I'm getting weird effing looks, From everyone I hold dear, Don't send me any postcards, Not least till I get better, Go tell the English coastguard, To readdress my letters, Let's travel back to Manchester, England for an ElectroClash song about the desire for the new, wanting all of everything, all the time. Is it a crime?: "the song’s a satire on how people see bisexuality - so it was a way of owning the caricature! NYC dance punk, Peaches & M.I.A were an influence." spotify:track:6V7E6yq3c9xHTSZ4H0Tls1:small RxGhost – Sponsored Content Even the dullest setting on that light’s too bright Some of us are trying to sleep Being around is not the same as being here Neither of us are in this room Top ten places you can go to find yourself Click the link to find out more Looking at pictures of the places we won’t go I’d much rather stay indoors We now to Kansas City, MO. for a shoegazey Danger Pop track about what social gatherings, and reality in general, have become: sitting tight, and doom scroll. The content on the screen is just more real: "There were times when we were all hanging out and everyone was on their device and it got me thinking about how it’s just kind of a strange new option that wasn’t even available ten years ago, how you never really have to be bored at all anymore and how you can be in a group of people but everyone is in their own world. I feel like boredom can be helpful, in that it can trigger creativity, and now that there’s the option to never be bored, it’s a struggle to get people to try hard things or invent ways to entertain themselves, it’s just so easy and comfortable to scroll. And then that kind of spiraled into thinking about how inauthentic most of what we see is, and how advertising is creeping its way deeper and deeper into the content, so that the lines are super blurred between a real endorsement of something by someone you follow and a paid ad." spotify:track:44oBUcv9gsvueUwvSjB4TM:small Suspire – Deepstate Dive Say about your day, The thought fell away, You stare, Deep into my mind's eye, I'll take a step if you say, Bye, You think that you went for the one you wanted, You thought that you would get the gift of time, You said I could solve the case you're solving, Guess lies hard to do while the courts adjourned We end up in Oxford, England for an Indie Rock track with a punky bass that takes you on a blindsided trip down to nowhere and back, where nothing is what it was before: " Deepstate Dive explores a moment, or an encounter, that pushes the subject to a deeper level of consciousness. What follows is a spiral of reevaluating past choices that were made, and struggling to come to terms with how a newfound world view, doesn’t fit with the path of life they’ve walked down to date. The track treads a tightrope of ominous anthemia, reminiscent of Skinty Fia-era Fontaines DC." spotify:track:3c8GS83zYBpl77HMWzunQi:small Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!
records.author: osornios

records.text: #MusicMonday Review - January 2026 Part 1 #MusicMonday is the hashtag I've been using for quite a while to share music recommendations from up-and-coming artists. Always fresh, and always different, trying to look for trends before they become one. You can check December's review for more music. In this issue, the music is bold, deeply reflective, and unapologetically honest. Give them a listen, with a word from the artists themselves. 🎧 AVA RENN – None the Wiser This land I’ve walked is cold and barren No time to talk, no I’m not sharing anymore... Black and blue, the sirens blaring Vow of silence quiets the screaming in my core Find no peace beside this water So I wade out where I can’t breathe I’ll get no rest inside this slaughterhouse I’ve built... These walls have robbed me of my sleep Hello, from Marfa, TX. We begin in full force with a gritty Rock track about the pieces that are missing, and the colors not seen by the numbness we all occasionally feel: "Thematically, the song is about a desperate try to break cycles that harm you. As humans the knowledge of what should be done sometimes gets lost in excuses or turmoil or bouts of depression. This song is an emblem of introspection to try and get out of that deep end." spotify:track:7kpD4QFkmmyIcWTUw8ZfSl:small Vik Faded – Sansa Stark tus stories me dan celos cada vez que tú sales roneando por los bares que cualquiera te vale vivo encaprichao’ de ti buscándote en mil lugares pensando que lo haces pa dejarme señales We now fly to Madrid, Spain, for a Latin Pop track about those physical, but toxic, relationships you can't get out of, just because it makes you feel truly alive: "I came up with the idea in the studio with Mena Labib. It took as one hour and a half completing the first demo, the inspiration was literally the two of us making noise!" spotify:track:3wG7i2SsymfWHslZkDcAmQ:small Project Overload – Late Night Dancing Our heads in the clouds While we run around No cares in the world This moment is ours Lost in a daydream I’m feeling hazy Wrapped in our fun We're going crazy From Coventry, England we get a Janglepop track about the joy of dancing, being surrounded with like-minded people. Get into the groove: "It’s our third single of the year and the final part of a three-song narrative. Silhouettes was starry-eyed romance, Wildfire was that new love breaking down, and Late Night Dancing is about moving on and dancing the blues away. It started as a rehearsal jam with some new effects pedals, then sprang to life taking inspiration from Nile Rodgers, cowbells, water-glass chimes, ‘50s B-movies and Blondie’s Heart of Glass. We played it live for Record Store Day the week it was written, with lyric sheets scattered on the stage. It came together pretty quickly, and though it doesn’t sound much like the rest of the new album, we knew it was a banger, so it had to be a single. I wrote the chords as a bit of fun but it seemed to work because we all really like this one." spotify:track:09Nu3wglBGgIHLMQiQFbRU:small Jared Popma – The Cosmic Joke Do you ever feel like you're not where you should be And everything comes a little too slowly And no one's saying you got so much time to figure it out? Maybe it's time now to figure it out So you get a job where you make all your money It's kind of sad but you laugh like it's funny When it feels like you're part of a big cosmic joke You work your whole life but you still wind up broke Let's travel now to Vancouver, BC, for a Folk Pop track about finding out who you really are, by looking for your place in the world: "I was in nearing the end of my degree, and struggling with the weight of beginning life in the 'real world'. Would I be able to make it in music? Is that what I even want? If not, what else am I going to do? It all felt overwhelming, but I also felt like my feelings didn’t matter since everyone has to go through similar things. It’s an acknowledgment that my struggles are nothing necessarily special, but still wishing for some sort of validation in my feelings." spotify:track:6skEfSyls9yXF6lUPB74D4:small Pinocchio is dead – The boy who never dies Paper heart, folded twice, Kept it burning under ice. Every truth I tried to say Turned to ash and blew away. Mirror eyes, wooden smile, I’ve been lying all the while. If you cut my strings tonight, Would I fall or learn to fly? Our trip ends in Rome, Italy, for a Synth Pop track about performing to others in automatic, without questioning why. Part exploration, part emotion, who are you performing for? "The track came from real life... moments, mistakes, and all the noise in between. The Boy Who Never Die is about looking back, cutting loose what holds you down, and finding peace in the wreckage. Not everyone’s meant for your story, and that’s fine. In my mind, it had to sound poetic, dark, and a little theatrical 😜 🤣 it’s about the fragility and the rebellion of someone who can’t help but stay true to who they are." spotify:track:6t0pEQSffKYxzPNh2Jwl7k:small #MusicMonday 2025 It's time to recap 2025 with this year's compilation. 76 titles from original artists covering all types of genres, represent the multiple trends independent artists were proud to establish. Have a listen: spotify:playlist:71fadol0hgsdSjLpM828HZ Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!
records.author: osornios

records.text: Playlist options for personal app development Hello Developer community, and Spotify. Longtime listener , first-time caller . Investor , also, if that matters. Plan Premium Country USA Device iPhone 17 Pro Max, Macbook Air 2019, iPad Pro (newest) Operating System iOS 26, MacOS Sonoma My Question or Issue I am a personal user aspiring to enhance music recommendations in the context of my life - what I'm doing, where I'm going, etc. I have built an app designed to be used by me and only me, but I'm having trouble getting anything playlist related to work (i.e. adding to one, getting track lists from existing, saving liked songs, etc). I receive a 403 forbidden error. From what I can tell, this is because I am in "development mode". Since it's a personal application, I don't qualify for "extended quota mode". I have an LLC I could use to request EQ mode but am wondering if it'll get approved, much less whether it's necessary. Is there a path for personal/developer use that unlocks read access to playlist tracks and basic library write operations? These are operations on my own data, for my own account, running on my own infrastructure. More Details ## Context I'm building a personal music curation tool — a single-user app that only I use. It tracks my listening history, builds a taste model from my own library, discovers new music from artists I follow, and creates curated sessions. It runs on Cloudflare Workers (serverless) and interacts solely with my own Spotify account. My account is Premium. My Spotify email is listed in the app's User Management. The app has both "Web API" and "Web Playback SDK" checked in the dashboard. ## What works These endpoints function correctly in Development Mode: | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | |----------|--------|---------| | `/v1/me` | GET | User profile | | `/v1/me/tracks` | GET | Read saved/liked songs | | `/v1/me/top/tracks` | GET | Top tracks (all time ranges) | | `/v1/me/top/artists` | GET | Top artists (all time ranges) | | `/v1/me/playlists` | GET | List user's playlists | | `/v1/me/following` | GET | Followed artists | | `/v1/me/player/currently-playing` | GET | Currently playing track | | `/v1/me/player/queue` | POST | Add track to queue | | `/v1/search` | GET | Search for tracks/artists/playlists | | `/v1/artists/{id}/albums` | GET | Artist discography | ## What is blocked (403 Forbidden) ### Playlist track access (READ) | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Impact | |----------|--------|---------|--------| | `/v1/playlists/{id}/tracks` | GET | Read tracks from any playlist | **Cannot read tracks from my own playlists.** This is the single biggest limitation. My app builds a taste model partly from seasonal playlists I've manually curated — I need to read the tracks in my own playlists to know what songs I've associated with each season. This is read-only access to my own data. | This also blocks reading Spotify editorial playlists (New Music Friday, Fresh Finds, etc.) for music discovery. ### Browse endpoints (READ) | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Impact | |----------|--------|---------|--------| | `/v1/browse/new-releases` | GET | New album/single releases | Cannot discover new releases. Workaround: using `/v1/search` with `year:2026` filter, but this is less comprehensive. | | `/v1/browse/featured-playlists` | GET | Spotify's featured playlists | Cannot browse editorial playlist selections. | ### Library write operations | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Impact | |----------|--------|---------|--------| | `/v1/me/tracks` | PUT | Save a track to Liked Songs | Cannot like/save tracks programmatically. My dashboard has a "like" button that can't function. | | `/v1/me/tracks` | DELETE | Remove from Liked Songs | Cannot unlike tracks. | | `/v1/playlists/{id}/tracks` | POST | Add tracks to a playlist | **Cannot populate playlists.** I can create empty playlists but cannot add tracks to them. This means my curated sessions can only output via direct playback or queue — I can't save a session as a playlist for later. | ### Playback (intermittent) | Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Impact | |----------|--------|---------|--------| | `/v1/me/player/play` | PUT | Start playback | Sometimes returns 403. May be device-dependent rather than a blanket restriction. Playback via queue (`POST /me/player/queue`) works reliably. | ## Summary of impact on my app 1. **Taste model is degraded.** I can't read my own playlist tracks, so my seasonal playlist analysis (a core feature) depends on tokens that intermittently gain/lose this permission. 2. **Music discovery is limited.** I can't scan new releases or editorial playlists. I work around this with search, but miss breadth. 3. **No playlist output.** Curated sessions can play immediately or queue, but I can't save them as playlists for later listening. 4. **No library management.** Can't like or unlike tracks from my app's interface. 5. **No editorial playlist scanning.** Can't read tracks from Spotify-owned playlists like New Music Friday, which would significantly improve discovery quality. ## My request I'm a single user building a personal tool. I'm not building a commercial product. I understand Extended Quota Mode requires 250K+ MAU and a registered business — that's clearly not applicable here. Is there a path for personal/developer use that unlocks read access to playlist tracks and basic library write operations? These are operations on my own data, for my own account, running on my own infrastructure. Specific asks, in priority order: 1. `GET /v1/playlists/{id}/tracks` — read my own playlists 2. `PUT /v1/me/tracks` — save tracks to my own library 3. `POST /v1/playlists/{id}/tracks` — add tracks to my own playlists 4. `GET /v1/browse/new-releases` — browse new releases
records.author: chrisbuice

metadata.title: Playlist options for personal app development
metadata.search_snippet: Hello Developer community, and Spotify. Longtime listener , first-time caller . Investor , also, if that matters. Plan Premium Country USA Device iPhone 17 Pro Max, Macbook Air 2019, iPad Pro (newest) Operating System iOS 26, MacOS Sonoma My Question or Issue I am a personal user aspiring to enhance music recommendations in the context of my life - what I'm doing, where I'm going, etc. I have built an app designed to be used by me and only me, but I'm having trouble getting anything playlist related to work (i.e. adding to one, getting track lists from existing, saving liked songs, etc). I receive a 403 forbidden error. From what I can tell, this is because I am in "development mode". Since it's a personal application, I don't qualify for "extended quota mode". I have an LLC I could use to request EQ mode but am wondering if it'll get approved, much less whether it's necessary. Is there a path for personal/developer use that unlocks read access to playlist tracks and basic library write operations? These are operations on my own data, for my own account, running on my own infrastructure. More Details ## Context I'm building a personal music curation tool — a single-user app that only I use.
