records.text: Feature idea: Talk radio + replace music breaks with my Spotify playlist Hi Spotify Product Team, I’d like to share a feature idea that I think many “talk radio” listeners would love. I often listen to live radio stations for the spoken segments (for example P4 Göteborg), but I don’t always enjoy the music the station plays between talk segments. A great Spotify feature would be a “Talk Radio Mode” for live radio streams: Let the user listen to a live radio station as usual for talk/news/interviews. When the station switches to music, Spotify automatically fades out the radio and plays tracks from a Spotify playlist the user selects. When the station returns to talk, Spotify switches back to the live radio stream. Ideally, the transition would be seamless (quick fade/crossfade), and the user could choose the playlist (or “DJ”/recommendations) for the music breaks. I’m not sure what technical constraints exist around detecting talk vs. music in a live stream, but if it’s feasible, I think it would be a really compelling way to combine live radio content with Spotify’s personalized music experience. Thanks for considering the idea, and I’d be happy to clarify the user experience if helpful. Best regards, Robert Wiesel
records.author: Wieselan

records.text: Add an Incognito Mode for Listening That Does Not Affect Taste Profile Spotify should add an Incognito Mode for listening. Sometimes users want to play music that does not reflect their actual taste. This can happen when we are playing music for friends, working with children, testing songs, listening to sleep sounds, checking out a viral track, or playing music for a specific event. Those listens should not reshape our recommendations, Discover Weekly, Daily Mixes, Spotify Wrapped, or overall taste profile. Spotify already gives users some control with Private Session and the option to exclude certain tracks or playlists from the taste profile. However, users still need a simpler session-based option. The feature could work like this: Turn on Incognito Mode Listen normally Spotify still plays music as usual Those streams do not affect the user’s taste profile Similar songs are not pushed into recommendations afterward The mode automatically turns off after a set time, like Private Session This would give users more control over personalization without removing personalization completely. It would also make Spotify feel more accurate because recommendations would reflect what users genuinely enjoy, not every temporary, social, or situational listening session. Please consider adding a true Incognito Mode for Spotify listening.
records.author: riccixrose

It immediately starts another recommended song, and like always, recommended songs have that ”X” or ”+” buttons. + to add to liked songs, but pressing the ”x” gives a popup where it says that it will hide this song from this playlist. WHAT PLAYLIST?? it also has another add-on option to not recommend this song for 30 days which is self explanatory. But what playlist are they talking about? And what is it hiding? If I go to the artist page, the song is still there, if I search for the song, it comes up. So it’s not hidden. what if I wanna undo this hidden thing, whatever that is, later on? perhaps this is a weird question but I would really like to know cause I wanna know how I influence my algorithms.

records.text: Re: Discovery weekly hasn't worked for months Hey there @Phatency , Thanks for reaching out on the Community! And kudos for including some details on what's happening from your end - we appreciate it. Just to confirm, do you notice any difference if you connect your device(s) to another internet connection? For example, if you're currently on WiFi, you can try mobile data to check if you're able to select a genre. If you're using any VPN, make sure to switch it off, as it could interfere with the service. As the Discover Weekly playlist is dynamic and reflects your latest listening habits, it might not always have enough content to go off to create the perfect mix for you. We'd recommend to keep listening to the music you love and giving us feedback (such as liking or hiding songs in the list), and with a bit of time, your Discover Weekly should update and better reflect your taste. Keep in mind that the more you listen to tracks outside of a Private Session , or by adding the tracks you love to Liked Songs and other playlists, the better our recommendations would be. Cheers!
records.author: Maria

records.text: Help the new ai DJ Hi Spotify team, I am a big fan of the new AI DJ feature, and I would love to suggest a few enhancements to make the experience even more personalized: 1) Contextual Interaction: I would love the ability to communicate directly with the AI DJ. It would be great to inform the DJ about the activities I am about to start (e.g., working out, studying, hosting a dinner). Allowing the AI to understand the context of my current situation would help it curate the perfect soundtrack for that specific moment. 2)Granular Feedback System: Currently, it is hard to steer the DJ’s selections. I would like to be able to tell the DJ exactly why I like or dislike a track. By providing specific feedback—such as "too loud," "too much bass," or "too many lyrics"—the AI could learn my preferences on a deeper level and refine its future recommendations to better suit my taste. I believe these additions would significantly deepen the connection between the user and the AI DJ. Thank you for your time and for the great work on this feature!
records.author: rtsrooia

records.text: Regional Focus: Allow users to select preferred locales/regions for algorithm recommendations Current Behaviour: Right now, Spotify's automated discovery algorithms (Discover Weekly, Release Radar, Smart Shuffle, and Autoplay) default heavily toward mainstream global hits, which are overwhelmingly dominated by American artists. Even if a user’s personal listening history consists primarily of UK, European, or other regional music, the algorithm frequently overrides these habits to push US-centric music. There is currently no setting to specify which geographic music scenes you want to discover or prioritise. My Suggestion for Improvement: I propose adding a "Preferred Regions/Locales" setting within the app preferences, or a localized filter toggle directly on automated playlists. This feature would allow users to select specific regions (e.g., "UK & Ireland", "Europe", "Nordic", etc.) that they want the algorithm to actively prioritize. Selecting a region would adjust the recommendation weights, preventing the feed from constantly defaulting to American charts. As a global platform, Spotify should allow listeners to explicitly support and discover talent from their own regions or specific cultures they are interested in, rather than pushing a one-size-fits-all, US-heavy music feed.
records.author: opalfruit1979

records.text: Option to Reset Listening History & Recommendations Idea Description: I’d like to suggest a feature that allows users to fully reset their listening history and music recommendations on Spotify. Currently, recommendations such as Discover Weekly, Release Radar, and Home feed suggestions are heavily influenced by past listening activity. This becomes an issue in cases where: An account is shared between multiple users with very different music tastes A user’s preferences significantly change over time The account has been used temporarily by someone else In such situations, recommendations become inaccurate and no longer reflect the primary user’s preferences. Proposed Solution: Introduce a “Reset Listening History” or “Start Fresh” option in account settings that: Clears all listening history Resets algorithmic recommendations (Discover Weekly, Daily Mixes, etc.) Optionally allows users to retain saved songs and playlists Benefits: Gives users control over their recommendation system Improves personalization accuracy Helps users who share accounts or undergo taste changes Enhances overall user satisfaction Additional Suggestion (Optional): Spotify could also consider introducing separate user profiles under one Premium account, similar to other streaming platforms, to avoid this issue altogether. Thanks for considering this feature!
records.author: rj_devil_killa_91

records.text: Re: [Amazon Alexa] Swap Spotify accounts without changing Alexa account I’m reporting a long-standing integration problem between Spotify and Amazon Alexa that affects multi-user households. Spotify allows multiple profiles under Family/Duo plans, but Alexa can only link one single Spotify account per Amazon account/device. There is no support for switching Spotify profiles based on voice profiles, Amazon Household members, or speaker recognition. As a result: All users share one Spotify profile on Alexa Listening history and recommendations are mixed Family plans lose their core benefit on voice assistants This is not an Alexa limitation. Apple Music and Amazon Music both support per-person profile mapping on Alexa. Spotify does not expose profile context via the Alexa integration. This is a design/architecture gap, not a configuration issue, and it has been raised by users for years without resolution. Please escalate this as a feature/architecture issue: Expose Spotify Family/Duo profiles to Alexa and allow profile selection via voice or account context. This is critical for families and a major reason users consider switching away from Spotify.
records.author: Fab_Joe91

records.text: Re: Release Radar messed up Hi there @Hoofmark , Thanks for reaching out! Ultimately, just like other auto-generated playlists, the Release Radar is based on your past music habits. While yes, it includes music from artists you follow, new releases from similar artists are also added in. The more you listen to your favorite content, the better recommendations you'll get. You can save your favorite content by clicking on the plus (+) icon and this will help the suggested playlists/albums to be updated so it can include more personalized results. Keep in mind that the content shown in the Discover section can vary, and it depends on different factors like your recently played content, listening habits and content that other users with similar taste listen to. We also suggest activating a Private Session when you want to listen to the relaxing frequencies you've mentioned. That way the content you'd listened to at that moment wouldn't feed your algorithm. You can find more info on how to activate a Private Session here . If you have any other doubts, just let us know!
records.author: Novy

You can save your favorite content by clicking on the plus (+) icon and this will help the suggested playlists/albums to be updated so it can include more personalized results. Keep in mind that the content shown in the Discover section can vary, and it depends on different factors like your recently played content, listening habits and content that other users with similar taste listen to. We also suggest activating a Private Session when you want to listen to the relaxing frequencies you've mentioned. That way the content you'd listened to at that moment wouldn't feed your algorithm. You can find more info on how to activate a Private Session here . If you have any other doubts, just let us know!

records.text: Re: Unwanted items in library Hi, @Momma_Jo , The screenshot you've attached actually showcases the Home page, not Your Library. The 8 cards shown at the top are shortcuts to content you've recently interacted with, while the remaining sections on the Home page are usually various recommendations from Spotify which you might like. They aren't necessarily part of Your Library, unless you've manually saved them via the (+) button. This dynamic content is subject to change and frequently updates based on your taste profile . Your Library can be accessed from its dedicated button located on the bottom and it's where you'll find your playlists, saved albums and the artists/shows you follow. 💡 Tip: You can hide certain episode- and playlist suggestions from the Home page by tapping the three dots menu and selecting ' Not interested '. Feel free to give it a try, if you haven't already - just make sure you're running the latest app updates . Podcast suggestion 'Not interested ' button Playlist suggestion 'Not interested ' button Hope this clears things up. The Community is here if there's anything else you need assistance with!
records.author: Yordan

The 8 cards shown at the top are shortcuts to content you've recently interacted with, while the remaining sections on the Home page are usually various recommendations from Spotify which you might like. They aren't necessarily part of Your Library, unless you've manually saved them via the (+) button. This dynamic content is subject to change and frequently updates based on your taste profile . Your Library can be accessed from its dedicated button located on the bottom and it's where you'll find your playlists, saved albums and the artists/shows you follow. 💡 Tip: You can hide certain episode- and playlist suggestions from the Home page by tapping the three dots menu and selecting ' Not interested '. Feel free to give it a try, if you haven't already - just make sure you're running the latest app updates . Podcast suggestion 'Not interested ' button Playlist suggestion 'Not interested ' button Hope this clears things up. The Community is here if there's anything else you need assistance with!

records.text: Ban certain podcasts from recommendation and autoplay As has for example been voiced as a concern by various users in the following board: https://community.spotify.com/t5/Other-Podcasts-Partners-etc/How-do-I-stop-a-podcast-I-don-t-like-from-autoplaying/td-p/5571492 It can be unpleasant to have Spotify's algorithm repeatedly choose to autoplay episodes of a podcast that the user has decided is not in their interest. Even giving a podcast a bad rating does not affect this recommendation and autoplay behaviour and I would propose alongside other users that were bothered by the same issue that an option is implemented in regards to removing certain podcasts from an eligible list for the autoplay algorithm to pick from. This does not have to result in any punishment of the podcast itself, but it should be possible for a user to remove content that they are consciously choosing not to be interested in to appear in proposals and autoplay. Thank you very much for considering this request and patching the app on all OS.

records.text: ability for users to adjust the AI recommendation algorithm At the moment, recommendations are generated automatically, but it would be very useful to give users some control over how those recommendations are shaped. For example, users could choose whether they want suggested tracks to be based more on their listening history, favorite genres, mood, recently played songs, discovery of new artists, or similarity to a specific track or playlist. At the moment, recommendations are generated automatically, but they are often not accurate enough. For example, when selecting “Go to Radio” from a specific song, the tracks in the generated radio often seem to match mainly the song’s release period or year, rather than its actual sound, style, mood, or genre. This can make the listening experience feel less relevant and less personalized than expected. It would be very useful to give users more control over how recommendations are generated. For example, users could choose whether suggested tracks should be based more on sonic similarity, genre, mood, favorite artists, listening history, recently played songs, or music discovery
records.author: mpiras-it

records.text: Restore a permanent Recents row on the desktop Home page The desktop app used to provide quick and reliable access to recently played albums and playlists through a dedicated Recents section on the Home page. In recent versions, the Recents row appears inconsistently and often disappears altogether, making it much harder to return to content that users have recently been listening to. I would like Spotify to restore a permanent, always-visible Recents row on the desktop Home page, similar to the experience offered in the mobile app. This section should remain available regardless of what recommendations, personalised playlists, podcasts, audiobooks or other promotional content Spotify chooses to display elsewhere on the Home page. Many users use the Home page primarily as a starting point for returning to albums and playlists they already know and listen to regularly. Having to search through activity feeds or recommendation modules to find recently used content adds unnecessary friction to a task that should be immediate. Please restore a dedicated, permanently visible Recents row on the desktop Home page.
records.author: hfsbro

records.text: Re: Autoplay activated for Apple TV. Spotify Connect problematic functioning. Hey folks, We appreciate your patience while the team looked into this. The Apple TV app doesn't have a lot of settings at the moment and they're limited, but as we're always looking for ways to improve and offer better functions, there's a possibility that more will be added in the future, so that you'll be able to change the settings directly from there. We really appreciate your feedback on the matter and we've made sure to pass your comments on to the relevant team. @-Karma- the songs that you see happen to be recommended tracks from Spotify which share the same icon as Smart Shuffle recommendations, and this is causing a bit of a confusion since Smart Shuffle is not yet supported on Apple TV. We wanted to share this detail to hopefully clear things up a bit. Once again, thank you both for posting in the forum and should you need help with anything else, we're just a post away. Cheers!
records.author: Ivelina

records.text: Channel/Radio Session options I recently came over to Spotify from Pandora sort of reluctantly. I had been with Pandora for 10 years, and I had plenty of reasons I chose to stay that long, but now I had to switch and I have some recommendations of features I really liked on Pandora. When I would listen to the radio function of an artist (my most preffered way of listening to music) I was able to select the mood of the session on the radio. It had roughly 8 functions ranging from Artist only to Relax or Energize and the function worked extremely well. It would play music in that genre or similar to that artist but it could be tailored to how you were feeling. It helped me find new music with Discover and it helped me find music in my favorite genre or by my favorite artists that was maybe more calm and relaxing for when I wanted to study or wind down for the day.

records.text: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs Please give us the option to exclude AI-generated tracks from: • Taste profile and personalized recommendations • Made For You playlists and Smart Playlists • Radio and autoplay • Release Radar and Discover Weekly I’m not against AI music in principle. But I want to be the one who decides whether I listen to it. Right now, algorithmically generated, factory-produced tracks are quietly mixed into my playlists without any way for me to opt out — and that undermines the trust I place in Spotify’s recommendations. The new AI disclosure in Song Credits is a step in the right direction, but transparency alone isn’t enough. Labeling without a filter option is like putting an allergen on a menu and then telling the kitchen they don’t have to accommodate dietary restrictions. Please make this a user setting — not a workaround, not a maybe, but an actual toggle. The choice of what I listen to should belong to me.
records.author: Herg

records.text: Incognito Mode for Listening (Exclude from Recommendations Algorithm) You know how sometimes you just want to listen to something once a random genre, a throwback, or even something you don’t normally like without messing up your recommendations? Right now, even a short listening session can influence: DJ suggestions Daily Mixes Release Radar / Discover Weekly Home recommendations Proposed solution: Add an Incognito Mode (similar to private browsing in browsers like Microsoft Edge): When enabled: Listening activity is not used for personalization Songs are not saved to taste profile DJ and algorithm ignore that session Use cases: Exploring new genres without “polluting” your algorithm Playing music for other people (friends, family, kids) One-time moods that don’t represent your taste Testing playlists or songs Why this matters: Spotify’s strength is personalization, but right now there’s no way to protect it . One session can skew recommendations for days. An Incognito Mode would give users control over their algorithm , not just their library.
records.author: Richinpr

records.text: DJ X History & Replay Spotify’s AI DJ is great at creating personalized listening sessions, but currently there is no way to view or revisit previous DJ segments. I would like Spotify to add a DJ History page where users can see previously played DJ sessions and segments. Features could include: • View previously played DJ segments • Replay an entire DJ segment • See which songs were played and which were skipped • Save a full segment to a playlist • Add all songs from a segment to Liked Songs • Rate a segment with a thumbs up or thumbs down to help improve future recommendations Sometimes the AI DJ creates an amazing mix of songs, but if you leave the session or forget a song, there is no easy way to find it again. A DJ History feature would make it easier to rediscover music, revisit favorite sessions, and provide better feedback to improve recommendations. Thank you for considering this idea.
records.author: Mrblueshorts

records.text: Simplify the interface to improve the user experience I would like to share some feedback regarding the Spotify interface, which I use regularly. For some time now, I have noticed a growing sense of visual clutter within the application. Between recommendations, sponsored playlists, podcasts, and other promoted content, navigation sometimes feels less fluid and harder to read. By comparison, some competing platforms offer a more streamlined interface, which improves overall usability and makes it easier to quickly access desired content. In my daily use, this results in: difficulty quickly finding my music or playlists a feeling of overcrowding on the home screen a loss of simplicity compared to what originally made Spotify strong I understand the value of offering recommendations and new content, but an option to simplify or further customize the interface (such as a “minimalist” mode) would be greatly appreciated. Thank you for your attention and for the continuous improvements made to the application.
records.author: sebastien_45

records.text: Better Assistant LLM Your customer support is, without a doubt, 5-star. The only recommendations I have are regarding your AI assistant. I believe that if these areas were improved, it would free up human professionals to dedicate their time to handling more critical, complex issues.

records.text: Re: My spotify premium got cancelled on vacation. Hey @idk121212 , thanks for reaching out to the Community! The country or region of your Spotify account is based on where you were when you signed up. If you move somewhere else where the Spotify service is available, you need to update your country or region so that the appropriate service for your new country of residence can be made available to you, which may cause changes to the applicable pricing and catalog availability. It sounds like you've been in a new region for an extended period of time which is why this may have happened to you. When you return back to Egypt, you'll be able to subscribe to Premium. You won't lose any saved content/recommendations when plans change. More information on changing your country/region settings here . Hope this helps!
records.author: Billy-J

records.text: Re: Age verification error Hey again @OwenJB , Thanks for returning with this additional info. In Spain, currently the minimum required age for signing up is 14 years and this would explain why you're unable to create a separate account for your daughter. There is, however, an option to create a managed account for her which we think that would be suitable for you. This way, your daughter can have a separate account and music recommendations for her while you as a parent have control over the managed account's settings. More info you can read in this article . We hope this info helped clear things up and we remain a post away if you've got any other questions.

records.text: Increase Lock Screen Playback Button Size (Safety & Accessibility Improvement) On Android devices (e.g., Google Pixel), the lock screen playback controls — particularly the “Next” and “Previous” buttons — have relatively small tap targets. While mobile usage while driving is discouraged, in real-world conditions many users briefly interact with playback controls during navigation. Small tap targets increase mis-taps and require longer visual focus, which can increase distraction. This feels more like a desktop-oriented design choice rather than one optimized for dynamic, glance-based environments. Suggestion: • Increase the effective tap area for primary playback buttons on the lock screen • Prioritize accessibility and real-world usability over compact layout aesthetics • Align tap targets more comfortably with Android accessibility recommendations Even a modest increase in touch target size could meaningfully improve usability and reduce distraction. This is not about adding features — just improving ergonomics of an existing one.
records.author: cotskafkalias

metadata.title: Increase Lock Screen Playback Button Size (Safety & Accessibility Improvement)
metadata.search_snippet: On Android devices (e.g., Google Pixel), the lock screen playback controls — particularly the “Next” and “Previous” buttons — have relatively small tap targets. While mobile usage while driving is discouraged, in real-world conditions many users briefly interact with playback controls during navigation. Small tap targets increase mis-taps and require longer visual focus, which can increase distraction. This feels more like a desktop-oriented design choice rather than one optimized for dynamic, glance-based environments. Suggestion: • Increase the effective tap area for primary playback buttons on the lock screen • Prioritize accessibility and real-world usability over compact layout aesthetics • Align tap targets more comfortably with Android accessibility recommendations Even a modest increase in touch target size could meaningfully improve usability and reduce distraction. This is not about adding features — just improving ergonomics of an existing one.

records.text: Re: Daylist title is a different language Hi there @meryrocks , Thanks for posting on the Community! Recommendations and the title of the daylist are based on your registered location. While you've already updated your language settings and performed the double-login, it can sometimes take a little while for these changes to sync across all personalized content. If you're still seeing a mix of English and Italian, your network location or VPN might be the cause. Try disabling any VPNs you may be using to see if the title reverts. Additionally, if you're on an iPhone, check Settings > General > Language & Region . If Italian is listed as a secondary language, remove it and restart Spotify. Hopefully these steps are helpful and we remain a post away if there's anything else we can assist you with.
records.author: Ivelina

metadata.title: Re: Daylist title is a different language
metadata.search_snippet: Hi there @meryrocks , Thanks for posting on the Community! Recommendations and the title of the daylist are based on your registered location. While you've already updated your language settings and performed the double-login, it can sometimes take a little while for these changes to sync across all personalized content. If you're still seeing a mix of English and Italian, your network location or VPN might be the cause. Try disabling any VPNs you may be using to see if the title reverts. Additionally, if you're on an iPhone, check Settings > General > Language & Region . If Italian is listed as a secondary language, remove it and restart Spotify. Hopefully these steps are helpful and we remain a post away if there's anything else we can assist you with.

records.text: remove sponsored recommendation It makes no sense for me to pay for the Premium version of the platform and still receive ads and content that are not aligned with my interests. My preferred musical genres are rock/rap/classical, and the platform frequently recommends songs that are out of context, from unrelated genres, or by unknown artists under the following excuse: “These recommendations are sponsored but match your musical taste.” In addition, it does not even offer a “dislike” option. This is advertising to someone who paid specifically not to have advertising. Furthermore, the service has declined in quality. Spotify used to be praised for its algorithm that truly matched music to the user’s taste. Now you “tilt the balance” to push sponsored songs. As a user, I am disappointed. When I tried to contact support, there was an attempt to justify the algorithm with arguments such as “there is a song like that in one of your playlists” or “you’ve listened to that genre before.” These are weak and unviable arguments, especially considering the platform has removed the “dislike” option. I request that the platform return to offering a service that prioritizes user satisfaction rather than “musical pamphleteering".
records.author: Drayetuoh

records.text: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs The only thing that's going to make Spotify change their system and their stance is a mass migration of users. It was clearly labeled.

Did you notice the same regardless of the playlist you choose to add songs to? A video recording. You can attach the video in your next reply in mp4 format, or upload it to YouTube or Google Drive and make it public. Note: Be aware this is a public space, so please don't share any personal info on the video you'll send us. Keep us in the loop! usable_records.text: Ways To Get Fresh Music Out of Spotify DJ Without Blocking Everything Account Wide This is a request for feature improvements. I am already aware of "exclude from taste profile," "do not play this artist," "hide this track," and the ability to type and speak instructions to the DJ. Please take the time to actually read the following, as your representatives did not do. (BTW, "hide this track" only works per session. It will play the song again in the next login. This is also true when you tell DJ, "no not play any music from my library." It will do this for a short period of time. Then default back to playing whatever it wants.) (The pdf is attached and the actual text is pasted below.) Thank you. Hi, you're through to Spotify support. Please describe your issue to get started. Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 agent You at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 We're gathering your account information. This will only take a few seconds. Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 We will connect you to an advisor! Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 An advisor is available. Would you like to chat now? Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 Yes You at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 We’ll connect you now. An advisor will review the conversation before responding. Spotify at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 To verify you’re the account owner, we may ask you to provide proof. Spotify at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 Note: If this is requested, please hide any sensitive information before sharing documentation with us, e.g. payment card or bank account numbers. Spotify at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 Danish Md is now connected and reviewing your question. They’ll message you shortly. Hey there! I will be assisting you today! I hope you are having a great day. Danish Md at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 I do understand your issue, please give me some time I will check and get back to you. Danish Md at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 Can you please elaborate your issue once? Danish Md at 10:40 AM, Jan 9 I have a complaint that is a major issue to me concerning Spotify's artist blocking and song hiding features. You at 10:40 AM, Jan 9 To confirm are you an artist? Danish Md at 10:42 AM, Jan 9 I am an artist, but this is completely unrelated to my artist account and solely related to my listening activity. You at 10:43 AM, Jan 9 Hello? You at 10:45 AM, Jan 9 Perfect do you have any screenshot or any error message? Danish Md at 10:46 AM, Jan 9 No You at 10:46 AM, Jan 9 No worries, please tell me any other issue if you want me to add it? Danish Md at 10:48 AM, Jan 9 Do you speak English, Danish Md? Your response time is very slow. You at 10:48 AM, Jan 9 I am really for this, I was forwarding the issue to the specialist team. They will look into and and the backend team will make the changes. Danish Md at 10:49 AM, Jan 9 What issue and what changes? We haven't even discussed it. You at 10:49 AM, Jan 9 I will mention the issue now, that is the reason I was asking that please add the exact issue on it so I can get it fixed through them. Danish Md at 10:50 AM, Jan 9 May I please to speak to someone else now? You at 10:51 AM, Jan 9 You mean you wanted to talk to the manager? Danish Md at 10:51 AM, Jan 9 Sure. You at 10:52 AM, Jan 9 You can tell me, I will help you with this. If you have any suggestions I will help you with this. Danish Md at 10:52 AM, Jan 9 So far, you don't seem to understand anything I've said since we began this conversation. I would prefer to speak to another agent or your supervisor. You at 10:53 AM, Jan 9 I understand that you want to share some concern regarding the Artist blocking and song hiding features. Danish Md at 10:54 AM, Jan 9 Please elaborate the issue, I will surely help you with this. Danish Md at 10:55 AM, Jan 9 When I listen to Spotify, I like as much variety and new music as possible with as few repeats as possible. The AI DJ X feature is very bad about playing the same 20 songs over and over even when I have thousands of songs in my library. Also, the shuffle feature on Spotify is very bad, but that's a separate issue that someone should address... You at 10:57 AM, Jan 9 I thought that I had found a solution by using the "don't play this artist" and "hide track" features while listening to DJ X, which finally made me very happy. However,... You at 10:58 AM, Jan 9 No we can also add the shuffle feature with this, I will add that too. Danish Md at 10:58 AM, Jan 9 Okay so now the solution is not working for you? Danish Md at 10:59 AM, Jan 9 I thought when I did that, it only applied to the DJ feature. I just found out that it is global on my account. I reorganized my playlists, singles and albums on my PC app, and now it tells me, "we can't play this until you allow this artist in the spotify phone app"! This is ridiculous! You at 11:00 AM, Jan 9 I still want to be able to play those songs and artists when I feel like manually playing them. I just don't want the DJ to play them. You at 11:01 AM, Jan 9 And why can't I re-enable them on my PC anyway if I have to do that? I have to go through everything I've just organized on the PC on my phone now and re-enable each artist and song individually? That's awful! You at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 Aare you trying it on the application or the web browser? Danish Md at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 And I don't want to do that anyway because then the DJ way start playing it again! You at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 The PC app. You at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 No worries, I will share this to the team. Danish Md at 11:05 AM, Jan 9 Okay so it is happening on every device? Danish Md at 11:05 AM, Jan 9 Thank you for letting us know, We really appreciate your providing the valuable information and helping us get better. Yeah sure I will definitely take your feedback on this and inform the relevant team. I will go ahead and pass this information to our content folks. As they are the team who will look into this and get this right. Danish Md at 11:06 AM, Jan 9 I pay for the family plan. I've been paying this premium amount for years. One reason I do that is because my original account started playing nothing but stoner-rock on the DJ. I like stoner-rock, but I don't want to hear it exclusively, so I created a second profile (this one) with no stoner-rock. Now, I'm running into another issue. It's getting really old. You at 11:06 AM, Jan 9 In case I become unavailable on your next reply, your conversation will be connected to the next available advisor so request you to stay connected. Don't worry, I've saved notes on your account, so the next advisor will know what we've discussed and take it up from there. Appreciate your patience I truly understand your concern and I agree that you have bene paying for so long and you should definitely get to use all the features. Danish Md at 11:09 AM, Jan 9 Another thing is that when I reorganized, all I did was create some folders on the PC in my Spotify library called "Singles", "Albums", "Local File Albums", etc. and moved the playlists to those folders. I didn't even block those artists or anything, but just moving them now gives me the message, ""we can't play this until you allow this artist in the spotify phone app". You at 11:10 AM, Jan 9 Sean Andrei T is now connected and reviewing your question. They’ll message you shortly. Hello there! You are now connected to English Support. I'm Sean, and I'm here to assist you with any of your concerns. How are you doing? Sean Andrei T at 11:15 AM, Jan 9 hello You at 11:17 AM, Jan 9 I can see that your case has been reassigned. Don't worry, I'm here to help further. Sean Andrei T at 11:18 AM, Jan 9 ok You at 11:18 AM, Jan 9 All caught up now. Sean Andrei T at 11:20 AM, Jan 9 ok You at 11:20 AM, Jan 9 Seems like you're having issues with the listening experience you're having when trying our DJ feature. May I know when did this problem start? Sean Andrei T at 11:21 AM, Jan 9 That's a difficult question to answer. Really, since the feature became available, but even before that, I've always had issues with Spotify's shuffle algorithm. It constantly plays the same 20 songs even when I have thousands of songs in my library. I hoped the DJ would recommend fresh music including old music I hadn't heard yet and new music that was just released, but it mostly plays stuff already in my library and hits or very popular songs that are from the same bands in my library or very similar bands that were very popular at the same time rather than new, fresh, underground stuff like I want to hear. You at 11:26 AM, Jan 9 Regarding the issues you're having with the genre of music being played, you can try this step so our DJ can recommend other songs: Simply hold down the DJ button to fine-tune your session with requests like “more upbeat” or “more new releases” and DJ will refresh your session. Or you can skip a section altogether by tapping the DJ button at the bottom of your screen. At launch, the most successful sessions are generated through a combination of “genre,” “mood,” “decade,” or “artist” requests. You can also ask DJ to: • • Play new releases • Explore your library in new ways, such as playing your liked songs • Play songs you haven’t heard in a while • Mix different artists • Adjust for tone/pace/energy levels (e.g., genre, high BPM) • Retrieve specific tracks, artists, albums, etc. However, hyper-specific queries could result in low-quality recommendations. Sean Andrei T at 11:26 AM, Jan 9 Thanks for the added context. Let me look into that further. Sean Andrei T at 11:27 AM, Jan 9 As I told the other agent, I was in love with the "do not play this artist" and "hide this track" features when I discovered them, but I thought they only applied to the DJ, not my entire account. Now, i realize i can no longer manually play those bands or songs when I want to, and since organizing my playlists on my pc, i can no longer play them. You at 11:27 AM, Jan 9 I understand your concern about that. We also have an option to exclude music from your Spotify Taste Profile, tap the three dots next to a song or playlist and select "Exclude from your taste profile," which stops it from influencing recommendations, perfect for functional listening like sleep music or kids' tunes, and you can easily toggle it back on later. This gives you more control so your personalized playlists stay relevant to your actual music taste. Have you already tried that? Sean Andrei T at 11:30 AM, Jan 9 By the way, I'm about to take my break now so in case I was not able to respond on your next reply immediately, your case will be connected to the next available advisor. Don't worry though, one of my colleagues will take over our conversation here and pick up where we left off once you reply. You at 11:32 AM, Jan 9 yes, i use the the "exclude from taste profile" feature. it helps some, but not enough. Ananth at 11:46 AM, Jan 9 how will you do that? You at 11:46 AM, Jan 9 I have completely understood your issue and I will communicate the same to the right folks and they will make sure that you won't face this issue again. Ananth at 11:48 AM, Jan 9 which issue in particular? You at 11:49 AM, Jan 9 This is the only way to turn it off • Listening to DJ only happens when you press play. • To listen, head to the Music Feed on Home, click on the DJ card, and press play. • When you’re done, just press stop, exit out of it, or hit play on another track, playlist, podcast, or audiobook! Ananth at 11:54 AM, Jan 9 I honestly have no idea what you're talking about. It is unrelated to my issue. You at 11:55 AM, Jan 9 do you have a corporate email address to contact about my concerns? You at 11:56 AM, Jan 9 Thank you for the details. Allow me a moment to check this for you. Ananth at 11:57 AM, Jan 9 **bleep** where you can write all your concern. Ananth at 11:59 AM, Jan 9 (they bleeped out the support email address) Out of the 4 or 5 agents I spoke to, I think you understood the least, but thank you for the email address at least. You at 12:01 PM, Jan 9 (I emailed he support email address this conversation and got the following email, which is why I'm here now.) Hey there! Thanks for contacting us. We understand you used the option to exclude content and also to not play this artist in our DJ app; however, those options have been applied to the rest of your app and your entire account. For now, the DJ app works this way, our goal is always to offer better listening experiences and fair value for every plan, but I can see how this situation feels far from that. I’ve shared your feedback internally so it can be reviewed by the right teams — comments like yours really help us improve and shape future decisions. To help us get your idea heard, it’d be awesome if you could add it to our ideas board: https://community.spotify.com/t5/Ideas/ct-p/newideas Other listeners can then vote and show their support. Our product teams regularly review the forum, and many amazing features have emerged from it. Thanks again for being part of Spotify and for your idea. We truly appreciate your time, and we’ll keep working to make your experience better. Andrea Spotify Customer Support

metadata.title: Ways To Get Fresh Music Out of Spotify DJ Without Blocking Everything Account Wide
metadata.search_snippet: This is a request for feature improvements. I am already aware of "exclude from taste profile," "do not play this artist," "hide this track," and the ability to type and speak instructions to the DJ. Please take the time to actually read the following, as your representatives did not do. (BTW, "hide this track" only works per session. It will play the song again in the next login. This is also true when you tell DJ, "no not play any music from my library." It will do this for a short period of time. Then default back to playing whatever it wants.) (The pdf is attached and the actual text is pasted below.) Thank you. Hi, you're through to Spotify support. Please describe your issue to get started. Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 agent You at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 We're gathering your account information. This will only take a few seconds. Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 We will connect you to an advisor! Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 An advisor is available. Would you like to chat now? Spotify at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 Yes You at 10:38 AM, Jan 9 We’ll connect you now. An advisor will review the conversation before responding. Spotify at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 To verify you’re the account owner, we may ask you to provide proof. Spotify at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 Note: If this is requested, please hide any sensitive information before sharing documentation with us, e.g. payment card or bank account numbers. Spotify at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 Danish Md is now connected and reviewing your question. They’ll message you shortly. Hey there! I will be assisting you today! I hope you are having a great day. Danish Md at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 I do understand your issue, please give me some time I will check and get back to you. Danish Md at 10:39 AM, Jan 9 Can you please elaborate your issue once? Danish Md at 10:40 AM, Jan 9 I have a complaint that is a major issue to me concerning Spotify's artist blocking and song hiding features. You at 10:40 AM, Jan 9 To confirm are you an artist? Danish Md at 10:42 AM, Jan 9 I am an artist, but this is completely unrelated to my artist account and solely related to my listening activity. You at 10:43 AM, Jan 9 Hello? You at 10:45 AM, Jan 9 Perfect do you have any screenshot or any error message? Danish Md at 10:46 AM, Jan 9 No You at 10:46 AM, Jan 9 No worries, please tell me any other issue if you want me to add it? Danish Md at 10:48 AM, Jan 9 Do you speak English, Danish Md? Your response time is very slow. You at 10:48 AM, Jan 9 I am really for this, I was forwarding the issue to the specialist team. They will look into and and the backend team will make the changes. Danish Md at 10:49 AM, Jan 9 What issue and what changes? We haven't even discussed it. You at 10:49 AM, Jan 9 I will mention the issue now, that is the reason I was asking that please add the exact issue on it so I can get it fixed through them. Danish Md at 10:50 AM, Jan 9 May I please to speak to someone else now? You at 10:51 AM, Jan 9 You mean you wanted to talk to the manager? Danish Md at 10:51 AM, Jan 9 Sure. You at 10:52 AM, Jan 9 You can tell me, I will help you with this. If you have any suggestions I will help you with this. Danish Md at 10:52 AM, Jan 9 So far, you don't seem to understand anything I've said since we began this conversation. I would prefer to speak to another agent or your supervisor. You at 10:53 AM, Jan 9 I understand that you want to share some concern regarding the Artist blocking and song hiding features. Danish Md at 10:54 AM, Jan 9 Please elaborate the issue, I will surely help you with this. Danish Md at 10:55 AM, Jan 9 When I listen to Spotify, I like as much variety and new music as possible with as few repeats as possible. The AI DJ X feature is very bad about playing the same 20 songs over and over even when I have thousands of songs in my library. Also, the shuffle feature on Spotify is very bad, but that's a separate issue that someone should address... You at 10:57 AM, Jan 9 I thought that I had found a solution by using the "don't play this artist" and "hide track" features while listening to DJ X, which finally made me very happy. However,... You at 10:58 AM, Jan 9 No we can also add the shuffle feature with this, I will add that too. Danish Md at 10:58 AM, Jan 9 Okay so now the solution is not working for you? Danish Md at 10:59 AM, Jan 9 I thought when I did that, it only applied to the DJ feature. I just found out that it is global on my account. I reorganized my playlists, singles and albums on my PC app, and now it tells me, "we can't play this until you allow this artist in the spotify phone app"! This is ridiculous! You at 11:00 AM, Jan 9 I still want to be able to play those songs and artists when I feel like manually playing them. I just don't want the DJ to play them. You at 11:01 AM, Jan 9 And why can't I re-enable them on my PC anyway if I have to do that? I have to go through everything I've just organized on the PC on my phone now and re-enable each artist and song individually? That's awful! You at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 Aare you trying it on the application or the web browser? Danish Md at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 And I don't want to do that anyway because then the DJ way start playing it again! You at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 The PC app. You at 11:03 AM, Jan 9 No worries, I will share this to the team. Danish Md at 11:05 AM, Jan 9 Okay so it is happening on every device? Danish Md at 11:05 AM, Jan 9 Thank you for letting us know, We really appreciate your providing the valuable information and helping us get better. Yeah sure I will definitely take your feedback on this and inform the relevant team. I will go ahead and pass this information to our content folks. As they are the team who will look into this and get this right. Danish Md at 11:06 AM, Jan 9 I pay for the family plan. I've been paying this premium amount for years. One reason I do that is because my original account started playing nothing but stoner-rock on the DJ. I like stoner-rock, but I don't want to hear it exclusively, so I created a second profile (this one) with no stoner-rock. Now, I'm running into another issue. It's getting really old. You at 11:06 AM, Jan 9 In case I become unavailable on your next reply, your conversation will be connected to the next available advisor so request you to stay connected. Don't worry, I've saved notes on your account, so the next advisor will know what we've discussed and take it up from there. Appreciate your patience I truly understand your concern and I agree that you have bene paying for so long and you should definitely get to use all the features. Danish Md at 11:09 AM, Jan 9 Another thing is that when I reorganized, all I did was create some folders on the PC in my Spotify library called "Singles", "Albums", "Local File Albums", etc. and moved the playlists to those folders. I didn't even block those artists or anything, but just moving them now gives me the message, ""we can't play this until you allow this artist in the spotify phone app". You at 11:10 AM, Jan 9 Sean Andrei T is now connected and reviewing your question. They’ll message you shortly. Hello there! You are now connected to English Support. I'm Sean, and I'm here to assist you with any of your concerns. How are you doing? Sean Andrei T at 11:15 AM, Jan 9 hello You at 11:17 AM, Jan 9 I can see that your case has been reassigned. Don't worry, I'm here to help further. Sean Andrei T at 11:18 AM, Jan 9 ok You at 11:18 AM, Jan 9 All caught up now. Sean Andrei T at 11:20 AM, Jan 9 ok You at 11:20 AM, Jan 9 Seems like you're having issues with the listening experience you're having when trying our DJ feature. May I know when did this problem start? Sean Andrei T at 11:21 AM, Jan 9 That's a difficult question to answer. Really, since the feature became available, but even before that, I've always had issues with Spotify's shuffle algorithm. It constantly plays the same 20 songs even when I have thousands of songs in my library. I hoped the DJ would recommend fresh music including old music I hadn't heard yet and new music that was just released, but it mostly plays stuff already in my library and hits or very popular songs that are from the same bands in my library or very similar bands that were very popular at the same time rather than new, fresh, underground stuff like I want to hear. You at 11:26 AM, Jan 9 Regarding the issues you're having with the genre of music being played, you can try this step so our DJ can recommend other songs: Simply hold down the DJ button to fine-tune your session with requests like “more upbeat” or “more new releases” and DJ will refresh your session. Or you can skip a section altogether by tapping the DJ button at the bottom of your screen. At launch, the most successful sessions are generated through a combination of “genre,” “mood,” “decade,” or “artist” requests. You can also ask DJ to: • • Play new releases • Explore your library in new ways, such as playing your liked songs • Play songs you haven’t heard in a while • Mix different artists • Adjust for tone/pace/energy levels (e.g., genre, high BPM) • Retrieve specific tracks, artists, albums, etc. However, hyper-specific queries could result in low-quality recommendations. Sean Andrei T at 11:26 AM, Jan 9 Thanks for the added context. Let me look into that further. Sean Andrei T at 11:27 AM, Jan 9 As I told the other agent, I was in love with the "do not play this artist" and "hide this track" features when I discovered them, but I thought they only applied to the DJ, not my entire account. Now, i realize i can no longer manually play those bands or songs when I want to, and since organizing my playlists on my pc, i can no longer play them. You at 11:27 AM, Jan 9 I understand your concern about that. We also have an option to exclude music from your Spotify Taste Profile, tap the three dots next to a song or playlist and select "Exclude from your taste profile," which stops it from influencing recommendations, perfect for functional listening like sleep music or kids' tunes, and you can easily toggle it back on later. This gives you more control so your personalized playlists stay relevant to your actual music taste. Have you already tried that? Sean Andrei T at 11:30 AM, Jan 9 By the way, I'm about to take my break now so in case I was not able to respond on your next reply immediately, your case will be connected to the next available advisor. Don't worry though, one of my colleagues will take over our conversation here and pick up where we left off once you reply. After that reply, there may be a moment of silence for about 4-5 minutes which is for when they're trying to connect. Sean Andrei T at 11:30 AM, Jan 9 Those features *seem* like they would help, but they don't. no matter the mood or genre i give it, it still plays **bleep** i've already heard a million times. i tried telling it "do not play any music currently in my library", which it does, but only for a short length of time, then I have to do it over and over. it always defaults back to it's basic routine after a set of songs and disregards my requests. i don't want to do that every time i run the thing. the way that I *thought* those 2 features i mentioned worked were great, until i went back and tried to manually play them and wasn't able to. if that alone were fixed, i think that would help tremendously! You at 11:32 AM, Jan 9 yes, i use the the "exclude from taste profile" feature. it helps some, but not enough. You at 11:34 AM, Jan 9 alright You at 11:36 AM, Jan 9 Ananth is now connected and reviewing your question. They’ll message you shortly. Hi, thanks for reaching out! I'll be helping you today. Ananth at 11:37 AM, Jan 9 alright. you have a very long conversation to catch up on. You at 11:37 AM, Jan 9 Yah allow me a moment to understand your concern. Ananth at 11:38 AM, Jan 9 ok You at 11:39 AM, Jan 9 I understand your concern and take it as a feedback. Ananth at 11:42 AM, Jan 9 We also have an option to exclude music from your Spotify Taste Profile, tap the three dots next to a song or playlist and select "Exclude from your taste profile," which stops it from influencing Ananth at 11:42 AM, Jan 9 that was discussed already You at 11:43 AM, Jan 9 I will take this as a feedback and make sure it reaches the right folks and make sure that you won't face this issue ever again. Ananth at 11:46 AM, Jan 9 how will you do that? You at 11:46 AM, Jan 9 I have completely understood your issue and I will communicate the same to the right folks and they will make sure that you won't face this issue again. Ananth at 11:48 AM, Jan 9 which issue in particular? You at 11:49 AM, Jan 9 This is the only way to turn it off • Listening to DJ only happens when you press play. • To listen, head to the Music Feed on Home, click on the DJ card, and press play. • When you’re done, just press stop, exit out of it, or hit play on another track, playlist, podcast, or audiobook! Ananth at 11:54 AM, Jan 9 I honestly have no idea what you're talking about. It is unrelated to my issue. You at 11:55 AM, Jan 9 do you have a corporate email address to contact about my concerns? You at 11:56 AM, Jan 9 Thank you for the details. Allow me a moment to check this for you. Ananth at 11:57 AM, Jan 9 **bleep** where you can write all your concern. Ananth at 11:59 AM, Jan 9 (they bleeped out the support email address) Out of the 4 or 5 agents I spoke to, I think you understood the least, but thank you for the email address at least. You at 12:01 PM, Jan 9 (I emailed he support email address this conversation and got the following email, which is why I'm here now.) Hey there! Thanks for contacting us. We understand you used the option to exclude content and also to not play this artist in our DJ app; however, those options have been applied to the rest of your app and your entire account. For now, the DJ app works this way, our goal is always to offer better listening experiences and fair value for every plan, but I can see how this situation feels far from that. I’ve shared your feedback internally so it can be reviewed by the right teams — comments like yours really help us improve and shape future decisions. To help us get your idea heard, it’d be awesome if you could add it to our ideas board: https://community.spotify.com/t5/Ideas/ct-p/newideas Other listeners can then vote and show their support. usable_records.text: Ongoing Issues Review: May 2026 Welcome to this month's Ongoing Issues Review where we keep you in the loop about some of the most trending ongoing issues reported here in the Spotify Community. This is where we share updates from behind the scenes and let you know what has been fixed, what we’re tracking, what we need your help with, and where things stand. You can check the full Ongoing Issue section here . FIXED 1. "Add" button in playlists - limited number of suggested songs Some changes have been made in the backend by the internal teams and the number of recommendations you're getting should be back to normal. Just make sure you're running the latest version . 😎 2. Newly saved music in "Your Library" only shows placeholder cover until actually playing a track We got confirmation in the thread that all cover art loads correctly now! 💚 3. Issues with the Search function across devices in the EU We had reports about issues with Search within Spotify and that users were getting a 'Couldn't find this' error. We can confirm that this was fixed! 4. New Episodes "Continue listening" section broken/not showing up The relevant team made some changes backstage so this should now be fixed for everyone 😎 5. Tracks skipping on desktop This was fixed - m ake sure to keep your app up-to-date with these steps . The version should be at least 1.2.88 . 6. Playback stops after a track ends Things should be up and running again - just make sure you have app version 9.1.36 (or a later one) installed ! 🎊 7. "Music/Following" filter doesn't work with large amount of followed artists Everything works fine now and we already received some user confirmations in the thread! 🎊 8. Playback issues on multiple devices All good now! Just make sure to restart the app in case you still get this behavior. 9. Spotify Windows Installer Bug, Impossible to Install Spotify! This should now be resolved for everyone - thanks to everyone who let us know in the thread! 💚 10. Downtime May 12th, 2026: Some services are down This was a short-lived ongoing issue, which was quickly resolved! ✌️ 11. Downtime May 13th: Support Site not working The same goes for this one, everything was quickly back to normal! 12. Currently playing song not highlighted in playlist while watching the music video We can confirm that this should be fixed now. We'd suggest keeping your app updated so you don't miss out on any developments. 13. Move up feature in queue creates duplicates Our teams have made some changes and this issue should be fixed as well! 😎 14. Discover Weekly playlist is blank or missing entirely We’re happy to say that this should now be fixed for everyone! 🎊 15. Spotify app crashes during video podcast on Apple TV We can confirm some changes have been made backstage and this should no longer happen! 🤞 16. Blank Now Playing View on iPad We just got confirmation that this should be fixed now! 🎊 UNDER INVESTIGATION 17. Your Episodes, Remove finished episodes after playing doesn't work We've been getting reports that the setting to remove finished episodes from the Your Episodes list no longer works for podcasts that have been downloaded either manually or via auto-download. Streamed episodes are still removed from Your Episodes as expected. 18. Unable to Join Family Plan Some of you may have noticed that there are some issues when adding new members to your Family plans. This is on our radar and it's being looked into. 19. Google Cast/ Chromecast devices unavailable for Spotify Connect Happy to announce that a new update will be available to you starting next week, which should clear this ongoing issue! 🤞 Keep us posted on how things go after you get the 9.1.48 update. 20. Saved tracks not showing up on DJ platforms We've received reports that saved tracks on users' playlists in DJ platforms or software like Serato and Rekordbox fail to load or are missing. 21. CarPlay shows the previous track's album artwork The relevant team is currently looking into this. If you're experiencing this issue, make sure to add your vote and Subscribe to the thread from the three dots menu at the top to stay tuned. ✍️ NEED MORE INFO 22. "Make this a playlist" malfunctioning. This should be fixed, but we haven't heard back from anyone experiencing this in the thread yet 👀 23. The Spotify embed is accessible due to the ""Preview" label We've asked for more info, so we can investigate further, so if you're experiencing this, make sure to join the thread! 24. Artist View crashes iPad app A new app version is slowly being rolled out and you should notice some improvements with it 🙂 Make sure to update the app - it should be at least 9.1.46. So far, we've only heard back from one user, so let us know if this was fixed on your end as well 💚 25. Fitness videos aren't working . We're trying to gather some more info from anyone affected by this, so make sure to share if you're experiencing this ✍️ NOT RIGHT NOW 26. [iOS] Switch to video button overlaps with lyrics We've heard back from the respective team and they're unable to provide an ETA for a fix at this time, but rest assured this will remain on their radar. 27. [Android Auto] Back button not working. Bricking the app . This issue is being investigated by the Android Auto team directly in Google's support community, so we'd suggest adding your feedback to Google's bug report here to stay on top of any updates. 🏎 28. DJ Recommending the same set of songs We appreciate your feedback on this, but we're unable to provide an exact ETA for a fix at this time. Make sure you're always running the latest version of Spotify so you don't miss out on any updates. 29. Playlists invisible on iPhone and iPod All details have already been passed on to the right team, but we're afraid we don't have a timeline for a fix at the moment. 30. Playing song after playlist ends does not reset queue This is the current expected behavior - playing a new track no longer reshuffles the queue, rather it replaces what your current track is and once you finish the last song, you will go back to the beginning as normal. ✔️ [New!] Focused Spotlight: Accessibility Fixes In this month's edition, we're trying something new. Alongside our usual ongoing issues roundup, we're including our first Focused Spotlight , shining a light on a specific area where your reports have led to real improvements. For this first spotlight, we're focusing on accessibility . May 21st is Global Accessibility Awareness Day , so it felt like the right time to share what's been happening behind the scenes when it comes to making Spotify easier to use for everyone. Over the past few months, a number of accessibility-related issues reported through the Community and our support channels have been investigated and resolved. Here's a rundown of some of what's been fixed between in recent months: KEYBOARD AND NAVIGATION 1. iPad Magic Keyboard shortcuts stopped working | Fixed February 2026 iPad users with a Magic Keyboard found that the space bar stopped working for play/pause. This has been fixed. Keyboard shortcuts on iPad with Magic Keyboard are working again as expected. 2. Search keyboard not appearing on mobile | Fixed February 2026 On mobile, double-tapping the Search icon wasn't bringing up the keyboard automatically, which meant extra steps to start typing. The keyboard now appears as it should when you tap into Search. Community thread 3. Play/pause keyboard button not working on video podcasts (Mac) | Fixed February 2026 On Mac, the play/pause button on the keyboard wasn't responding properly during video podcasts. This has been resolved. Keyboard media controls now work consistently across audio and video content on Mac. Community thread 4. iPad Library flickering with Magic Keyboard | Fixed April 2026 The iPad Library screen was flickering when connected to an Apple Magic Keyboard. This visual glitch has been fixed. The Library now displays normally with an external keyboard attached. 5. Tab switching resetting your scroll position on Android | Fixed April 2026 On Android, switching between tabs (like Home, Search, Library) was resetting your scroll position, so you'd lose your place. Tab switching now preserves where you were. You won't be sent back to the top when moving between sections. TRANSCRIPTS AND CAPTIONS 1. Podcast transcripts couldn't be turned off | Fixed February 2026 The "Read Along" transcript option was at times covering the podcast artwork, and there was no way to turn it off. Users now have more control over transcript display. This has been resolved. 2. Podcast episode list kept resetting to the top | Fixed February 2026 Podcast episode lists were resetting to the top of the list whenever you navigated away and came back. Your scroll position is now saved, so you can pick up where you left off. These are just a few of the recent fixes, however we’re hoping to bring you more Team Spotlights in the future to highlight different areas where your feedback has made a difference. If you've run into any issues recently, please keep sharing it here. It really does help us make Spotify better for everyone! For more information on Accessibility at Spotify, or for our dedicated Accessibility feedback form head here ! What if you spot an OI? Check our Ongoing Issues board and hit +VOTE if you’re affected—it helps us prioritize. Need quick help? Post your question in the Help Boards Got feedback? Share it in Ideas If you'd also like to see some changes before other users you can sign-up to join our thousands of Beta testers here . https://open.spotify.com/track/0xjkgYSzHjBZNvyUaC9cXX?si=3efa4844d42a4462
usable_records.author: Dessi

Playlists invisible on iPhone and iPod All details have already been passed on to the right team, but we're afraid we don't have a timeline for a fix at the moment. 30. Playing song after playlist ends does not reset queue This is the current expected behavior - playing a new track no longer reshuffles the queue, rather it replaces what your current track is and once you finish the last song, you will go back to the beginning as normal. ✔️ [New!] Focused Spotlight: Accessibility Fixes In this month's edition, we're trying something new. Alongside our usual ongoing issues roundup, we're including our first Focused Spotlight , shining a light on a specific area where your reports have led to real improvements. For this first spotlight, we're focusing on accessibility . May 21st is Global Accessibility Awareness Day , so it felt like the right time to share what's been happening behind the scenes when it comes to making Spotify easier to use for everyone. Over the past few months, a number of accessibility-related issues reported through the Community and our support channels have been investigated and resolved. Here's a rundown of some of what's been fixed between in recent months: KEYBOARD AND NAVIGATION 1. iPad Magic Keyboard shortcuts stopped working | Fixed February 2026 iPad users with a Magic Keyboard found that the space bar stopped working for play/pause. This has been fixed. Keyboard shortcuts on iPad with Magic Keyboard are working again as expected. 2. Search keyboard not appearing on mobile | Fixed February 2026 On mobile, double-tapping the Search icon wasn't bringing up the keyboard automatically, which meant extra steps to start typing. The keyboard now appears as it should when you tap into Search. Community thread 3. Play/pause keyboard button not working on video podcasts (Mac) | Fixed February 2026 On Mac, the play/pause button on the keyboard wasn't responding properly during video podcasts. This has been resolved. Keyboard media controls now work consistently across audio and video content on Mac. Community thread 4. iPad Library flickering with Magic Keyboard | Fixed April 2026 The iPad Library screen was flickering when connected to an Apple Magic Keyboard. This visual glitch has been fixed. The Library now displays normally with an external keyboard attached. 5. Tab switching resetting your scroll position on Android | Fixed April 2026 On Android, switching between tabs (like Home, Search, Library) was resetting your scroll position, so you'd lose your place. Tab switching now preserves where you were. You won't be sent back to the top when moving between sections. TRANSCRIPTS AND CAPTIONS 1. Podcast transcripts couldn't be turned off | Fixed February 2026 The "Read Along" transcript option was at times covering the podcast artwork, and there was no way to turn it off. Users now have more control over transcript display. This has been resolved. 2. Podcast episode list kept resetting to the top | Fixed February 2026 Podcast episode lists were resetting to the top of the list whenever you navigated away and came back. Your scroll position is now saved, so you can pick up where you left off. These are just a few of the recent fixes, however we’re hoping to bring you more Team Spotlights in the future to highlight different areas where your feedback has made a difference. If you've run into any issues recently, please keep sharing it here. It really does help us make Spotify better for everyone! For more information on Accessibility at Spotify, or for our dedicated Accessibility feedback form head here ! What if you spot an OI? Check our Ongoing Issues board and hit +VOTE if you’re affected—it helps us prioritize. Need quick help? Post your question in the Help Boards Got feedback? Share it in Ideas If you'd also like to see some changes before other users you can sign-up to join our thousands of Beta testers here . https://open.spotify.com/track/0xjkgYSzHjBZNvyUaC9cXX?si=3efa4844d42a4462

usable_records.text: #MusicMonday Review - February 2026 Part 2 #MusicMonday is the hashtag I've been using for quite a while to share music recommendations from up-and-coming artists. Always fresh, and always different, trying to look for trends before they become one. You can check February's Part 1 review for more music. In this edition, let's explore the dark side of technology, not without looking back on how history, and our families, can give us a guiding light to navigate through life. Give them a listen, with a word from the artists themselves. 🎧 Thistle Sifter – Ghost Acres "We're totally dependent on farming, and it's surrounded by all these sort of comforting myths and stories for the very young children about this lovely livestock farm where there's one horse, one cow , one pig, one cat, one dog living in harmony, and that's how we'd like to see it, but the reality is it's the greatest cause of habitat destruction, greatest cause of extinction, one of the greatest causes of climate breakdown, of fresh water pollution, of marine pollution, also major cause actually of air pollution. We don't think about that very often. It's a major cause of soil loss, a major cause of water use. It is amazing how little it features in our environmental consciousness. We talk about transport, we talk about home insulation, we talk about industry, and yet somehow we don't want to go there." We begin our travels in Utrecht, NL with a Progressive Post-Rock track about the classic line of thought regarding the countryside as the seed of innocence and purity, while making the city an evil, corrupt, entity. Is there more to the story? "Ghost Acres was inspired by the book Regenesis by George Monbiot, and also features a quote from Monbiot talking about the effects of modern day agriculture on our natural environment. In an age where most developed countries outsource food production, ghost acres represent the real amount of land that is needed to produce the products that we consume. The title of the track symbolizes the idea that most Westerners live their lives dependent on ghost acreage, far removed from the origins of the food they are consuming." spotify:track:3k3E4vHumb9ANAwncKKAhb:small CALL IT VAIN – Saturday Superhero I’m left with these growing pains But nothing else feels the same As it once did, the silence Covered in laughs as a kid What I’d do to bring it all back It’s just a sigh for attention To teach me a lesson again I find myself staring at the ceiling, then I try to, don’t like to Lock it up, can’t cry, no time to We go now to Yorkshire, England for an Indie Rock track about that person whose guidance saves you from breaking down, and makes you nostalgic from remembering everything you did together: "I wrote this when I was 18 and wanted to use it as a way of expressing my relationship with my dad. I’ve always had a lot of respect for my dad and I’m very grateful for everything he’s done for me. I’ve always wanted to make him proud and that’s something I go into in the song. I talk about feelings of self-doubt, admiration and anything else that makes a father-son relationship. This is my favourite song to play live because it always reminds me of one of the reasons I started playing music in the first place, because of him." spotify:track:32Hv3TIUaYi1MK6FfTDdL4:small The Corner Laughers – Rainbow Cardigan I climbed up to Kendal Castle on a cold and rainy day Saw the ruins all around us and the town so old and grey Wrapped in a rainbow cardigan to keep the blues at bay I went whistling through the graveyard, for I didn't know the way Stumbled in the muddy puddles past the stone walls in decay Wrapped in a rainbow cardigan to keep above the fray From Redwood City, CA, we get an Indie Pop/Folk song about being old, and young, at the same time, with all the faults, and responsibilities, that makes us who we are : "Often when I write a song, it starts with a little scrap of something and only after it’s all stitched together is the pattern unveiled. Here, the concept seems to be of a woven textile piece representing the knitting together of time and space – a kind of cosmic map or web. The song is a bit of a microcosm of the album as a whole, with its imagery of colors and shades, its distinctive blend of sadness and joy, anxiety and wonder. There is, in fact, a real rainbow cardigan, which I fell in love with and bought from a little shop with a wonderful owner in the town of Kendal, England. Looking around at the ruins, I realized that old as they were, in the grand, universal scheme of things, they – and all of humankind’s civilizations and buildings – they really aren’t old at all. And that leads into how it feels to me, heading into my own 'middle ages', sometimes unspeakably ancient and sometimes like a little child. Perhaps you’ve felt the same? We then move to one of my favorite places on Earth – Lake Lagunitas, in Fairfax, California, where I grew up – and to the way reliving past mistakes can seem to replay endlessly. I was thinking of earthquake faults and topographic maps, which lead me back to textiles. All the good and bad and colors and textures of life woven and wrapped up together, I suppose." spotify:track:2zRXdw8Ool5XE5VaHxA4Li:small Baby Condor – Backcountry Towns Your eyes betray all lies you ever told your smile fails to disguise the ones still on your tongue If only you were stolen from your golden coach to roam the concrete jungle home to common folk You’d know We fly now to Deventer, NL for a Modern Americana track about how simple life in the country is, without the complications of technology: "Well it’s basically a protest song against the modern oligarchs, mainly because of what’s happening in the US with the big tech bros. consolidating power by controlling the information flow and killing our critical thinking. Small town simple truths and humaneness for the win 😄 " spotify:track:5qGzVDh9YMXhz1OSK35V1u:small Howling Hawk – Nine Miles High There’s poison in the air and there’s lead in the water And we're dead in the water if we believe the news Nine miles high, there’s an eye in the sky They see inside and they don’t like the view They see you in the morning and they see you in the dark And you’re halfway there if you take it all to heart Nine miles high, electric is the night They’re anywhere and everywhere you are Our musical trip ends in London, England with a Britpop track helping someone with a serious technology addiction, where sunshine seems so far behind: "A friend of ours was going absolutely mental, spending too much time online and believing in all the noise. He got really paranoid, thought people were spying on him through his phone. The song is about pulling someone out of that hole." spotify:track:3vxnvMgQC2JnnSBqpi3Xgo:small #MusicMonday 2025 It's time to recap 2025 with this year's compilation. 76 titles from original artists covering all types of genres, represent the multiple trends independent artists were proud to establish. Have a listen: spotify:playlist:71fadol0hgsdSjLpM828HZ Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!
usable_records.author: osornios
