usable_records.text: Avoid Duplicate Tracks in Radio Stations When Identical Versions Already Exist in the Library The “Radio” feature from a song or artist is one of the best parts of Spotify and something I use almost every day. It’s a great way to discover music and keep a vibe going. However, there is a frustrating issue that happens quite often. Radio stations frequently recommend songs without checking if an identical version of that track already exists in the listener’s library or listening history. Because of this, the same song can appear repeatedly, just from different releases or publications. For example, I really like the rock band Audioslave. For some reason, their label has released many duplicate versions of the same songs across different albums, compilations, and editions. I assume this might be related to catalog or monetization strategies, but the result is that Spotify treats them as different tracks. Because of this, whenever I start almost any rock radio station, I often hear “Like a Stone” again, but from a different release. Over time, I ended up liking multiple identical versions of the same song because they appear separately. I currently have several copies of the exact same track in my library, with the same length and recording, just linked to different releases. The bigger problem is repetition! Since the platform treats these versions as separate tracks, the radio algorithm can recommend them multiple times, which makes the listening experience feel repetitive and eventually reduces the enjoyment of songs I actually like. A possible improvement would be to detect identical recordings across different releases and treat them as a single track for radio recommendations and library management. This could help reduce repetition and create a much smoother listening experience.
usable_records.author: btz1

metadata.title: Avoid Duplicate Tracks in Radio Stations When Identical Versions Already Exist in the Library
metadata.search_snippet: The “Radio” feature from a song or artist is one of the best parts of Spotify and something I use almost every day. It’s a great way to discover music and keep a vibe going. However, there is a frustrating issue that happens quite often. Radio stations frequently recommend songs without checking if an identical version of that track already exists in the listener’s library or listening history. Because of this, the same song can appear repeatedly, just from different releases or publications. For example, I really like the rock band Audioslave. For some reason, their label has released many duplicate versions of the same songs across different albums, compilations, and editions. I assume this might be related to catalog or monetization strategies, but the result is that Spotify treats them as different tracks. A possible improvement would be to detect identical recordings across different releases and treat them as a single track for radio recommendations and library management. This could help reduce repetition and create a much smoother listening experience.

usable_records.text: How do I clean up the app and remove recommendations? Device Samsung S23U/android My Question or Issue Over the last year or so, the app has become so cluttered with excessive suggestions/recommendations and it's really ruining what used to be a nice clean experience. In the past my home page would immediately give me access to the most recent, and most commonly played songs/shows Now, the grid at the top is taken up mostly by things I listened to once, or ages ago and have no interest in. An extra search bar because why not and after that I'm bombarded with complete nonsense, from audio books I don't want, prompts I never asked for and why am I constantly being told to listen to conspiracy theory and engineering podcasts?? And then double it all because videos now. Sorry Spotify but you'll never be YouTube or Audible, and the more aggressively you try the more you're just making the experience worse of what you do best, music and podcasts. 80% of the usable space in the all page has now become "try this..." "explore this..." "recommend..." "suggested.." instead of the things I actually want, the things I actually like and know I want to listen to. It feels like that is being behind recommendation spam fueld by an algorithm that died two years ago, recommending artists and songs I've not bothered with for ages, and "new" videos from creators I watched on YouTube a month ago. And I have to click through 5 pages and a filter just to get to my own selection. Is there any way to disable this? To put things back the way they were a year or so ago? Thanks
usable_records.author: Minska

usable_records.text: Comprehensive AI Policy on Songs, Album Artwork, Music Videos, and Spotify UI I've seen a lot of complaints about various AI-related issues so I wanted to write my thoughts covering pretty much all of the ones I'm aware of in one post. Ideally, AI generated content would be banned entirely, but that probably won't happen. That being said, Artists should be required to disclose if a song was written, composed, "sung," or any combination of the three by AI, if the album artwork was generated by AI, and if the music video was generated by AI before they upload songs/albums. People who are caught lying should be subject to warnings and have the song, album artwork, and/or music video in question marked as AI and people who lie three or more times should be banned. Any song that was written, composed, or "sung" by AI should be marked as such not just in the credits but next to the title in the same manner that songs are labeled as explicit. Any album artwork or music video generated by AI should have a label in the corner of the image/video saying so. Artists should be required to disclose if they are AI "artists" - i.e. "musicians" who exclusively upload songs that are written, composed, and/or "sung" by AI. Users should have the right to block songs and artists that have been labeled as AI from playing and showing up in their algorithms in forms such as Daily Mixes, genre mixes, Discover Weekly, and home page recommendations. Any Spotify feature powered by AI should be clearly labeled as such. Users should have the right to disable individual AI powered Spotify features in their settings.
usable_records.author: leukos

usable_records.text: Re: Issues with using "Search" - keyboard does not appear automatically "This is how it works today. To open keyboard when hitting the search button: 1. Tap search -> moves to page for search 2. Tap search field at top of search page. Old searches and recommendations can be viewed here-> field transforms to a writeable field in grey 3. Tap the grey field to type in it -> keyboard upens up." It works, thanks. But it's ridiculous. First of all, it was possible to tap the lens twice: the first tap would show the search box, the second tap would show the keyboard. It was perfect, why change it? Now, the second tap brings you back. But not only it brings you back: it stacks tabs! So, if you really want to go back, you have to swipe left multiple times to go back and forth multiple times through the Search tab until you get back to the home. This is already a nuisance. But, even if we accept the fact that it's not possible to tap the lens twice, the first tap on the "What do you want to listen" (or whatever it's called in English, I'm translating literally from the Italian version) box should open the keyboard already. There's no way an expert UX Designer would accept a UX where the use case is "User wants to search for an item" and you have to tap the same element twice to open the keyboard. And the fact that so many people couldn't find a way to show the keyboard at all (including me, and I am a software engineer...) and thought this was a bug shows how counterintuitive this solution is.
usable_records.author: tompabes

usable_records.text: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs A mod said that AI disclosure will not do anything for song recommendations but it NEEDS to. Discover Weekly is my favorite feature on Spotify. It has been since it existed and it’s where I have found my favorite songs. I am maybe 10 songs into my Discover Weekly today at so far 3 artists were AI. All of these took music created from real people and make them “reimagined.” Many of them had covers featuring AI generated Black Artists. Only 1 disclosed that it was AI. You have robots pretending to be Black Artists stealing songs from real people. Remember how it felt to learn about Milli Vanilli? This is worse. I have been Ride or Die for Spotify since middle school. It was the first subscription services I ever paid for and it’s the one I’ve always said I’d never give up. But this is insane. This is I miss my Discover Weekly giving me fresh, new artists and songs. Spotify, you MUST: 1. Put a button in the settings that can opt out of AI artists (I know you can do this, it will basically serve the same function as opting out of explicit songs) 2. All AI songs need to have a sticker or some flag to designate its AI-generated status 3. Any artists that does not disclose they are an AI artist should have the ability to be reported and investigated. 4. At the very least, allow us to have a button to take AI songs off of our Discover Weekly, Release Radars, and other mixes and recommendations.
usable_records.author: coniejo

usable_records.text: Seamless Multi-User Profile Support for tvOS App (Like Apple Music) (Time is precious, so I asked a generative AI to write this using the prompt: "You're a Spotify Community expert. Help me write the best idea suggestion making the Spotify multiple user experience seemless on the current version of tvOS. Maybe comparing to other tvOS music apps that already have this feature.") Idea: Implement native multi-user profile switching in the Spotify tvOS app using Apple's "Runs as Current User" APIs, for a household-friendly experience matching Apple Music and other tvOS apps. In households sharing Apple TV 4K (tvOS 18+), multiple user profiles provide personalized Up Next queues, recommendations, and settings across apps. Spotify's tvOS app lags behind: no profile picker, no automatic login per system user—forcing manual logouts/logins or clunky Spotify Connect workarounds that don't sync playlists or history. Why it matters: Families on Premium Family plans can't easily access individual libraries, breaking the shared living room vibe. Competitors nail this: - Apple Music: Auto-detects active TV profile, loads user-specific music, radio, and recommendations seamlessly. - YouTube Music/Premium: Quick profile switcher respects TV users. Apple's APIs make it straightforward—tvOS 16+ handles profile mapping automatically via UserDefaults and Keychain for secure, per-user data (no more interstitial prompts). A simple checkbox in app settings could opt-in: "Support Multiple Users. Requested features: - Profile picker on launch/switch, storing preferences per TV user. - Per-profile: Playlists, listening history, recommendations, login credentials. - Backward-compatible for single-user setups. This would boost retention on Apple TV—please prioritize! Upvote if your household needs it.
usable_records.author: djdare9

usable_records.text: Re: Autoplay/ Radio not working properly I can confirm a similar issue on the same Spotify desktop version. Plan: Premium Device: PC Operating System: Windows 11 64-bit Spotify version: 1.2.90.451.gb094aab0 The issue seems related to playback context handling after the recent Autoplay / Radio problems. Scenario: Artist discography playback. Spotify recently improved the desktop experience by adding better Discography sorting, including the ability to sort albums from oldest to newest. This finally allowed users to listen through an artist's catalog chronologically. Steps to reproduce: Open an artist page in the desktop app. Go to Discography. Sort albums from oldest to newest. Start playing the first album in the sorted discography. Wait until the album finishes. Expected behavior: Spotify should preserve the selected Discography playback context and continue through the artist's albums according to the chosen sorting. Actual behavior: Spotify leaves the Discography context and switches to recommendation / Artist Radio behavior instead. This makes the recently added Discography sorting feature much less useful. The sorting works visually, but the playback queue no longer follows the user's selected context. This is not a request to remove Autoplay or recommendations. They are useful features when intentionally selected. The problem is that playback started from a sorted Discography view should behave differently from starting a radio or recommendation session. Please check if the recent Autoplay / Radio changes affected Discography playback context handling. https://community.spotify.com/t5/Your-Library/Can-t-play-an-artist-s-discography-on-the-desktop-app/m-p/7453206#M70011
usable_records.author: mikcio2

usable_records.text: Missed recommendation recap The idea is about implementing in Spotify short audio previews of songs suggested by Autoplay / Radio / Smart Shuffle Idea Sometimes Spotify recommends great songs during everyday listening (work, driving, running), but users cannot save them in time. Currently, once the session ends, these recommendations are effectively lost. I propose a weekly recap containing short previews (10–30 seconds) of tracks that Spotify recommended to the user but were not liked or saved. This recap would appear on the Home screen as a temporary playlist or story-style card: “Missed Recommendations This Week” --- How it works Spotify already tracks recommended tracks during listening sessions (Autoplay, Radio, Smart Shuffle, Search suggestions). Once per week Spotify would: 1. Collect recommended tracks played but not saved 2. Select the most relevant ones 3. Generate short preview snippets (10–30 seconds) 4. Present them as a swipeable recap For each track the user could: - ❤️ Like - ➕ Add to playlist - ❌ Dismiss --- Why this matters Many users listen while busy (sports, commuting, work). Discovery is passive — saving is active. Currently discovery quality is high, but retention of discovered music is low because users cannot react in the moment. This feature: - prevents losing good recommendations - increases saves and engagement - improves trust in the recommendation system - creates a lightweight weekly ritual similar to Wrapped, but frequent --- User value Spotify becomes not only a player but a memory of discovery. Users rediscover what they almost liked. --- Optional extension Daily short recap or auto-generated “discovery mashup” shareable as a story.
usable_records.author: Czyzyk06

usable_records.text: Removal of “Clear Queue” on mobile breaks intent-based listening and creates driving distractions I’m a long-time Premium subscriber and multi-genre listener. Recent mobile app updates removed the ability to clear the playback queue, which has significantly degraded the listening experience. Previously, users could intentionally reset the queue and start a new listening session. Now, Spotify auto-injects “Recommended tracks” that cannot be cleared in bulk. This creates several problems: • Breaks intent-based listening — I often switch genres based on mood or context (e.g., classical in the morning, hip-hop later, jazz in the evening). Having prior genres forced into the queue makes listening chaotic rather than curated. • Forces algorithmic recommendations over user control — even when Autoplay is disabled, the queue remains polluted unless overridden by starting new playback. • Creates a driving safety issue — when listening in the car (including CarPlay), unexpected tracks from past listening sessions begin playing after selections end. This is distracting and requires additional interaction while driving to correct. • This is a regression — the functionality to clear the queue existed and was removed without an equivalent replacement. Autoplay recommendations can be a useful feature when they are optional and context-aware, but users should retain full control over their playback queue. Requested fixes (any would resolve this): • Restore a “Clear Queue” option on mobile • Allow full disabling of recommendation injection into the queue • Separate user-queued content from algorithmic recommendations Spotify works best when it supports intentional listening, not when it overrides it.
usable_records.author: Kastrothfl

usable_records.text: Re: "not interested" button has disappeared Hi there! Thank you for bringing up the concern. You’re not doing anything wrong. The “Not interested” option has been removed or hidden for many users as part of recent Home feed changes and testing. That’s why it suddenly disappeared after the update. It’s not device-specific and not something you can turn back on locally. A few things that might reduce this content, even though none fully replace that button: Tap the three dots on the show and choose Hide this show if that option still appears. It’s inconsistently available, but it does sometimes work. Go to Settings > Content preferences and turn off Video podcasts where available. This doesn’t always apply retroactively, but it can reduce future recommendations. Make sure you’re not following or auto-following any shows tied to those categories. Unfollow them explicitly if needed. That said, your main point is valid. Removing user-level negative feedback while increasing algorithmic recommendations leaves people stuck with content they’ve clearly signaled they don’t want. Reporting shows doesn’t solve that, especially when the issue is recommendation logic, not just policy violations. This is something Spotify really needs to address: users should be able to say “don’t show me this” without jumping through hoops. You’re far from the only person raising this concern. If nothing else, upvoting similar threads helps keep it visible to Spotify staff.
usable_records.author: Koyubi

usable_records.text: AI Generated Music Recommendations I was listening to 60s music and was recommended an AI generated song of the 60s surf rock genre, which I found problematic. Spotify Support suggested that I was recommended AI music based on my listening style of 40s-60s music... which is an absurdity considering AI didn't exist in the 40s-60s, but they also suggested I block the artist to train my algorithm. The issue is that if I block the artist, this affects my listening algorithm of the genuinely produced genre. If I turn off Autoplay (as recommended by Support), all other recommended songs won't automatically play... The fact that AI generated music exists is one thing, the fact that it's a Spotify recommendation is another, but the added salt of it affecting the algorithm searching for music I'd genuinely like to hear is insulting. But there's a simple solution. There exists software that can detect the likelihood of AI generated content. I argue that uploaded media should be screened upon submission and AI generated music should be tagged as such. With this tag, there should be a filter in settings "Do not recommend me AI content". This would preserve your algorithm while avoiding AI generated music. This is a serious issue as AI generated music is not seen as separate from human made music, such that you cannot target AI content against your listening algorithm which severely impacts user experience.
usable_records.author: ZacL

usable_records.text: Re: Wrapped 2025 MY 2025 WRAPPED SONGS ARE ALMOST ALL FROM PLAYLISTS I HAD MARKED AS EXCLUDED FROM TASTE PROFILE I very often play chill, repetitive, low rhythmic songs like some LoFi, synthwave and such, only for study or work sessions. I don't actually love or hate them, obviously I like them enough to have them in a playlist but MAINLY I just use them as a tool so that I can focus. The thing is, I deliberately have such songs in separate playlists not just for the convenience but also because I don't want these to be included in the recommendation algorithm, so I got them marked as 'exclude from taste profile'. It was working fine, they weren't included in my weekly recommendations and such but then the 2025 wrapped content came out and what I was fearing happened: almost all my 'favorite/most played songs' from 2025 were not the ones I actually like the most but the 'tool' songs I thought (or wanted to think) were being excluded. In my opinion, these songs that exists in this excluded playlist and nowhere else for my profile, should be excluded not only for taste profile but also for any content you guys create for us. Does this behavior make sense to you guys? Please share some feedback on why this is working as expected, otherwise I'd much appreciate it gets fixed for my Wrapped 2026. Thanks! Luis.

usable_records.text: Discovery weekly hasn't worked for months First of all, the buttons for genres in the discovery weekly do nothing at all, they just result in a spinning animation around the button and ultimately do nothing. This is on both the windows app (not website) and on android. Website doesn't even have the buttons. Secondly, the content is not like anything I listen to. It used to be full of songs that were similar to my tastes but new. Like it should be. Now discovery weekly has been ONLY the most generic Finnish songs that you'd hear from a "classics" FM radio for months now. The recommendations have nothing to do with what I listen to, I am absolutely sure of that. I've listened to mostly jazz/doom metal/progressive rock for the past few months, without any lyrics whatsoever. So how could every song in the discovery have lyrics in Finnish? None of the songs even match the genres I listen to. It's like the algorithm just gives the most common recommendations based on what a Finnish man in his 40's-50's would listen to. I have tried to unfollow the playlist and refollow it to no avail. Plan Premium Country Finland Device Samsung galaxy S25 ultra, and Windows PC Operating System Android, Windows 11 My Question or Issue

usable_records.text: Re: When listening to Release Radar or Discover Weekly and hiding a song, the wrong song is played n This issue with Release Radar or Discover Weekly usually happens due to how the app refreshes its queue after you hide a track. When you hide a song, the system removes it from your personalized playlist, but sometimes the playback queue doesn’t update instantly. As a result, it may skip incorrectly or play a different (seemingly wrong) next track. Here are a few things you can try to fix it: Refresh the playlist after hiding a song (pull down or reopen the app) Clear the queue and restart playback from the playlist Log out and log back into your account Make sure your app is updated to the latest version Clear cache (especially on mobile devices) This is mostly a temporary glitch rather than a major issue, and updates usually fix it over time. Also, just like how a helps organize and refine your design choices, music algorithms are constantly learning and adjusting based on your preferences. So hiding songs will eventually improve your recommendations, even if small playback bugs happen in the short term. If the issue keeps happening, it’s best to report it through the app’s support so it can be fixed in future updates.
usable_records.author: freecolortool

usable_records.text: Custom radio or playlist based on personal playlist I’d like to propose a feature that allows users to generate a radio station based on an entire playlist, not just a single track. Right now, Spotify only offers the “Go to radio” option for individual songs, and while helpful, it doesn’t capture the full range of a listener’s taste when that taste is represented through a curated playlist. The suggested songs section beneath playlists also falls short, as it often repeats the same recommendations or misses the overall mood and direction of the playlist entirely. A playlist-based radio feature would take the combined data from all songs within a user’s playlist and create a dynamic radio station or auto-generated playlist that reflects the broader style, tone, and preferences the user has built over time. This would be especially beneficial for users with large or thoughtfully curated playlists, as it would open the door to discovering new music that aligns much more accurately with their actual listening patterns. It differentiates itself from current features by using the playlist as a whole, resulting in recommendations that are more precise, varied, and relevant. This enhancement would deepen personalization and make music discovery far more engaging for users who invest time into shaping their playlists.
usable_records.author: Michael3857

usable_records.text: Revert to old model for recommended tracks Hi there, I have been wanting to give feedback for months. Something very bad is happening with your recommendation systems. Discovery and serendipity have dropped dramatically and it is horrible. It means I am in an echo chamber of music. Not only that, but another weird thing I have noticed is that when I put on my 60's/70's rock playlist, when it ends it moves onto recommended tracks. This used to continue on with the same taste profile of the playlist itself which often would surface music along the same profile and was a great outcome. In the last 3-6 months I have noticed when a playlist ends, it recommended tracks reverts to my recent listening history and regurgitates what I already listen to. So in the case of this 60's/70's rock playlist, when it ends, it plays maybe 1-2 songs of 60's/70's rock and then dives into my overall music taste of deep house and it just plays deep house tracks from my 2026 Summer playlist. This is such a jarring and glaring issue. Your recommendation engines are not doing well and I urge you to revert to an older model. The new one IS NOT GOOD.
usable_records.author: ritchles

usable_records.text: Volume-Based Recommendations: The Missing Signal User behavior on mobile devices shows a distinct "High-Affinity" signal: The Volume Spike. When a user discovers a track they love, their first physical interaction is often a significant increase in volume (30%–50%+ increase). Conversely, a decrease in volume or a "mute" during a track is a subtle negative signal that current skip-logic misses. The Proposed Feature: "Volume-Weighted Affinity" I suggest integrating Volume Delta (the change in volume level) as a third-layer feedback signal to refine the "Taste Profile." Positive Reinforcement: A volume increase of >X% within the first 60 seconds of a track correlates to high emotional resonance. The algorithm should weight the metadata of this track more heavily for immediate next-track selection. The "Environmental" Filter: To avoid noise (e.g., turning up volume because the bus got loud), the signal can be normalized using the device's microphone (Ambient Noise Floor) or Accelerometer (detecting movement). If the environment is stable but the volume goes up, the signal is "Active Interest." Negative Refinement: A "Fade Out" (slowly lowering volume) during a track provides a more nuanced negative signal than a "Hard Skip," suggesting the track doesn't fit the current mood even if it fits the genre .
usable_records.author: georgelightning

usable_records.text: Idea: optional filter to exclude AI-generated music from recommendations Hi everyone, I’ve been a Spotify user for years and music has always meant something deeply personal to me. Not background noise — real human connection. Recently I found out that an artist I genuinely enjoyed, Morgan Luna, doesn’t exist. There’s no real person behind the voice. It’s a fully AI-generated project designed to look and feel like a real singer, with a profile, album covers, and millions of streams. I had no idea. Spotify gave me close to zero signals — I had to figure it out myself outside the platform. I’m not against AI music existing. But I am against not being able to choose. My idea is simple: give users an optional filter in Settings to exclude AI-generated music from recommendations, radio, and search results. Think of it like Waze letting you avoid toll roads. Nobody bans the tolls — they just give you the choice. I want to listen to music made by humans, for humans. I just want Spotify to let me choose that. Please vote if you feel the same way. The more of us ask for this, the harder it is to ignore. Thanks for reading.
usable_records.author: Alberto_MG

usable_records.text: What if Spotify just knew — the moment you opened it? What if Spotify had AI? Not just recommendations. Something that actually understands you. I commute to office every day. And every morning it's the same thing open Spotify, the music is there, the mood is there, I even have playlists I made myself. But in that moment I can never find the song that matches exactly how I feel. So I built a concept around this problem. A feature where you tell it once whether you're commuting, working from home, at the gym, or just relaxing. It learns your environment and combines it with your time of day and weather to build your perfect soundtrack automatically. No input needed every single day. It also has an AI Search tab. Not keyword search. Emotion search. I typed "I had a tiring day" and it gave me songs to lift me up. There's also a Rediscovered section. Because we all have songs we deeply loved and completely forgot about. Songs sitting unplayed in our library for months. The AI brings them back at the right moment. Got tired of not always finding the right song for the right moment and wondered if AI could just do it.
usable_records.author: bhumikaaa

usable_records.text: Re: LABEL AI MUSIC Adding a comment here to increase visibility of this issue. I really enjoy finding new music from the discover weekly playlist but after listening to the first few tracks I noticed that none of the artists that I have listened to are real people. To me, this is an affront to all Spotify users and feels incredibly soulless. After investigating I found that two of the AI generated artists in my discover weekly were created by the same entrepreneur named Lawrence Vavra, who owns a label of several different AI "artists". This has completely broken my trust because I used to feel like Spotify recommendations were made for me and my tastes but now I feel like I'm being hand fed literal slop. I now Google every single artist name to make sure they exist before I add them to my Library. I will likely cancel my service if this does not change soon. Let me remove AI garbage from my experience that I pay for, please. This will snowball into a huge issue as AI takes up more and more of our digital space, it would be great to be able to, at the very least, filter out this swill.
usable_records.author: isaiahiswicked

usable_records.text: Find someone with nearly identical taste in music, then show what else they listen to Look at my top 100 songs of 2025 (Wrapped) and give me a list of ten people who have the most overlap in their top 100. Eg this anonymous person from Finland has 90.3% overlap, this person from Wisconsin has 85% overlap, and so on. Then let me look at their top 100 and sort it by what the overlap is AND what songs they listened to that I haven't. In other words, if this person, who has very similar taste in music as I do, has repeatedly listened to some song, there's a high likelihood that I'll dig it. "Find your Taste Match". Many people are averse to AI and AI recommendations. Discover Weekly is notoriously bad for people with unique taste. You can really please music aficionados if you brand this human-centric. These are real people, with really good taste. Now you get to discover music through other people. If it's impractical to do this on a personal level, why not aggregate? "These are the songs you would really like, based on people who share your taste in music (+90% overlap in Wrapped)".
usable_records.author: jaakkoj

usable_records.text: A button to remove recently played songs and playlist from suggested on the home page Spotify should let users long-press any suggested playlist, song, album or recommendation on the homepage to bring up a simple menu that includes Remove from Homepage or Hide This Suggestion. Selecting it would instantly take that item out of the feed so it no longer appears as a suggestion. It could also have an option for Hide similar content to reduce recommendations in the same genre or from the same artists. This would give direct and immediate control over the home screen. The algorithm would receive clear feedback that something does not fit and update much quicker than relying only on ongoing listening to gradually override old data. The homepage would remain clean, relevant and truly personalized every time the app is opened, with no waiting or extra work required. In cases where someone used my account and listened to Spanish or Mexican music and no matter how much music I listen to afterward it doesn't disappear, this feature would help in such cases by letting you remove those stubborn suggestions right away instead of dealing with them lingering for weeks.
usable_records.author: Thor895

It could also have an option for Hide similar content to reduce recommendations in the same genre or from the same artists. This would give direct and immediate control over the home screen. The algorithm would receive clear feedback that something does not fit and update much quicker than relying only on ongoing listening to gradually override old data. In cases where someone used my account and listened to Spanish or Mexican music and no matter how much music I listen to afterward it doesn't disappear, this feature would help in such cases by letting you remove those stubborn suggestions right away instead of dealing with them lingering for weeks.

usable_records.text: Stop recommending the Bill Simmons podcast. The Bill Simmons sports show keeps getting recommended, shoved up to the front of my home screen and even auto starts if I let podcasts play after one another. I don't like sports. I'm not interested in sports. I actively dislike sports. I've clicked the not interested button multiple times over the years so I guess it's broken. Or maybe you don't care and just push whatever sponsored content you want to make a profit. When you force a sponsored sports podcast like Bill Simmons in my face and down my throat, wedged into my ear canals, I actively want to delete the app and cancel my subscription I'm so upset. There might be something more going on in my life but this lack of control violates something deep inside me. This is my personal audio listening experience. I like my music. I like my podcasts about topics I'm actually interested in. I DON'T WANT TO LISTEN TO A DUDE BLATHER ON ABOUT PUTTING A SPORTS BALL IN A HOOP OR AN ENDZONE. Please stop promoting that show to me or add ways to block content permanently or something comparable.

usable_records.text: AI DJ algorithm feels ad driven Plan Premium Country U.S My Question or Issue I tend to use DJ quite often but recently the use of top songs on Spotify or songs with the focus being popular with others and not my normal curated picks has left me dumbfounded. The songs I see given to me during my time using DJ has been flat out baffling. For some context I tend to stay near the same genres as Underground, Webcore, Shoegaze and Metal. But find DJ giving me songs from Mexico like Ranchera, or just plain Latin music in general. I also have noticed a uptick in what it calls currently top songs on Spotify which include the most out of touch for my algorithm mainstream songs to include things like Taylor Swift and more. Would it be possible at this moment in time to disable the music recommendations on DJ that tend to lean more into the what others enjoy rather then recommending what I may enjoy? I have a growing suspension that this DJ may be ad driven based on if the artist paid to be on the discovery incentive or not.

usable_records.text: Option to turn off AI features There should be the option to turn off AI features in the app settings. I'm having a less enjoyable experience using Spotify than 5 years ago when I first joined. It's far more complicated that it needs to be. I'm still unhappy about the desktop UI change in 2023. The AI prompted playlists are not good, and a waste of money in my opinion. I've attempted to use them to find new music, writing a detailed description of the kinds of sounds and genres I would like to hear whilst allowing it to scan my listening history. I find the recommendations are not allowing me to expand my music taste. They send you round in circles. I've had better results without AI through manual searches. Because of the UI layout, it difficult to browse for new music. I never use the AI DJ, it's a novelty I don't wish to pay for. I think if Spotify wants to experiment with AI, that's fine but they shouldn't inflict it on their users. If I could switch to an older version of Spotify I would instantly.

usable_records.text: Add feedback option for Daily Mixes and Made for You playlists Hello everyone, I wanted to make a suggestion that I honestly think would really improve the Spotify experience. I feel like there should be an option for users to give feedback on Daily Mixes and the Made for You playlists . Sometimes the recommendations are great, but other times they feel kind of random and not really based on what I’m currently listening to. For example, there are times when a certain artist keeps showing up in my Daily Mix cover or in the playlist suggestions even though I barely listen to them anymore, and there’s no real way to let Spotify know that the recommendations are off. It would be really helpful if there was a feature where we could rate the playlist suggestions, like/dislike the mix as a whole, or leave feedback on whether the recommendations actually match our taste right now. I think this would help improve and make the personalized playlists feel more accurate and less random over time. Would anyone else like to see something like this?

usable_records.text: Re: February 2026 Spotify for Developers update: thread This is why people will leave development on the Spotify platform. Things like this. This limiting of the API and placing it behind a paywall is going to negatively impact simple development. I'm am using the API to show real-time updates on own music via the /artists endpoint. With this update I'm inclined to switch to Apple Music with MusicKit, which has way better functionality then Spotify at the moment. The removal of the preview_url was a signal of the downfall. People will be inclined to switch to the Deezer API. But knowing the industry, I'd reckon that they'll too will update their API, so you'll be stuck on this endless cycle of finding a new service for simple tasks. Oh well, I'm happy that I have an Apple Developer account. And it's less of a *bleep*fest than these new ""improvements"". Do better Spotify. Fix the AI recommendations first. Of course no hate to ThePodfather since he's basically the messenger of bad news. Man, I feel bad for small hobbyist developers.

usable_records.text: Bring back the old Discover Weekly algorithm Discover Weekly used to be the strongest reason I subscribed to Spotify. For years, it was how I found my favorite artists — songs I'd never have heard otherwise, but that landed perfectly in my taste. Something has changed. For months now, the tracks Discover Weekly serves me are not just unfamiliar, they're songs I actively dislike. The playlist no longer feels like discovery — it feels like a random feed. And it's not just me. Several friends and colleagues have brought this up, all describing the same experience: a playlist that used to feel hand-picked now feels generic and off-target. I'm the type of listener your discovery features were built for: someone who actively wants to find new artists. But discovery only works if the recommendations start from my actual taste. When that foundation breaks, I have no reason to stay on the platform. If Discover Weekly doesn't return to what it once was, I'll cancel my subscription — and based on the conversations I'm having, I won't be the only one.

usable_records.text: Re: How do I clear the que on Android Galaxy Hey there @Karaptian , Thanks for getting in touch about this here in the Community. Currently, if you tap on a song in a playlist/album, the playback won't stop after that song, but it'll carry on to the next song in that playlist/album. If you only want to hear one song, you can search for it from the main Search menu and play it from there. If you don't want to get recommendations, you need to disable the Autoplay option from the app settings. That being said, you can only clear the songs/playlists from the queue you've added to it manually. For example, if you're listening to a playlist, and add a song to the queue, the "Clear" option will appear in the queue so you can remove it easily. Also, if you want to remove particular songs from it, you can swipe left them and they'll be removed. Hope this helps. If you need a hand with anything else, the Community is here for you.

usable_records.text: Re: Shared playlists & audiobooks not working on Kids/Managed accounts Glad I gave up on waiting for Spotify even before they decided to backtrack and retcon it to "this is what it is supposed to be like" - which is obviously not true, seeing that new releases are available on managed accounts. Like many in this thread, I cancelled my Premium Family subscription and after the recommendations here, we are trying out Deezer. So far I like it, it feels like Spotify used to be like a few years ago, fresher and snappier. And no videos and other useless detractions from what I had it for: LISTENING. To music AND radio plays. I miss Spotify Connect a little bit on my few devices that supported it, but Deezer has direct Google Cast & Airplay support that works well for us, and Connect didn't work at all for Managed Accounts anyway which for us made it almost useless. Spotify really botched their family account concept, and lost a decade-long faithful customer in the process. I'm not coming back.

usable_records.text: Re: Unwanted items in library Hi there, @Momma_Jo Thank you for your message. Just to clarify, it's considered as normal behavior of the app having in the home page "recommended for today" , "new releases for you" , "based on your listening" and other boards because that's how the Spotify algorithm works and that's how the app is built, so you can discover new music all the time. On another note, you can identify any recommended music content on the Now Playing Bar as it will have a label next to the artist name, like this one: Also, bear in mind that you can disable Smart Shuffle in your mobile app to avoid those added recommendations by following these steps: Tap your profile picture at the top. Tap Settings and privacy . Select Playback . Switch Include Smart Shuffle in play modes to off if it's on. Lastly, it's also worth checking if the " Autoplay " option is enabled in the same Playback section of the configuration. If you have any other doubts, just let us know!

usable_records.text: Re: Saving Playlists by Spotify Hi there, @Beezkneez Thanks for reaching out! Just to confirm, do you mean playlists created directly by Spotify (daily mix, some artist's radio, weekly discovery, recommendations)? If you mean those types of playlists, keep in mind that the " daily mix " and the " weekly discovery " ones change every day or every week, so you cannot save them exactly as they were the first time you listened to them. If you liked one of Spotify's created playlists a lot, you can add it to your own library with the (+) button. Or you can create a new playlist from your own profile adding the songs you loved from those playlists to it, like @Eni mentioned before. If you're referring to playlists created by yourself and you are not able to save new songs there, you can share more details about it (the type of device you're using to save the content in your playlists with the corresponding OS version, and the version of your Spotify app). Keep us posted!

usable_records.text: Re: When listening to Release Radar or Discover Weekly and hiding a song, the wrong song is playe... Hi there, @supershippy Thanks for reaching out! Keep in mind that when you hide a song on Release Radar or Discover Weekly, the app might skip one or more songs, playing a track further down the list rather than the next one in order. This issue may be happening because the Hide function acts as a skip rather than removing the song entirely. Also, take into account that Hide song may only affect your current session and could be not synched between devices (a song might be hidden in a specific playlist on your mobile but then you can see it again on your desktop device on the same playlist). We suggest using the Don't play this option instead of just hiding the song. You can go to the artist's profile > 3-dots menu > Don't play this artist to permanently block them and improve your recommendations. Hope this helps! Keep us posted!
usable_records.author: Paula_Vi

usable_records.text: Re: Spotify Suggestions Hey @autonewman, thanks for providing some more context, I understand what you mean now. You can influence your taste profile by using the 'x' and '+' buttons on mobile. So, when autoplay is active and new songs are queued after the end of a playlist, you can tap 'x' on the songs that you don't like or want to be played. That should hopefully shake up the algorithm so that it recommends a wider variety of songs. If you're noticing this happening on one specific device, it's worth trying a clean reinstall of the app, which gets rid of any cache or cookies that could be influencing the algorithm. Let me know if that changes anything. Regarding paid song recommendations, I am not too clued up on this subject, but I did find an article which goes into detail as to how that works (specifically the last paragraph on commercial considerations). I don't think this is necessarily the case for you, but still worth a read.

usable_records.text: Chat-based music recommendations using natural language (mood & daily context) Hello Spotify team and community, I’d like to share an idea based on my daily listening experience. I really enjoy discovering music on Spotify, especially when I unexpectedly encounter songs I’ve never heard before — both new releases and older hidden gems. It feels similar to listening to the radio in the past, but more personal. I was thinking it would be great if Spotify had a chat-based feature, where users could describe their current mood or situation in natural language, for example: “I want relaxing, instrumental music for my morning commute.” Then Spotify could respond with several playlists like: Calm & gentle morning focus Stress-free commute Soft instrumental music to ease into work This would allow users to explore music not by genre or artist, but by how they feel in that moment. I believe this kind of interaction could make music discovery more flexible, intuitive, and emotionally engaging. Thank you for considering this idea!
usable_records.author: MusicForestExplorer

usable_records.text: Block specific podcasts from auto-playing or appearing in recommendations I rarely listen to podcasts, but tonight I decided to put on a sleep podcast since I was having trouble falling asleep. Unfortunately, after the sleep podcast ended Spotify started auto-playing a popular podcast that included an extremely graphic, violent and sexual rant. I woke up having a nightmare as a result of Spotify playing this disgusting content that could have been prevented.Turning off explicit content wouldn't have helped because the podcast wasn't even flagged as explicit in the first place, and besides I'm an adult and don't want to exclude good music just because it has an F bomb or two. I already reported the podcast, but there's no way to stop that specific podcast from auto-playing or showing up on my home page again which is disappointing. Heck, there's not even a way to mark it as "not interested" which would have been at least somewhat helpful.

usable_records.text: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs I think this is becoming an urgent issue. My Discover Weekly (and, more generally, the entire Spotify experience) is becoming increasingly crowded with AI-generated music. In my opinion, these songs do not have the same artistic merit, creativity, or emotional depth as music created by humans. Therefore, I do not want AI-generated songs included in my recommendations. As a user, it is becoming increasingly frustrating to distinguish genuine artists and songs from AI-generated tracks that are often produced in large quantities simply to generate revenue from the platform. It ruins the user experience because you have to go track by track detecting if it’s a real song or AI slop. Please consider implementing an option in the user settings that allows listeners to exclude AI-generated music from their recommendations. Otherwise, many users may choose to migrate to other platforms that place a greater emphasis on supporting music and human artistry.
usable_records.author: Sopo87

metadata.title: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs
metadata.search_snippet: I think this is becoming an urgent issue. My Discover Weekly (and, more generally, the entire Spotify experience) is becoming increasingly crowded with AI-generated music. In my opinion, these songs do not have the same artistic merit, creativity, or emotional depth as music created by humans. Therefore, I do not want AI-generated songs included in my recommendations. As a user, it is becoming increasingly frustrating to distinguish genuine artists and songs from AI-generated tracks that are often produced in large quantities simply to generate revenue from the platform. It ruins the user experience because you have to go track by track detecting if it’s a real song or AI slop. Please consider implementing an option in the user settings that allows listeners to exclude AI-generated music from their recommendations. Otherwise, many users may choose to migrate to other platforms that place a greater emphasis on supporting music and human artistry.

usable_records.text: Betreff: LABEL AI MUSIC Right, Spotify community managers, moderators and rock stars should at least merge related ideas to collect the votes in one thread in my opinion. Concerning recognising and labeling AI-generated content you can use Deezer, they have a software for that and put a note about it on their album pages. They also automatically exclude these releases from your recommendations, so you don't have to do that yourself. It's a lot of work having to check all Spotify tracks in Discover Weekly and Release Radar (230 per week in my case) in Deezer of course, and I might just quit listening to them and use the related Deezer playlists instead. Deezer is still available for free in some countries, so you don't have to subscribe to them if you only want to check Spotify's AI music. But they have lossless streaming for a long time already, and I also like the better sound compared to Spotify Free.
usable_records.author: hans-jürgen

usable_records.text: Block genres After six months of Discover Weekly trying to convert me, I have come to the conclusion that my Spotify account may be possessed by the spirit of Jesus Christ. Every Monday I perform an exorcism by hiding tracks, skipping songs and blocking artists. Yet the following week He returns. Stronger. More determined. More CEDM. Meanwhile, Daylist, AI DJ and my Mixes understand my taste perfectly. Only Discover Weekly seems convinced that my true calling is "Jesus Is The King (D&B Remix)". My suggestion: please give users the option to block an entire genre from recommendations. Sometimes I don't dislike a few artists—I simply never want recommendations from a specific genre cluster. For now I have blocked every Christian EDM artist I could find. If next week's Discover Weekly is still full of worship drum & bass, I will assume Spotify's recommendation engine has achieved sentience and is actively trying to save my soul.
usable_records.author: JillesvA

usable_records.text: Re: Despite Premium plan, randomly my currently playing playlist is abandoned and forced into shu... Hey, @20joneskat , Welcome to the Spotify Community! I suspect that this might have something to do with the Autoplay feature - when your playlist ends, it populates the play queue with similar song recommendations so that the playback never ends. You can disable it in the app's Settings and privacy > Playback. If you've noticed that this happens when using Smart Shuffle for your playlist, I'd suggest disabling it entirely as well. The option is again found under Settings and privacy > Playback > Include Smart Shuffle in play modes. As for this occurring even mid-playlist, perhaps the app has been backgrounded for too long and the operating system needs to reclaim resources, so it suspends it. What you can experiment with here is switching between having Background App Refresh enabled and disabled to see if that does the trick. Hope this helps! Cheers 🤘
usable_records.author: Yordan

usable_records.text: AI "verified artists" with "About" never saying it's AI Recently had Lyra Vesperi in my Discover Weekly, which I immediately knew was AI from the art. But how much of the music Spotify is pushing is AI and harder to catch? Why do they allow it and not make it it's own genre or at least tag it with a 'Generative AI' flag? I don't like the truth that Spotify is sneaking AI 'music' into it's recommendations. Time spent listening to the slop is listening time where Spotify doesn't have to pay a real artist. How can I trust any 'artist' on Spotify now? Am I forced to google every artist I hear to make sure Spotify isn't trying to trick me? For all that's holy, Please remove all AI content, or give listeners the option to 'opt-in' to AI music. Nobody wants to listen to AI music. It's not art, and it's not music.
usable_records.author: Clemato

usable_records.text: Re: Spotfy keeps recommending me random songs even though I turned automatic suggestions OFF Hey @Meneer , welcome to the Spotify Community! 🎵 Do you happen to be using the free version of Spotify? In that case, there are a few things that you can try: If the song recommendation has an 'X' button, you can click that. This will make sure that the song is not played again in that particular playlist. Spotify learns from your listening habits, so the recommendations should get better over time in general. To block any specific artist, you can open their profile and click the 3 dots menu. Click don't play this artist to skip them in recommendations as well. I am linking some related articles with more details that you can check out: Hide and Unhide Songs | Your Taste Profile | Understanding recommendations on Spotify I hope that helps. Let me know if you need anything else. 🙂
usable_records.author: Amay

usable_records.text: Re: [All Platforms] Blocking podcasts shows Please share instructions or add a feature to Spotify on how to block podcasts. I paid for the app to avoid videos and not have recommendations I'm not interested in forced. The excessive video and podcasts recommendations (in the typical YouTube clickbait format -- "3 ways you can build wealth you wouldn't believe" if a foolish thumbnail) are really degrading the Spotify experience; to the point of probable cancellation for a 10+ years user. It doesn't make sense to use it if it doesn't let me do that. If I wanted a video or a podcast, I could use YouTube Premium. It's annoying for adult users, and it's a deal breaker for children's usage. It's really degrading the experience and I could not find instructions on how to block, not even specific podcast creators like we can with artists; much less not get any recommendations at all.

usable_records.text: Improve Song Radio Recommendations to Prioritize Sound-Based Discovery I’ve noticed a clear difference in the quality of recommendations between “Go to Song Radio” and simply playing a song directly from search (with shuffle turned off). When using Song Radio, the suggestions often feel repetitive - frequently looping in artists I already listen to or leaning heavily toward more popular, algorithm-driven choices. In contrast, when I search for a specific track and let Spotify continue playing naturally, the following songs are often more diverse, unique, and introduce smaller or lesser-known artists. It feels like the search-based playback is more focused on the actual sound and characteristics of the song, rather than broader listening habits or popularity signals. It would be great to see Song Radio improved to match this experience - prioritising sound-based discovery and surfacing more varied and emerging artists, rather than repeating familiar or mainstream recommendations.
usable_records.author: Cliffin

usable_records.text: Having the option to create multiple profiles under one cost Please consider adding the ability to create multiple user profiles under a single, standard Spotify account, similar to how Netflix, Disney+, or Prime Video work. Why this would be useful: Convenience: It allows family members or couples to keep their music libraries, daily mixes, and wrapped statistics completely separate without the need to manage entirely different email logins. Smart Device Sharing: Many households share a single tablet or smart speaker. Swapping profiles quickly on a shared device would stop one person's listening habits from ruining the other person's algorithms and recommendations. Better Value: It fills the gap for users who want to share a plan but don't need the full setup or separate email account requirements of a Duo or Family plan. Please vote for this if you want an easier way to share Spotify at home : )
usable_records.author: Susan256

usable_records.text: Re: Weird song recommendations in the discover weekly I know this is old but this exact thing has just happened to my Spotify. I opened Discover Weekly and it contained only Latin music, which I never listen to. After removing all of the songs from the playlist Spotify then went on to recommend more Latin playlists to me. When I exited the Discover Weekly playlist there is no sign of Latin music in my Home page suggesting it is not from someone having hacked my account and playing Latin music, as it would show up in other ways, such as playlist suggestions. For eg. 'Jump back in' - all normal, 'Recents' - aside from Discover Weekly all normal, ie. everything I have listen to. 'Recommended Stations' and 'Made for x'? all normal. This seems to me to be something screwy going on with Spotify's AI. Cheers.

usable_records.text: Give people the ability to shut off idiotic "audiobooks" recommendations Spotify's "audiobook" recommendations are awful, terrible, embarrassing, extremely dumb, and utterly useless. I never listen to audio books with Spotify, I never have and I never will. I have absolutely no interest in audio books. I do not want Spotify's audio book recommendations. I want to completely turn them off, I want them to disappear forever, I want to have a switch in my app settings that says "Turn off audiobook recommendations" so that this totally useless garbage will never clutter my home screen with its seemingly endlessly idiotic suggestions about conspiracy theories, orgasms, cooking, and the Third Reich. This algorithm is utterly braindead, totally fried, and more desperate to get my attention than an all-caps clickbait article. Give your users the option to shovel this garbage out of their home feeds.
usable_records.author: as_if_qwerty

usable_records.text: Please Don’t Include Audiobook Chapters in Music Playlists Dear Spotify Team, I use Spotify every day and truly appreciate the personalized playlists such as “On Repeat,” “Your Artist Mixes,” and the “Daily Mixes.” These features are a big part of what makes Spotify enjoyable for me. However, I’ve noticed that these automatically generated playlists often include individual chapters from audiobooks I listen to occasionally. In music‑focused playlists, this doesn’t make much sense: audiobook chapters interrupt the musical flow and don’t fit the purpose or structure of these playlists. My request would be to avoid mixing audiobook content into music playlists. It would be wonderful if Spotify could separate music and audiobooks more clearly within personalized recommendations. Thank you for your attention and for the great work you’re already doing. Kind regards Peter ( )
usable_records.author: rocketman99

usable_records.text: Re: blatant advertisement on Home page of desktop Spotify Hey @pavowren , Thanks for reaching out! Spotify doesn’t play or include ads that interrupt the music listening experience of Premium subscribers. However, you might still see sponsored content in other areas of the app. This might include personalized recommendations based on your listening history, or you might get a banner letting you know you're also eligible for other Premium subscriptions, such as e.g. Premium Duo or Family. Nevertheless, these are just recommendations and will not interrupt your ad-free music listening time 🙂 As for the 'Not Interested' button seemingly not doing anything, we do thank you for bringing this to our attention. In case there's anything else in the meantime, don't hesitate to reach back to us. We're just a post away 🙌🏼
usable_records.author: Vasil

usable_records.text: Re: Playlist recommend not showing up Hey there @pepsishark , Thanks for posting in the Community and welcome. We'd like to gather some additional info to have a better idea of what is happening on your side. Please send us: The exact Spotify version you're running. Did you notice if this started to happen after a specific event such as an OS update? Did you notice the same regardless of the playlist you choose to add songs to? A video recording. You can attach the video in your next reply in mp4 format, or upload it to YouTube or Google Drive and make it public. Note: Be aware this is a public space, so please don't share any personal info on the video you'll send us. Keep us in the loop!

usable_records.text: "Refresh" button to instantly update Daylist and Daily Mixes I propose adding a "Refresh" or "Regenerate" button to personalized playlists like the Daylist and Daily Mixes. This button would allow users to instantly update the entire tracklist with new recommendations if the current selection doesn't match their mood or liking. Currently, if a Daylist or Daily Mix doesn't look appealing, i have to wait hours (for Daylists) or an entire day (for Daily Mixes) for a dynamic update.Additionally, these algorithmic playlists tend to loop and repeat the exact same songs frequently, instead of diving into Spotify's vast catalog. A manual refresh button would give users immediate control to break these repetition loops, discover new music actively, and enjoy a truly personalized, intentional listening experience without artificial waiting times.
usable_records.author: Blandem

usable_records.text: Disable All AI Features In Spotify I want the ability to turn off all AI features from my Spotify settings. While I'm sure some customers find value in these features (or at the very least are willing to swallow them) they are a waste of space for me, and make the platform less enjoyable for me. I don't want an AI D.J. (Hate.) I don't want to ask an AI for recommendations. (Hate.) I don't want an AI to generate a playlist for me. (Hate.) Again, I don't expect to Spotify to get rid of these feature entirely. But the the very presence of this stuff on the home screen drags down the experience for me. We should be able to toggle this stuff out.

usable_records.text: Re: Add "remove video content" option to Spotify Family This is what support told me today: After checking, I can confirm that for the managed accounts can only access music on Spotify; no podcasts, Radio or audiobook content included. Here's the Young listener's app features * Only have access to music (no podcasts, radio or audiobook content/recommendations) * Have their own Library and listening experience/history * Can listen offline * Can use Spotify Connect in speakers only (excluding 'Cast Touch'). No other device types are allowed. * Can't make purchases or see countdown pages * Can't join collaborative and interactive/social features In your case, you can revert your daughter's account to regular, then restricted the Explicit content. Stephen C. at 09:23 AM, Jan 8

usable_records.text: Re: Ads in Home Screen with Premium Hey @Digitalmus , Spotify doesn't play or include ads that interrupt the music listening experience for Premium subscribers. However, you might still see sponsored content in other areas of the app. This might include personalized recommendations based on your listening history, or you might get a banner letting you know you're also eligible for other Premium subscriptions, such as e.g. Premium Duo or Family. Nevertheless, these are just recommendations and will not interrupt your ad-free music listening time. I appreciate you sharing this info to me as I will make sure to pass it on to the right folks. Hope this clears things up!

usable_records.text: Add a “Super Like” to Improve Recommendations Description: The current “Like” button treats all positive feedback the same. But not all likes are equal — some songs are casual approvals, others are core favorites. I’d like to see a “Super Like” option that signals a stronger preference. For example, a Super Liked song could: Heavily influence Daily Mixes and AI DJ sessions. Increase weighting for similar artists/genres. Anchor radio stations and autoplay sessions. Be prioritized in future recommendations. This would: Improve recommendation quality by increasing signal strength. Help users shape their taste profile more precisely. Reduce the need to repeatedly skip or manually curate to influence the algorithm.
usable_records.author: dicen

usable_records.text: More Variety in Spotify Radio & Generated Playlists I find it really frustrating that so many Spotify-generated playlists and radios based on my music taste basically contain the exact same songs over and over again. The whole reason I choose a radio or a generated playlist is because I want to discover something different when I’m tired of my own music. But instead, it often feels like I’m just listening to the same playlist in different versions. Even across completely different playlists, many of the same songs keep repeating. I’d love more variety, deeper discovery, and less repetition in Spotify’s recommendations.

usable_records.text: Re: DJ Recommending the same set of songs I was able to fix the AI DJ's recommendations so that it wouldn't keep suggesting the same songs I'd liked before. Previously, I had created a playlist that completely duplicated the “Liked Songs” playlist, and that was causing the recommendations to go haywire. I went to the settings for that playlist and selected “ Exclude from your taste profile ” After that, I added a couple of albums by other users that I found on playlost.fm, and my recommendations were restored; now it suggests new songs much more often.

usable_records.text: Re: Allow users to block certain artist Hey @Joseph1234 , thanks for reaching out to the Community! On Android and iOS, you can remove content recommendations from the "Music" and " Podcasts & Shows" feeds of the Home screen via the Not Interested button 👎 . You can find more info about it in the Status update here . You can also block specific artists you don't want the app playing by heading to the artist page you'd like to block > tap on the 3 dots > select Do not play this artist . I hope this clears things up!

metadata.title: Make Local Files Affect Recommendations
metadata.search_snippet: Hello. I, have been a spotify listgher for over 5 years now. I'd like for it to be possible for songs on the local files playlist to influence recommendations, for instance, being added to on repeat playlist or being played by AI DJ. This would be helpful for instance if you've created AI songs, and added them to your playlist.

usable_records.text: Re: How can I remove the “playing recommended songs for you” feature To stop “playing recommended songs for you,” open your music app’s settings and disable Autoplay, Smart Shuffle, or Recommendations, if available. If no option exists, turn off repeat/autoplay after your queue ends or create a manual playlist to avoid automatic suggestions.
usable_records.author: mubashiriqbal

metadata.title: Re: How can I remove the “playing recommended songs for you” feature
metadata.search_snippet: To stop “playing recommended songs for you,” open your music app’s settings and disable Autoplay, Smart Shuffle, or Recommendations, if available. If no option exists, turn off repeat/autoplay after your queue ends or create a manual playlist to avoid automatic suggestions.

usable_records.text: Re: Remove an entire genre from my taste profile That's a great idea. We have to have control over our taste. I don't care if Spotify thinks I'll like a song; if I exclude a genre, it means I don't want any recommendations from it at all, and that should be respected.
usable_records.author: luizclaudio

metadata.title: Re: Remove an entire genre from my taste profile
metadata.search_snippet: That's a great idea. We have to have control over our taste. I don't care if Spotify thinks I'll like a song; if I exclude a genre, it means I don't want any recommendations from it at all, and that should be respected.

usable_records.text: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs My Release Radar this morning featured no AI. Not seeing any AI in my recommendations either. It's all about whom you FOLLOW and the albums and tracks that you save and stream. If you don't want AI, make the simple adjustments.
usable_records.author: jazzmanjohn

metadata.title: Re: Mark / Disable AI Generated Songs
metadata.search_snippet: My Release Radar this morning featured no AI. Not seeing any AI in my recommendations either. It's all about whom you FOLLOW and the albums and tracks that you save and stream. If you don't want AI, make the simple adjustments.

usable_records.text: Re: New discover music I think the issue is that spotify recommendations overall are not great. I don't think the swiping feature would help. They just need to refine the recommendation features. If my daily mixes and personalised playlists were better I'd use them more.
usable_records.author: genericcode

metadata.title: Re: New discover music
metadata.search_snippet: I think the issue is that spotify recommendations overall are not great. I don't think the swiping feature would help. They just need to refine the recommendation features. If my daily mixes and personalised playlists were better I'd use them more.

usable_records.text: Limited control over listening history Users cannot edit or clean their listening history. A single accidental listen can affect recommendations for a long time. Adding options like removing a track from the taste profile would help keep recommendations accurate.
usable_records.author: Sade9i

metadata.title: Limited control over listening history
metadata.search_snippet: Users cannot edit or clean their listening history. A single accidental listen can affect recommendations for a long time. Adding options like removing a track from the taste profile would help keep recommendations accurate.

usable_records.text: #MusicMonday Review - May 2026 #MusicMonday is the hashtag I've been using for quite a while to share music recommendations from up-and-coming artists. Always fresh, and always different, trying to look for trends before they become one. You can check April's review for more music. It doesn't matter if you want to raise attention to government, environmental, or personal issues, music is the perfect way to express yourself, whatever genre you may prefer. Give them a listen, with a word from the artists themselves. 🎧 June Holland – Mouldy Orange I spent so much time just Sat on my own They said I disrupted the rest of the kids So they left me alone But I was a fire until you put out my flames Labelled me naughty and stupid When deep down I knew I had brains Can't help but be mad that school Could've been easier If only I'd have had an educated teacher We begin our trip in South Manchester, England for a soulful Indie Folk track about a girl who has being punished for being herself, but with time understood she's perfect just as she is: "It’s a personal experience, I was always labelled the naughty kid in primary school I got in trouble a lot but no one ever suggested it could be ADHD, I’m not sure it was really explored in my school especially in girls… When I reached my twenties I finally got diagnosed with ADHD and although it didn’t come as a surprise it did bring up a lot of emotions including anger and grief for the young misunderstood girl I was growing up. So this song was kind of like therapy for me. Getting out all the emotions I felt and reflecting on my life. But I also wanted to create this song and share my experience because I know there will be many others like me out there and I want to make them feel seen and know that they aren’t alone. The response has been amazing so far it’s been wonderful to see people connecting with the track. 🥹🧡 😉 " spotify:track:4KepSGT6osc3dZwyCldBAC:small Shines – Late for Days Since you went away it's hard to fall asleep your t-shirt smells the same you left it here with me Let's travel east to Grimsby, England, for an Indie Rock track about those really long conversations that seem to go on for hours in the middle of the night : "I think it's one of them open ended songs that sort of content different meanings. Whether it be sort of the breakup of a relationship, or a friendship or just sort of whatever's left after that's happened. But also it came together really quick for us. Just got sort of jamming over a little riff and we're sort of working like that for all our stuff this year. It's not overthinking stuff, not polishing stuff, and just keeping it nice and fresh, and just getting it out there. I'd say the style of this song is more shoegaze than what we've done prior, but there's a lot of elements in our first album that's shoegaze. It's probably inspired by great bands like Beach Fossils , Wild Nothing , you know, The Drums . I like all them type of bands. They've all got a good groove." spotify:track:2s5fhMX6PvJNh3Sx2heiMb:small Fine Night Elements – All We Know Valleys low to mountains high Rivers running from the beginning of time Sun shines through to the forest floor Last flower raises its head But only once more Leave us alone I hear them cry Don't speak your language but why should we die? Do you ever think that this world does not revolve around you? Up north in Gateshead, England, we get an Alt Rock track blended with some Indie vibes about human's inherent appetite to destroy what's in our path, and take everything we can. It's all we know : "Initially the music was laid down just through jamming, but the experience that inspired the lyrics are just the constant information about global warming that we’re consistently ignoring. The track discusses the path we’re on as a species and how we need to change." spotify:track:2vZ5Hd8r42VoqrmtMz61kP:small Draken – It Serves You Right You screwed up, you crossed the line So don’t act so surprised, my friend We always knew it was a matter of time Before you’d be knee deep in sh*t again Slowly dying Thought you were death defying They got you dead to rights And truth be told… it serves you right We fly now to Oslo, Norway for a Metal track that tells what happens when arrogance makes you blind, while leaving your decency behind. Bassist Hallward explains: "It's about power hungry politicians, and corruption. I wrote the lyrics when there was a series of political scandals in Norway; politicians taking advantage of the system, misuse of public funds, etc." spotify:track:3ZqpEOcb4Xq0Yo6T2FKqpV:small dimblue – One Hundred Thoughts I'm not defined by my sense of revenge I drop my innocence and I don't want it back Thoughts travel so fast that I cannot bend My heart is broken Some things you cannot mend Compelling answers don't always let you rest Satisfaction isn't always the best Some say that truth lies in the middle ground But our subconscious wish is still to be found We now travel to Bari, Italy for an Indie Folk track with some Lo-Fi details about taking time to reflect on something you have experienced, and trying to come up with an adequate response: "Overthinking is exactly what gave life to this track. All those thoughts that flow incessantly, often leaving us exhausted. I believe the song was born from the desire to bring some order, while at the same time immersing myself in the creative flow. I think the filter was the instrument itself—the acoustic guitar—which somehow created the sense of order I was searching for in that creative moment. The electric guitar lines, on the other hand, allowed me to push into the background all those thoughts that tend to overlap." spotify:track:60fe6PmHwQuwrc9mcbaG2s:small Ismena – How Are You? You came into my life and you said you were mine And started a fire in my mind I thought I knew who you were but you're a liar And now I don't trust a single word Maybe one day it will turn out right Who do you think you're standing beside? Finally I am enlightened I see you for who you are We end out travels in Utrecht, NL. for a d reamy, melancholic Indie Folk track about being hurt to the point of making sure you won't cross paths with that person ever again: "T his was a very personal experience that affected me deeply. It's about trusting someone and gradually finding out that this person is not who they say they are. It can be hard to grasp when someone turns out to be completely different than what you always thought they were. It's also about finally accepting their true nature and not letting them affect you anymore. " spotify:track:0ubUu2y2iaCqUFyLZsdF65:small Listen to them and much more on the complete playlist spotify:playlist:2IYRLjcwf2X9vC0QrI0b0P See you next time!
usable_records.author: osornios
